It is supplied as solid crystals with a distinct metallic sheen and a characteristic vapour that forms at room temperature. Industrial users value iodine for its strong reactivity, broad functionality and reliability in chemical, pharmaceutical and nutrition production.
safetyHandling: Irritant and corrosive material that can affect skin, eyes and respiratory system. Vapour may cause headaches or irritation. Handle with PPE in well ventilated areas. Store away from reducing agents and strong acids.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kilogram steel drums, glass bottles or HDPE containers with inner liners. Store in cool, dry and dark conditions to limit sublimation and preserve purity.

name: Tetrabutyl Ammonium Bromide (TBAB)
description: Tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB) is a white crystalline powder with formula C16H36BrN and molecular weight 322.37 g/mol. Density 1.04 g/cm³, melting point 100-104°C, highly soluble in water and organics; quaternary ammonium salt.
physicalProperties: Tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB) is a white crystalline powder with formula C16H36BrN and molecular weight 322.37 g/mol. Density 1.04 g/cm³, melting point 100-104°C, highly soluble in water and organics; quaternary ammonium salt.
safetyHandling: Irritates skin, eyes, respiratory tract; harmful if swallowed. Avoid dust; incompatible with strong oxidizers. Low bioaccumulation; use PPE, hygiene practices.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg drums or bags; store in ventilated areas.

name: Tetrahydrothiophene
description: Tetrahydrothiophene, also known as thiolane or thiophane, is a colorless transparent volatile liquid with the formula C4H8S and molecular weight 88.17 g/mol. It has a strong unpleasant odor detectable at 0.01 ppm, density of about 1.0 g/cm³, boiling point 119°C, melting point -96°C, and flash point 12°C. Insoluble in water but miscible in ethanol, ether, benzene, and acetone, it is a cyclic thioalkane and saturated analog of thiophene, stable under normal conditions but flammable.
physicalProperties: Tetrahydrothiophene, also known as thiolane or thiophane, is a colorless transparent volatile liquid with the formula C4H8S and molecular weight 88.17 g/mol. It has a strong unpleasant odor detectable at 0.01 ppm, density of about 1.0 g/cm³, boiling point 119°C, melting point -96°C, and flash point 12°C. Insoluble in water but miscible in ethanol, ether, benzene, and acetone, it is a cyclic thioalkane and saturated analog of thiophene, stable under normal conditions but flammable.
safetyHandling: Highly flammable liquid (H225), acute toxicity by ingestion/inhalation/skin (H302, H312, H332), skin/eye irritation (H315, H319), aquatic toxicity (H402, H412); incompatible with acids, diazo/azo compounds, halocarbons, isocyanates, aldehydes, alkali metals, nitrides, hydrides—generates heat/hydrogen gas; chronic exposure may cause organ damage.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 200L steel drums with epoxy lining for corrosion resistance, or 1L glass bottles for lab use, ensuring vapor-tight seals as per ChemicalBook standards.

name: Isoamyl Acetate
description: Isoamyl acetate is a colorless liquid with a strong banana-like odor. It is an ester formed from isoamyl alcohol and acetic acid.
physicalProperties: Isoamyl acetate is a colorless liquid with a strong banana-like odor. It is an ester formed from isoamyl alcohol and acetic acid.
safetyHandling: Flammable liquid and vapor; inhalation may cause mild irritation. Keep away from ignition sources.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 180 kg drums or IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Tetrakis Ammonium Bromide
description: Tetrakis(decyl)ammonium bromide is a white crystalline powder with formula C40H84BrN and molecular weight 659.01 g/mol. It is a quaternary ammonium salt, soluble in water and organics, with melting point around 251°C (dec.), used as cationic surfactant; Beilstein/REAXYS 5842649, stable under normal conditions.
physicalProperties: Tetrakis(decyl)ammonium bromide is a white crystalline powder with formula C40H84BrN and molecular weight 659.01 g/mol. It is a quaternary ammonium salt, soluble in water and organics, with melting point around 251°C (dec.), used as cationic surfactant; Beilstein/REAXYS 5842649, stable under normal conditions.
safetyHandling: Irritant to skin/eyes/respiratory; harmful if swallowed/inhaled/absorbed through skin; combustible, avoid oxidizers; may cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. Low toxicity but handled with PPE, ventilate; stable but combustible; no specific hazard class listed, but follow quaternary ammonium safety protocols.
tradeRegulatory: Available in 100g or 500g glass bottles for laboratory applications, or 25kg fiber drums with double PE bags, as listed on Sigma-Aldrich product sheets.

name: Isononanoic Acid
description: Isononanoic acid is a branched-chain carboxylic acid with low volatility and excellent stability. It appears as a clear liquid with mild odor.
physicalProperties: Isononanoic acid is a branched-chain carboxylic acid with low volatility and excellent stability. It appears as a clear liquid with mild odor.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; mildly irritating to skin and eyes. Avoid prolonged heat exposure to prevent oxidation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 200 kg HDPE drums or IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and closed tightly.

name: Tetrakis Phosphonium Chloride
description: Tetrakis(hydroxymethyl)phosphonium chloride (THPC) is a clear pale pink or yellow-green liquid with formula C4H12ClO4P and molecular weight 190.56 g/mol. Density 1.341 g/mL at 25°C, highly soluble in water, used as organophosphorus salt; decomposes on heating releasing POx, HCl, bis(chloromethyl)ether; formed from phosphine, formaldehyde, HCl.
physicalProperties: Tetrakis(hydroxymethyl)phosphonium chloride (THPC) is a clear pale pink or yellow-green liquid with formula C4H12ClO4P and molecular weight 190.56 g/mol. Density 1.341 g/mL at 25°C, highly soluble in water, used as organophosphorus salt; decomposes on heating releasing POx, HCl, bis(chloromethyl)ether; formed from phosphine, formaldehyde, HCl.
safetyHandling: Corrosive, causes burns/irritation to skin/eyes/respiratory; releases hazardous gases on decomposition; reacts vigorously with oxidizers/alkalis; potential carcinogenic effects from byproducts. Chronic/acute hazards; incompatible with oxidizers; use PPE, ventilate; health/environmental risks noted in aquatic systems.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied as 80% aqueous solution in 200kg plastic drums or 1L bottles for lab use, ensuring compatibility with corrosives per PubChem data.

name: Tetrapotassium Pyrophosphate
description: Tetrapotassium pyrophosphate (TKPP), also known as potassium diphosphate tetrabasic, is a white granular solid with formula K4P2O7 and molecular weight 330.34 g/mol. Density 2.53 g/cm³, melting point 1109°C, highly soluble in water (187 g/100mL at 25°C), forming alkaline solutions; insoluble in alcohol; produced industrially, nontoxic.
physicalProperties: Tetrapotassium pyrophosphate (TKPP), also known as potassium diphosphate tetrabasic, is a white granular solid with formula K4P2O7 and molecular weight 330.34 g/mol. Density 2.53 g/cm³, melting point 1109°C, highly soluble in water (187 g/100mL at 25°C), forming alkaline solutions; insoluble in alcohol; produced industrially, nontoxic.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, irritates eyes/skin/respiratory; reproductive toxin, impairs fertility; ingestion causes gastrointestinal issues; decomposes to phosphorus oxides; may lead to environmental phosphate pollution. Avoid strong acids; noncombustible; Hazard Class 8; use PPE, ventilate; essential nutrients but regulate concentrations in water.
tradeRegulatory: Offered in 50 lb multi-wall paper bags with moisture barrier or 2000 lb supersacks, designed for bulk handling as per Brenntag specifications.

name: Tetrapropyl Ammonium Bromide
description: Tetrapropylammonium bromide (TPAB) is a white to off-white crystalline solid with formula C12H28BrN and molecular weight 266.26 g/mol. Melting point 266-272°C, density 1.4 g/cm³, highly soluble in water, weakly acidic solutions; quaternary ammonium salt, hygroscopic, incompatible with strong oxidizers.
physicalProperties: Tetrapropylammonium bromide (TPAB) is a white to off-white crystalline solid with formula C12H28BrN and molecular weight 266.26 g/mol. Melting point 266-272°C, density 1.4 g/cm³, highly soluble in water, weakly acidic solutions; quaternary ammonium salt, hygroscopic, incompatible with strong oxidizers.
safetyHandling: Irritant to skin/eyes/respiratory; harmful if swallowed; combustible, avoid oxidizers; may cause allergic skin reactions or long-term organ effects. Low toxicity but handled with PPE, ventilate; stable but combustible; no specific hazard class listed, but follow quaternary ammonium safety protocols carefully.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 5g, 25g, or 100g glass bottles for analytical use, or 25kg fiber drums, according to Sigma-Aldrich catalog options.

name: Tetrasodium Pyrophosphate (TSPP)
description: Tetrasodium pyrophosphate (TSPP) is an odorless white powder or granules with formula Na4P2O7 and molecular weight 265.90 g/mol. Density 2.53 g/cm³, melting point 988°C, highly soluble in water (3.16 g/100mL cold, 40.26 g/100mL boiling), alkaline pH; hydrated form Na4P2O7·10H2O loses water at 93.8°C.
physicalProperties: Tetrasodium pyrophosphate (TSPP) is an odorless white powder or granules with formula Na4P2O7 and molecular weight 265.90 g/mol. Density 2.53 g/cm³, melting point 988°C, highly soluble in water (3.16 g/100mL cold, 40.26 g/100mL boiling), alkaline pH; hydrated form Na4P2O7·10H2O loses water at 93.8°C.
safetyHandling: Corrosive, causes burns/eye damage/irritation; toxicity twice table salt orally; hydrolyzes to phosphate/sodium ions; may lead to environmental eutrophication issues. Avoid acids/metals; stable but light-sensitive; Hazard Class 8; prolonged exposure causes respiratory problems.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 50 lb kraft paper bags with polyethylene moisture barriers or 2000 lb bulk bags for industrial shipments, as detailed in Brenntag product info.

name: Tetrazene
description: Tetrazene is a chemical compound with the molecular formula H4N4 and molecular weight 60.06 g/mol. It is a colorless material, with density 1.74 g/cm³, and is known for its almost linear N4 chain structure where hydrogen atoms are bound to terminal nitrogen atoms. Unstable, it decomposes rapidly and is studied in research settings; produced for theoretical chemical analysis.
physicalProperties: Tetrazene is a chemical compound with the molecular formula H4N4 and molecular weight 60.06 g/mol. It is a colorless material, with density 1.74 g/cm³, and is known for its almost linear N4 chain structure where hydrogen atoms are bound to terminal nitrogen atoms. Unstable, it decomposes rapidly and is studied in research settings; produced for theoretical chemical analysis.
safetyHandling: Highly unstable and explosive material; sensitive to heat, shock, or friction causing decomposition. Toxic fumes may be released; irritant to eyes and skin. Avoid handling; use extreme caution, PPE; restricted for transport and storage.
tradeRegulatory: Handled in small quantities in sealed glass vials for lab use; store in cool, dry, shock-proof areas per safety protocols.

name: Isopropyl Alcohol
description: Isopropyl alcohol is a colorless, volatile liquid with a sharp odor. It is a secondary alcohol with fast evaporation and excellent solvency.
physicalProperties: Isopropyl alcohol is a colorless, volatile liquid with a sharp odor. It is a secondary alcohol with fast evaporation and excellent solvency.
safetyHandling: Highly flammable; inhalation of vapors may cause dizziness. Ensure proper ventilation and avoid open flames.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 160–200 kg drums or 1,000 L IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and tightly sealed.

name: Thiencarbazone Methyl
description: Thiencarbazone-methyl is a white solid with formula C12H14N4O7S2 and molecular weight 390.39 g/mol. It is a triazole sulfonylurea herbicide, N-sulfonylurea with thiophene group; CAS 317815-83-1, used for weed control; low solubility in water.
physicalProperties: Thiencarbazone-methyl is a white solid with formula C12H14N4O7S2 and molecular weight 390.39 g/mol. It is a triazole sulfonylurea herbicide, N-sulfonylurea with thiophene group; CAS 317815-83-1, used for weed control; low solubility in water.
safetyHandling: Potential toxicity to bladder/urinary; irritant; biodegradation studied with microbes. Handle with PPE; environmental remediation via fungal/bacterial.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg drums or bags; store in cool, dry areas.

name: Titanium Dioxide
description: Titanium dioxide (TiO2) is a white powder with formula TiO2 and molecular weight 79.87 g/mol. Density 4.23 g/cm³ (rutile), melting point 1843°C, boiling point 2972°C, insoluble in water/acids/bases; occurs as rutile, anatase, brookite; greatest hiding power among white pigments.
physicalProperties: Titanium dioxide (TiO2) is a white powder with formula TiO2 and molecular weight 79.87 g/mol. Density 4.23 g/cm³ (rutile), melting point 1843°C, boiling point 2972°C, insoluble in water/acids/bases; occurs as rutile, anatase, brookite; greatest hiding power among white pigments.
safetyHandling: May cause silicosis-like effects from inhalation; irritant to eyes/skin/respiratory; Prop 65 cancer warning (airborne); noncombustible but dust explosive; incompatible with strong oxidizers/acids.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or bulk; store in dry, ventilated areas.

name: Caffeine
description: Caffeine is a white crystalline xanthine alkaloid naturally found in coffee and tea. It acts as a central nervous system stimulant.
physicalProperties: Caffeine is a white crystalline xanthine alkaloid naturally found in coffee and tea. It acts as a central nervous system stimulant.
safetyHandling: Safe at moderate doses; excessive consumption may cause insomnia or palpitations. Avoid humidity during storage.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or PE bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Tin Chloride
description: Tin(II) chloride, also known as stannous chloride, is a white crystalline solid with strong reducing and catalytic properties.
physicalProperties: Tin(II) chloride, also known as stannous chloride, is a white crystalline solid with strong reducing and catalytic properties.
safetyHandling: Corrosive and moisture-sensitive; releases toxic fumes on heating. Use protective gloves and goggles.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg drums or 500 g reagent bottles. Store cool, dry, and sealed from moisture.

name: Triacetin
description: Triacetin, also known as glyceryl triacetate or 1,2,3-propanetriol triacetate, is a clear colorless liquid with formula C9H14O6 and molecular weight 218.2 g/mol. It has a density of 1.16 g/mL at 25°C, boiling point 258-260°C, melting point 3°C, refractive index 1.456, and is soluble in water (64 g/L at 20°C), miscible with ethanol and toluene. Odor characteristic, pH 5.0-6.0 (50g/L at 20°C), it is produced by esterification of glycerol with acetic acid or anhydride, often involving heating, reflux, dehydration, and distillation; synthetic food-grade humectant and solvent.
physicalProperties: Triacetin, also known as glyceryl triacetate or 1,2,3-propanetriol triacetate, is a clear colorless liquid with formula C9H14O6 and molecular weight 218.2 g/mol. It has a density of 1.16 g/mL at 25°C, boiling point 258-260°C, melting point 3°C, refractive index 1.456, and is soluble in water (64 g/L at 20°C), miscible with ethanol and toluene. Odor characteristic, pH 5.0-6.0 (50g/L at 20°C), it is produced by esterification of glycerol with acetic acid or anhydride, often involving heating, reflux, dehydration, and distillation; synthetic food-grade humectant and solvent.
safetyHandling: Harmful if swallowed (H303); eye irritant; combustible when heated. Toxicity LD50 oral (rat) 3000 mg/kg; non-mutagenic in Ames test; relatively nontoxic/nonirritant at excipient levels; ADI not specified (FAO/WHO, 2001). Incompatible with strong oxidizers and metals; may react or destroy rayon fabric. Use PPE, ventilate; avoid ingestion and eye contact; stable but store away from heat/flame.
tradeRegulatory: Sealed in dry, room temperature storage in well-closed, nonmetallic containers; cool, dry place to prevent moisture; dispose contaminated as unused product.

name: Tin Oxide
description: Tin oxide is a white to off-white crystalline powder composed primarily of tin(IV) oxide. It exhibits high chemical stability, hardness, and resistance to corrosion.
physicalProperties: Tin oxide is a white to off-white crystalline powder composed primarily of tin(IV) oxide. It exhibits high chemical stability, hardness, and resistance to corrosion.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and chemically stable. Fine dust may irritate the respiratory system; use dust control and protective masks.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or multi-layer paper bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed to prevent contamination.

name: Calcium Bromide
description: Calcium bromide is a white crystalline salt with high solubility in water and hygroscopic characteristics. It provides a high-density brine solution.
physicalProperties: Calcium bromide is a white crystalline salt with high solubility in water and hygroscopic characteristics. It provides a high-density brine solution.
safetyHandling: Irritating to eyes and skin; avoid contact with moisture and oxidizers.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25–1000 kg containers or IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and tightly sealed.

name: Calcium Fluoride
description: Calcium fluoride is a naturally occurring mineral (fluorspar) with low solubility and high optical transparency.
physicalProperties: Calcium fluoride is a naturally occurring mineral (fluorspar) with low solubility and high optical transparency.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but may release fluoride fumes when heated. Avoid inhalation of dust.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25–1000 kg bags or drums. Store dry and sealed.

name: Triethanolamine
description: Triethanolamine (TEA), also known as 2,2′,2”-nitrilotriethanol, is a colorless to pale yellow viscous liquid with formula C6H15NO3 and molecular weight 149.19 g/mol. It has a density of 1.124 g/cm³, boiling point 335°C, melting point 21°C, flash point 179°C, and refractive index 1.482. Highly soluble in water and ethanol, it has a mild ammoniacal odor and acts as a weak base (pH 10.5 in 1% solution)
physicalProperties: Triethanolamine (TEA), also known as 2,2′,2”-nitrilotriethanol, is a colorless to pale yellow viscous liquid with formula C6H15NO3 and molecular weight 149.19 g/mol. It has a density of 1.124 g/cm³, boiling point 335°C, melting point 21°C, flash point 179°C, and refractive index 1.482. Highly soluble in water and ethanol, it has a mild ammoniacal odor and acts as a weak base (pH 10.5 in 1% solution)
safetyHandling: Causes skin/eye/respiratory irritation (H315, H319, H335); suspected carcinogen if contaminated with nitrosamines; avoid breathing vapors (P261), use PPE (P280). Low toxicity LD50 oral >5000 mg/kg; combustible; incompatible with strong acids/oxidizers. Stable but may form explosive mixtures; chronic aquatic effects none; ventilate areas.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 200kg iron drums or 25kg plastic pails; store tightly closed in cool, dry, ventilated places away from incompatibles per SDS guidelines.

name: Calcium Carbonate
description: Calcium carbonate is a white powder or granular mineral found naturally as limestone, chalk, or marble. It is chemically stable and insoluble in water.
physicalProperties: Calcium carbonate is a white powder or granular mineral found naturally as limestone, chalk, or marble. It is chemically stable and insoluble in water.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and inert. Avoid dust inhalation.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg or 1,000 kg bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Calcium Chloride
description: Calcium chloride is a white, hygroscopic crystalline compound with strong deliquescent properties. It is highly soluble and exothermic in water.
physicalProperties: Calcium chloride is a white, hygroscopic crystalline compound with strong deliquescent properties. It is highly soluble and exothermic in water.
safetyHandling: Irritating to skin and eyes; handle with gloves. Keep sealed to prevent moisture absorption.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg bags, 500 kg big bags, or IBC tanks. Store in dry, ventilated conditions.

name: Tricalcium Phosphate
description: Tricalcium phosphate, also known as calcium phosphate tribasic, is a white amorphous powder with formula Ca3(PO4)2 and molecular weight 310.18 g/mol. It has a density of 3.14 g/cm³, melting point 1670°C, refractive index 1.63, and is insoluble in water but soluble in dilute acids. Hygroscopic, it is produced by reaction of calcium hydroxide with phosphoric acid or from bone ash; available as food or industrial grade for calcium/phosphorus supplementation.
physicalProperties: Tricalcium phosphate, also known as calcium phosphate tribasic, is a white amorphous powder with formula Ca3(PO4)2 and molecular weight 310.18 g/mol. It has a density of 3.14 g/cm³, melting point 1670°C, refractive index 1.63, and is insoluble in water but soluble in dilute acids. Hygroscopic, it is produced by reaction of calcium hydroxide with phosphoric acid or from bone ash; available as food or industrial grade for calcium/phosphorus supplementation.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, not irritating; toxic doses by ingestion >2 g/kg. May cause mild eye/respiratory irritation from dust; no skin sensitization. Stable under normal conditions; incompatible with strong acids. Use PPE; ventilate dusty areas; LD50 oral (rat) >2000 mg/kg; no carcinogenic effects reported.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg multi-wall paper bags with PE liners or 50kg fiber drums to prevent moisture; store tightly closed in cool, dry places; non-hazardous for transport.

name: Calcium Nitrate
description: Calcium nitrate is a white, hygroscopic crystalline salt highly soluble in water. It serves as a source of both calcium and nitrate nitrogen.
physicalProperties: Calcium nitrate is a white, hygroscopic crystalline salt highly soluble in water. It serves as a source of both calcium and nitrate nitrogen.
safetyHandling: Oxidizing material; avoid contact with combustible substances. Store in cool, dry conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg PE-lined bags or 1000 kg bulk sacks. Store sealed and protected from heat.

name: Tricalcium Citrate
description: Tricalcium citrate, also known as calcium citrate tribasic, is a white odorless powder with formula C12H10Ca3O14 and molecular weight 498.43 g/mol. It has a density of 1.8 g/cm³, decomposes above 200°C, and is slightly soluble in water (0.1 g/L at 25°C), soluble in acids forming calcium ions. Hygroscopic, it is the calcium salt of citric acid, produced by neutralization of citric acid with calcium hydroxide or carbonate; available as anhydrous or tetrahydrate forms, used as calcium source in nutrition.
physicalProperties: Tricalcium citrate, also known as calcium citrate tribasic, is a white odorless powder with formula C12H10Ca3O14 and molecular weight 498.43 g/mol. It has a density of 1.8 g/cm³, decomposes above 200°C, and is slightly soluble in water (0.1 g/L at 25°C), soluble in acids forming calcium ions. Hygroscopic, it is the calcium salt of citric acid, produced by neutralization of citric acid with calcium hydroxide or carbonate; available as anhydrous or tetrahydrate forms, used as calcium source in nutrition.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, GRAS by FDA; may cause mild eye/skin/respiratory irritation (H315, H319, H335). Avoid breathing dust (P261), use protective gloves/eye gear (P280), rinse if contacted (P302+P352, P305+P351+P338). Stable under ambient conditions; incompatible with strong acids.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg or 50kg multi-wall paper bags with PE liners or fiber drums to prevent moisture; store tightly closed in cool, dry, ventilated areas; non-hazardous for transport.

name: Calcium Pidolate
description: Calcium pidolate is an organic calcium salt of pyroglutamic acid with high bioavailability. It appears as a white crystalline powder.
physicalProperties: Calcium pidolate is an organic calcium salt of pyroglutamic acid with high bioavailability. It appears as a white crystalline powder.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable. Avoid moisture to prevent caking.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or foil bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Trichloroethylene
description: Trichloroethylene (TCE), also known as trichloroethene or TRI, is a clear, colorless volatile liquid with the formula C2HCl3 and molecular weight 131.39 g/mol. It has a density of 1.46 g/cm³, boiling point 87.2°C, melting point -84.7°C, flash point none (open cup), vapor pressure 58 mmHg at 20°C, and refractive index 1.477. Slightly soluble in water (1.1 g/L at 25°C), miscible with organics like ethanol and ether, it has a sweet chloroform-like odor and is produced by chlorination of ethylene or acetylene; nonflammable solvent with high volatility.
physicalProperties: Trichloroethylene (TCE), also known as trichloroethene or TRI, is a clear, colorless volatile liquid with the formula C2HCl3 and molecular weight 131.39 g/mol. It has a density of 1.46 g/cm³, boiling point 87.2°C, melting point -84.7°C, flash point none (open cup), vapor pressure 58 mmHg at 20°C, and refractive index 1.477. Slightly soluble in water (1.1 g/L at 25°C), miscible with organics like ethanol and ether, it has a sweet chloroform-like odor and is produced by chlorination of ethylene or acetylene; nonflammable solvent with high volatility.
safetyHandling: Highly toxic, suspected carcinogen (IARC Group 1) causing liver/kidney damage and neurotoxicity; irritates eyes/skin/respiratory tract (Category 2), skin sensitizer (Category 1), mutagen (Category 2). Inhalation leads to dizziness or unconsciousness; flammable vapors possible though nonflammable.
tradeRegulatory: Typically packaged in 290kg steel drums or glass bottles for lab use; store in tightly closed containers in cool, ventilated areas away from light and incompatibles per Fisher Scientific SDS to prevent vapor release.

name: Calcium Propionate
otherNames: Calcium Propanoate, Calcium Salt of Propionic Acid
description: Calcium propionate is a white crystalline powder functioning as a mold inhibitor and preservative.
physicalProperties: Calcium propionate is a white crystalline powder functioning as a mold inhibitor and preservative.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic at regulated levels; avoid dust inhalation.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags. Store cool, dry, and away from moisture.

name: Canthaxanthin
otherNames: Food Orange 8, Carophyll Red, Lucantin Red
description: Canthaxanthin is a carotenoid pigment with red-orange coloration. It is insoluble in water but soluble in oils and organic solvents.
physicalProperties: Canthaxanthin is a carotenoid pigment with red-orange coloration. It is insoluble in water but soluble in oils and organic solvents.
safetyHandling: Approved for limited use; handle under light-protected conditions to avoid oxidation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 1–25 kg foil-sealed drums or pouches. Store cool, dry, and dark.

name: Trioctyl Trimellitate
description: Trioctyl trimellitate (TOTM), also known as tri(2-ethylhexyl) trimellitate, is a clear, almost colorless to pale yellow viscous oily liquid with the formula C30H54O6 and molecular weight 546.78 g/mol. It has a density of 0.99 g/cm³, boiling point around 414°C, flash point >240°C (closed cup), refractive index 1.485, and viscosity 190-220 mPa·s at 20°C. Insoluble in water but miscible with organic solvents like ethanol and acetone, it has low odor and volatility, stable under normal conditions but decomposes at high temperatures. Produced by esterification of trimellitic anhydride with 2-ethylhexanol, it serves as a high-performance plasticizer.
physicalProperties: Trioctyl trimellitate (TOTM), also known as tri(2-ethylhexyl) trimellitate, is a clear, almost colorless to pale yellow viscous oily liquid with the formula C30H54O6 and molecular weight 546.78 g/mol. It has a density of 0.99 g/cm³, boiling point around 414°C, flash point >240°C (closed cup), refractive index 1.485, and viscosity 190-220 mPa·s at 20°C. Insoluble in water but miscible with organic solvents like ethanol and acetone, it has low odor and volatility, stable under normal conditions but decomposes at high temperatures. Produced by esterification of trimellitic anhydride with 2-ethylhexanol, it serves as a high-performance plasticizer.
safetyHandling: Low acute toxicity with LD50 oral >5000 mg/kg (rat); mild irritant to eyes/skin/respiratory (H315, H319, H335); not classified as hazardous by GHS but combustible—avoid ignition sources (H227). Incompatible with strong oxidizers; decomposes to COx on heating. Use PPE, ventilate; no carcinogenic effects but monitor chronic exposure.
tradeRegulatory: Typically packaged in 200kg or 1000kg iron drums/IBC totes with epoxy lining for corrosion resistance; store in cool, dry, ventilated areas away from heat/oxidizers per SDS guidelines.

name: Tripotassium Phosphate
description: Tripotassium phosphate (TKPP), also known as potassium phosphate tribasic, is white hygroscopic granules or powder with formula K3PO4 and molecular weight 212.27 g/mol (anhydrous). It has a density of 2.564 g/cm³, melting point 1340°C, and is highly soluble in water (50.8 g/100mL at 25°C), forming alkaline solutions with pH 11.5-12.5. Insoluble in alcohol, it is produced by neutralization of phosphoric acid with potassium hydroxide or carbonate; available as monohydrate or trihydrate forms.
physicalProperties: Tripotassium phosphate (TKPP), also known as potassium phosphate tribasic, is white hygroscopic granules or powder with formula K3PO4 and molecular weight 212.27 g/mol (anhydrous). It has a density of 2.564 g/cm³, melting point 1340°C, and is highly soluble in water (50.8 g/100mL at 25°C), forming alkaline solutions with pH 11.5-12.5. Insoluble in alcohol, it is produced by neutralization of phosphoric acid with potassium hydroxide or carbonate; available as monohydrate or trihydrate forms.
safetyHandling: Corrosive, causes severe eye irritation (H318), skin irritation (H315), and respiratory irritation (H335); harmful if swallowed (H302). Avoid breathing dust (P261), use protective gloves/eye protection (P280), rinse eyes/skin if contacted (P305+P351+P338, P302+P352). Incompatible with acids and metals; decomposes to phosphorus oxides. Low toxicity with LD50 oral (rat) >2000 mg/kg; use PPE and ventilate.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg or 50kg multi-wall paper bags with PE liners or fiber drums to prevent moisture; non-regulated for transport; store in cool, dry, ventilated areas.

name: Caprylic Acid
description: Caprylic acid is a colorless to slightly yellow liquid fatty acid with a faint odor. It has strong antimicrobial and emulsifying properties.
physicalProperties: Caprylic acid is a colorless to slightly yellow liquid fatty acid with a faint odor. It has strong antimicrobial and emulsifying properties.
safetyHandling: Mildly irritating; handle with gloves and avoid inhalation.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 180–200 kg HDPE drums or IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and tightly sealed.

name: Carboxymethyl Cellulose
description: Carboxymethyl cellulose is a water-soluble cellulose ether derived from natural cellulose. It forms stable, viscous solutions and is available in various viscosity grades.
physicalProperties: Carboxymethyl cellulose is a water-soluble cellulose ether derived from natural cellulose. It forms stable, viscous solutions and is available in various viscosity grades.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and biodegradable; avoid dust inhalation during handling.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg multiwall paper bags or fiber drums. Store in a cool, dry environment away from humidity.

name: Trisodium Dicarboxymethyl Alaninate
description: Trisodium dicarboxymethyl alaninate, also known as MGDA-Na3 or trisodium methylglycine diacetate, is a white to off-white powder or granules with the formula C7H8NNa3O6 and molecular weight 271.11 g/mol. It has a density of 0.69 g/cm³ (powder) or 1.31 g/cm³ (40% solution), highly soluble in water forming clear solutions, but insoluble in most organics. Odorless with pH 10-12 in solution, it is a biodegradable chelating agent produced as an eco-friendly alternative to EDTA, effective for binding hard water ions like calcium and magnesium.
physicalProperties: Trisodium dicarboxymethyl alaninate, also known as MGDA-Na3 or trisodium methylglycine diacetate, is a white to off-white powder or granules with the formula C7H8NNa3O6 and molecular weight 271.11 g/mol. It has a density of 0.69 g/cm³ (powder) or 1.31 g/cm³ (40% solution), highly soluble in water forming clear solutions, but insoluble in most organics. Odorless with pH 10-12 in solution, it is a biodegradable chelating agent produced as an eco-friendly alternative to EDTA, effective for binding hard water ions like calcium and magnesium.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity with excellent toxicological profile; mild skin irritant—avoid contact, rinse with water if exposed. Non-carcinogenic, biodegradable, and eco-friendly; no danger labeling required. Handle with gloves and eye protection; incompatible with strong acids. Stable under normal conditions but store away from moisture.
tradeRegulatory: Typically packaged in 25kg bags or 1000kg big bags with polyethylene liners; store in cool, dry, ventilated areas to prevent caking per Nouryon guidelines.

name: Trisodium Phosphate
description: Trisodium phosphate (TSP), also known as sodium phosphate tribasic, is a white odorless granular or crystalline solid with formula Na3PO4 and molecular weight 163.94 g/mol (anhydrous). It has a density of 2.536 g/cm³, melting point 1583°C, and decomposes before boiling. Highly soluble in water (12 g/100mL at 20°C) forming alkaline solutions with pH 11.7-12.5, it is insoluble in organics like ethanol.
physicalProperties: Trisodium phosphate (TSP), also known as sodium phosphate tribasic, is a white odorless granular or crystalline solid with formula Na3PO4 and molecular weight 163.94 g/mol (anhydrous). It has a density of 2.536 g/cm³, melting point 1583°C, and decomposes before boiling. Highly soluble in water (12 g/100mL at 20°C) forming alkaline solutions with pH 11.7-12.5, it is insoluble in organics like ethanol.
safetyHandling: Corrosive causing skin/eye/respiratory irritation; ingestion poisonous. Avoid dust (P261), use PPE (P280), rinse contact (P302+P352). LD50 oral 6500 mg/kg; long-term links to heart disease, bone loss; environmental eutrophication. Incompatible with aluminum; decomposes to carbon oxides. Ventilate areas.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25-50kg paper bags with moisture barriers or fiber drums; non-regulated transport; handle to minimize spills; dispose rinsed for recycling per SDS.

name: Carbomer
description: Carbomer refers to a group of synthetic high-molecular-weight polymers of acrylic acid crosslinked with polyalkenyl ethers. They appear as white, fluffy powders.
physicalProperties: Carbomer refers to a group of synthetic high-molecular-weight polymers of acrylic acid crosslinked with polyalkenyl ethers. They appear as white, fluffy powders.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; dust may cause irritation to eyes and airways. Handle in ventilated conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 20–25 kg fiber drums or lined paper bags. Store dry, sealed, and protected from moisture.

name: Trisodium Citrate
description: Trisodium citrate is a chemical compound with the molecular formula Na3C6H5O7. It is sometimes referred to simply as “sodium citrate”, though sodium citrate can refer to any of the three sodium salts of citric acid. It possesses a saline, mildly tart flavor, and is a mild alkali. It is white, odorless, and has a pH of 7.5-9.0.
physicalProperties: Trisodium citrate is a chemical compound with the molecular formula Na3C6H5O7. It is sometimes referred to simply as “sodium citrate”, though sodium citrate can refer to any of the three sodium salts of citric acid. It possesses a saline, mildly tart flavor, and is a mild alkali. It is white, odorless, and has a pH of 7.5-9.0.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, GRAS by FDA; may cause mild irritation to eyes, skin, and respiratory tract; avoid contact with strong oxidizers or acids. Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) by FDA for food use; handle with gloves and goggles; incompatible with metals, may corrode some surfaces; no significant fire hazard but use water spray or CO2 for fires; thermal decomposition can release irritating gases.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 250g or 500g containers for laboratory use, or bulk in fiber drums; rinse triply for recycling or puncture for sanitary landfill disposal per Thermo Fisher Scientific guidelines.

name: Carbohydrazide
description: Carbohydrazide is a white crystalline solid used as an oxygen scavenger and corrosion inhibitor. It decomposes cleanly to gases without residue.
physicalProperties: Carbohydrazide is a white crystalline solid used as an oxygen scavenger and corrosion inhibitor. It decomposes cleanly to gases without residue.
safetyHandling: Toxic by ingestion and skin absorption; use gloves, goggles, and ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined drums or bags. Store cool, dry, and away from oxidizing materials.

name: Vanadyl Sulfate
description: Vanadyl sulfate, also known as vanadium(IV) oxide sulfate, is a blue crystalline solid or powder with the formula VOSO4·xH2O (typically pentahydrate, molecular weight 253.08 g/mol). It has a density of 2.5 g/cm³, melting point around 105°C (decomposes), and is highly soluble in water (forming acidic solutions), insoluble in ethanol. Hygroscopic, it absorbs moisture from air and is produced by reducing vanadium pentoxide with sulfur dioxide.
physicalProperties: Vanadyl sulfate, also known as vanadium(IV) oxide sulfate, is a blue crystalline solid or powder with the formula VOSO4·xH2O (typically pentahydrate, molecular weight 253.08 g/mol). It has a density of 2.5 g/cm³, melting point around 105°C (decomposes), and is highly soluble in water (forming acidic solutions), insoluble in ethanol. Hygroscopic, it absorbs moisture from air and is produced by reducing vanadium pentoxide with sulfur dioxide.
safetyHandling: Toxic by ingestion and inhalation, causes irritation to eyes, skin, and respiratory tract; may lead to chronic bronchitis or kidney damage upon prolonged exposure. Avoid strong oxidizers; non-flammable but decomposes releasing sulfur oxides. Hazard Class 6.1; use PPE and ventilate areas.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg or 50kg polypropylene bags with inner polyethylene liners for moisture protection; store tightly closed in cool, well-ventilated places as per Fisher Scientific SDS guidelines.

name: Chloroform
description: Chloroform is a colorless, volatile liquid with a sweet odor. It is a chlorinated solvent with moderate polarity and high density.
physicalProperties: Chloroform is a colorless, volatile liquid with a sweet odor. It is a chlorinated solvent with moderate polarity and high density.
safetyHandling: Toxic by inhalation and skin absorption; suspected carcinogen. Handle only under fume hood with protective equipment.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 200 L steel drums or 1,000 L IBC tanks. Store cool, dark, and tightly sealed.

name: Cobalt Hydroxide
description: Cobalt hydroxide is a pink to reddish solid formed by precipitation from cobalt salts. It is a precursor for cobalt oxides and pigments.
physicalProperties: Cobalt hydroxide is a pink to reddish solid formed by precipitation from cobalt salts. It is a precursor for cobalt oxides and pigments.
safetyHandling: Toxic if inhaled or ingested; handle with gloves and masks. Avoid dust formation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or PE bags. Store dry and sealed.

name: Cobalt Chloride
description: Cobalt chloride hexahydrate is a purple crystalline compound that changes color to blue when dehydrated. It is highly soluble in water and ethanol.
physicalProperties: Cobalt chloride hexahydrate is a purple crystalline compound that changes color to blue when dehydrated. It is highly soluble in water and ethanol.
safetyHandling: Toxic and irritant; suspected carcinogen. Handle under ventilation with protective gear.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg drums or PE-lined bags. Store sealed, cool, and dry.

name: Cobalt Sulfate
description: Cobalt sulfate is a red crystalline salt soluble in water and ethanol. It is a key source of cobalt ions for various industrial uses.
physicalProperties: Cobalt sulfate is a red crystalline salt soluble in water and ethanol. It is a key source of cobalt ions for various industrial uses.
safetyHandling: Harmful if swallowed or inhaled; avoid prolonged exposure.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined bags or drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Vinylidene Fluoride
description: Vinylidene fluoride, also known as 1,1-difluoroethylene, is a colorless flammable gas with formula CH2=CF2 and molecular weight 64.03 g/mol. Density 0.617 g/cm³ (liquid), boiling point -85.7°C, melting point -144°C, flash point -78°C; slightly soluble in water, soluble in organics; monomer for polymers.
physicalProperties: Vinylidene fluoride, also known as 1,1-difluoroethylene, is a colorless flammable gas with formula CH2=CF2 and molecular weight 64.03 g/mol. Density 0.617 g/cm³ (liquid), boiling point -85.7°C, melting point -144°C, flash point -78°C; slightly soluble in water, soluble in organics; monomer for polymers.
safetyHandling: Highly flammable gas, forms explosive mixtures with air; toxic by inhalation, irritates eyes and respiratory tract. Asphyxiant in high concentrations; avoid heat and oxidizers. Hazard Class 2.1; use PPE, ventilate, no ignition sources.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in pressurized cylinders or tanks with safety valves; store in well-ventilated areas away from heat per Sigma-Aldrich SDS.

name: Zirconium Dioxide
description: Zirconium dioxide, or zirconia, is a white powder with formula ZrO2 and molecular weight 123.22 g/mol. Density 5.68 g/cm³, melting point 2715°C, boiling point 4300°C, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; high refractive index 2.13, used in ceramics.
physicalProperties: Zirconium dioxide, or zirconia, is a white powder with formula ZrO2 and molecular weight 123.22 g/mol. Density 5.68 g/cm³, melting point 2715°C, boiling point 4300°C, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; high refractive index 2.13, used in ceramics.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; dust irritates lungs causing fibrosis. Non-flammable; avoid inhalation. Stable; use PPE, ventilate; monitor for chronic exposure.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags or drums; store in dry areas per Sigma-Aldrich SDS.

name: Collagen
description: Collagen is a fibrous structural protein found in connective tissues. It appears as a white, odorless powder derived from bovine, porcine, or marine sources.
physicalProperties: Collagen is a fibrous structural protein found in connective tissues. It appears as a white, odorless powder derived from bovine, porcine, or marine sources.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and biocompatible. Protect from moisture to prevent microbial contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 10–25 kg aluminum-lined drums or vacuum-sealed foil bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Chondroitin Sulfate
description: Chondroitin sulfate is a purified glycosaminoglycan sourced from animal cartilage or produced via fermentation. It is supplied as a fine, quality-controlled powder widely used in nutraceutical, pharmaceutical, and personal-care formulations focused on joint, mobility, and hydration benefits.
physicalProperties: Chondroitin sulfate is a purified glycosaminoglycan sourced from animal cartilage or produced via fermentation. It is supplied as a fine, quality-controlled powder widely used in nutraceutical, pharmaceutical, and personal-care formulations focused on joint, mobility, and hydration benefits.
safetyHandling: Generally safe with low toxicity. Mild digestive discomfort possible. Animal-derived grades may carry allergen concerns. Use PPE to manage dust and ensure sourcing from certified, contamination-controlled facilities.
tradeRegulatory: Packed in 25 kg fibre drums with double PE liners. Store in cool, dry warehouses away from moisture.

name: Konjac Gum
description: Konjac gum is a plant-based hydrocolloid known for its exceptional thickening and gelling functionality. Produced from purified konjac tuber, it delivers strong water-binding capability and is widely used across food, supplement, and pharmaceutical applications requiring clean-label performance.
physicalProperties: Konjac gum is a plant-based hydrocolloid known for its exceptional thickening and gelling functionality. Produced from purified konjac tuber, it delivers strong water-binding capability and is widely used across food, supplement, and pharmaceutical applications requiring clean-label performance.
safetyHandling: GRAS and safe when properly used. Tablets must be taken with sufficient water to avoid choking hazards. Low toxicity; powder can irritate eyes and respiratory tract. Use standard PPE for handling.
tradeRegulatory: Packed in 25 kg multi-wall bags with PE liners. Store cool and dry to maintain viscosity performance.

name: Crystalline Fructose
description: Crystalline fructose is a premium, high-purity sweetener valued for its clean flavour, high solubility, and sweetness efficiency. Produced from starch-based glucose using enzymatic conversion, it provides consistent performance across food, nutraceutical, and cosmetic applications.
physicalProperties: Crystalline fructose is a premium, high-purity sweetener valued for its clean flavour, high solubility, and sweetness efficiency. Produced from starch-based glucose using enzymatic conversion, it provides consistent performance across food, nutraceutical, and cosmetic applications.
safetyHandling: Generally recognised as safe. Excessive intake may lead to digestive discomfort. Dust may irritate eyes or respiratory tract. Use appropriate handling PPE and maintain dry processing conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Packed in 25 kg multi-wall paper bags with PE liners. Store cool and dry to prevent lumping and maintain flowability.

name: Chrome Oxide Green
description: Chrome oxide green is a durable, high-purity pigment known for its excellent colour strength, UV stability, and resistance to chemicals and heat. Its stability makes it suitable for demanding industrial coloring and material-enhancement applications.
physicalProperties: Chrome oxide green is a durable, high-purity pigment known for its excellent colour strength, UV stability, and resistance to chemicals and heat. Its stability makes it suitable for demanding industrial coloring and material-enhancement applications.
safetyHandling: Low acute toxicity but inhalation of fine powders should be minimised. Potential trace hexavalent chromium residues may present long-term inhalation risks. Use dust control, PPE, and appropriate extraction systems.
tradeRegulatory: Available in 25 kg multi-wall bags or 500 kg big bags. Store dry to prevent clumping and maintain pigment quality.

name: Chromic Acid
description: Chromic acid is a high-purity industrial oxidising agent supplied as dark red crystalline solids. Known for its strong reactivity and reliability in metal finishing and surface preparation, it is used where precise oxidation performance and strict process control are required.
physicalProperties: Chromic acid is a high-purity industrial oxidising agent supplied as dark red crystalline solids. Known for its strong reactivity and reliability in metal finishing and surface preparation, it is used where precise oxidation performance and strict process control are required.
safetyHandling: A highly hazardous substance with severe corrosive, carcinogenic, mutagenic, and environmental risks. Requires strict containment, full PPE, proper ventilation, and compliance with all regulatory reporting. Avoid any contact with combustibles and segregate from incompatible materials. Personnel must be trained in handling hexavalent chromium.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25–50 kg steel drums or heavy-duty lined bags. Classified as UN 1463, Oxidising Agent. Store locked, dry, and isolated in well-ventilated facilities with spill-control systems.

name: Xanthan Gum
description: Xanthan gum is an off-white to light yellow free-flowing powder produced by fermentation of glucose or sucrose with Xanthomonas campestris bacteria. It has a high molecular weight polysaccharide structure ((C35H49O29)n), density ~1.5 g/cm³, and is highly soluble in cold/hot water forming viscous, pseudoplastic solutions, but insoluble in most organics. Stable over wide pH (2-12) and temperature ranges, it exhibits excellent thickening, suspending, and emulsifying properties even at low concentrations (0.1-1%).
physicalProperties: Xanthan gum is an off-white to light yellow free-flowing powder produced by fermentation of glucose or sucrose with Xanthomonas campestris bacteria. It has a high molecular weight polysaccharide structure ((C35H49O29)n), density ~1.5 g/cm³, and is highly soluble in cold/hot water forming viscous, pseudoplastic solutions, but insoluble in most organics. Stable over wide pH (2-12) and temperature ranges, it exhibits excellent thickening, suspending, and emulsifying properties even at low concentrations (0.1-1%).
safetyHandling: GRAS by FDA (E415); low toxicity, no adverse effects at food levels. May cause mild gastrointestinal discomfort in high doses; dust irritates eyes/respiratory. Stable; avoid strong oxidizers. Use PPE for handling.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg multi-wall paper bags with PE liners or cartons; store in cool, dry, ventilated areas to prevent caking per standard industry practice.

name: Allura Red AC
description: Allura Red AC (C₁₈H₁₄N₂Na₂O₈S₂) is a bright, water-soluble synthetic azo dye used for red to orange color shades. It offers excellent heat and light stability and is widely regulated as a food and cosmetic colorant.
physicalProperties: Allura Red AC (C₁₈H₁₄N₂Na₂O₈S₂) is a bright, water-soluble synthetic azo dye used for red to orange color shades. It offers excellent heat and light stability and is widely regulated as a food and cosmetic colorant.
safetyHandling: Approved within strict maximum-use levels set by regulatory authorities. Sensitive individuals may exhibit allergic reactions; handle with gloves and minimize dust generation. Store separate from reducing agents.
tradeRegulatory: Available as fine powder or granules in 20–25 kg lined cartons or fiber drums. Keep sealed, cool, and protected from light and humidity to maintain color strength.

name: Ethylhexyl Palmitate
description: Ethylhexyl palmitate (C₂₄H₄₈O₂) is an ester of 2-ethylhexanol and palmitic acid. It appears as a clear, colorless liquid with excellent spreading properties and a dry, silky skin feel.
physicalProperties: Ethylhexyl palmitate (C₂₄H₄₈O₂) is an ester of 2-ethylhexanol and palmitic acid. It appears as a clear, colorless liquid with excellent spreading properties and a dry, silky skin feel.
safetyHandling: Considered safe and non-irritating; biodegradable and stable under normal conditions. Avoid contamination with oxidizers.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 180–200 kg HDPE drums or IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and sealed to prevent oxidation and hydrolysis.

name: Menthol Crystal
description: Menthol crystals are transparent, needle-like solids derived from peppermint oil distillation. They have a strong mint aroma and cooling effect.
physicalProperties: Menthol crystals are transparent, needle-like solids derived from peppermint oil distillation. They have a strong mint aroma and cooling effect.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic in small concentrations; handle with gloves and avoid direct skin contact to prevent irritation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or aluminum foil bags. Store cool, dry, and protected from sunlight.

name: Propylene Glycol Methyl Ether
description: Propylene Glycol Methyl Ether is a colourless liquid solvent with excellent solvency and low odour. It offers good compatibility with water and organic systems and is valued for its controlled evaporation rate and formulation flexibility in industrial applications.
physicalProperties: Propylene Glycol Methyl Ether is a colourless liquid solvent with excellent solvency and low odour. It offers good compatibility with water and organic systems and is valued for its controlled evaporation rate and formulation flexibility in industrial applications.
safetyHandling: May cause irritation to eyes and respiratory system. Vapours may cause discomfort in enclosed spaces. Use appropriate PPE and adequate ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 180 kilogram steel drums, IBC containers or bulk supply. Store in cool and dry areas away from ignition sources.

name: Sodium Persulfate
description: Sodium persulfate is a high purity white crystalline oxidising agent used widely in industrial and chemical processing sectors. It provides strong and controlled oxidation performance with reliable activation in heat or alkaline conditions. The product is supplied as a free flowing granular material and is valued for its predictable behaviour, clean decomposition and stable handling in large scale manufacturing.
physicalProperties: Sodium persulfate is a high purity white crystalline oxidising agent used widely in industrial and chemical processing sectors. It provides strong and controlled oxidation performance with reliable activation in heat or alkaline conditions. The product is supplied as a free flowing granular material and is valued for its predictable behaviour, clean decomposition and stable handling in large scale manufacturing.
safetyHandling: Irritant and powerful oxidising substance. Avoid contact with organics or combustible materials. Dust may cause respiratory sensitivity. Use appropriate PPE, good ventilation and segregated storage. Thermal breakdown can liberate oxygen.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kilogram PE lined bags or fibre drums. Store in a cool and dry warehouse, protected from heat and incompatible substances in accordance with SDS instructions.

name: Spirulina
description: Spirulina is a nutrient-dense blue-green microalgae powder known for its high protein content, natural pigments, and broad nutraceutical value. Produced through controlled cultivation and gentle drying, it serves as a premium ingredient in health, food, and feed formulations.
physicalProperties: Spirulina is a nutrient-dense blue-green microalgae powder known for its high protein content, natural pigments, and broad nutraceutical value. Produced through controlled cultivation and gentle drying, it serves as a premium ingredient in health, food, and feed formulations.
safetyHandling: Generally safe, though quality depends on contamination-free cultivation. Poor sourcing may lead to microcystin or heavy-metal presence. Dust may irritate. Use PPE and procure from certified suppliers.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg kraft bags or drums with liners. Store in cool, dry, light-protected environments to preserve pigment and nutrient stability.

name: Acid Green
description: Acid Green refers to a group of anionic dyes that deliver bright green shades for textile, leather, and paper coloration. These dyes exhibit high solubility and excellent affinity toward protein and polyamide fibers, offering durable, vivid results.
physicalProperties: Acid Green refers to a group of anionic dyes that deliver bright green shades for textile, leather, and paper coloration. These dyes exhibit high solubility and excellent affinity toward protein and polyamide fibers, offering durable, vivid results.
safetyHandling: Powdered dye may irritate skin and respiratory tract. Avoid direct contact and airborne dust through localized extraction. Wastewater containing dye residues must be treated in accordance with environmental regulations to prevent coloration of effluents.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or multi-wall paper bags lined with polyethylene. Store in a cool, dry, well-ventilated area away from sunlight and moisture.

name: Heavy Soda Ash
description: Heavy soda ash is a high-density grade of sodium carbonate tailored for industrial processes requiring reduced dusting, improved flow, and higher bulk handling efficiency. Its consistent purity makes it a preferred raw material for large-scale formulation and production operations.
physicalProperties: Heavy soda ash is a high-density grade of sodium carbonate tailored for industrial processes requiring reduced dusting, improved flow, and higher bulk handling efficiency. Its consistent purity makes it a preferred raw material for large-scale formulation and production operations.
safetyHandling: Irritant to eyes, skin, and respiratory system. Use dust control and PPE. Stable under normal conditions but reacts strongly with acids, releasing CO₂. Maintain dry storage to avoid caking.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 50 kg sacks or 1000 kg FIBCs with PE liners. Store on pallets in dry, covered areas with minimal humidity exposure.

name: Aluminum Fluoride
description: Aluminum fluoride is a white inorganic compound supplied as a fine powder or granular material for industrial use. It is widely used in metallurgical and chemical processes due to its ability to modify melting behaviour and improve process efficiency. The product is valued for its high purity, stable composition and consistent performance in large scale operations.
physicalProperties: Aluminum fluoride is a white inorganic compound supplied as a fine powder or granular material for industrial use. It is widely used in metallurgical and chemical processes due to its ability to modify melting behaviour and improve process efficiency. The product is valued for its high purity, stable composition and consistent performance in large scale operations.
safetyHandling: Irritant to eyes, skin and respiratory system. Dust inhalation should be avoided. Use appropriate PPE and ensure good ventilation. Stable under normal storage conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kilogram bags or big bags with inner liners. Store in a cool and dry warehouse protected from moisture.

name: Ammonia
description: Ammonia (NH₃) is a colorless, pungent gas that serves as one of the most essential industrial chemicals worldwide. It is highly soluble in water and forms ammonium hydroxide upon dissolution. Its high nitrogen content makes it a foundational compound for fertilizers, refrigerants, and numerous syntheses.
physicalProperties: Ammonia (NH₃) is a colorless, pungent gas that serves as one of the most essential industrial chemicals worldwide. It is highly soluble in water and forms ammonium hydroxide upon dissolution. Its high nitrogen content makes it a foundational compound for fertilizers, refrigerants, and numerous syntheses.
safetyHandling: Toxic and corrosive gas; contact with eyes or skin causes burns. Inhalation can be fatal at high concentrations. Use only in closed systems with ventilation, gas detection, and PPE.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered as anhydrous liquid in pressurized cylinders or tanks, or as aqueous ammonia in HDPE drums. Store in cool, isolated areas with secondary containment.

name: Aluminum Sulfate
description: Aluminum sulfate (Al₂(SO₄)₃·18H₂O) is a white, crystalline salt widely recognized as alum. It acts as a coagulant and flocculant in water treatment and an acidifying agent in numerous industrial applications. Contact Now
physicalProperties: Aluminum sulfate (Al₂(SO₄)₃·18H₂O) is a white, crystalline salt widely recognized as alum. It acts as a coagulant and flocculant in water treatment and an acidifying agent in numerous industrial applications. Contact Now
safetyHandling: Mildly acidic and irritating to skin and eyes. Avoid prolonged contact with moisture or alkalis to prevent decomposition. Handle with gloves and goggles.
tradeRegulatory: Packed in 25–50 kg polyethylene-lined bags or bulk containers. Store dry, sealed, and away from bases and metallic powders.

name: Aluminum Chloride Hexahydrate
description: Aluminum Chloride Hexahydrate is a white to pale yellow crystalline salt supplied in high purity grades for industrial and formulation use. It is readily soluble in water and provides reliable aluminium ion availability in aqueous systems. The product is valued for its consistency, ease of handling and predictable performance across chemical processing, water treatment and personal care applications.
physicalProperties: Aluminum Chloride Hexahydrate is a white to pale yellow crystalline salt supplied in high purity grades for industrial and formulation use. It is readily soluble in water and provides reliable aluminium ion availability in aqueous systems. The product is valued for its consistency, ease of handling and predictable performance across chemical processing, water treatment and personal care applications.
safetyHandling: Irritant to skin, eyes and respiratory system. Avoid inhalation of dust and prolonged contact. Handle with appropriate PPE and provide adequate ventilation. Hygroscopic material that absorbs moisture from air. Store sealed to prevent caking.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kilogram bags or fibre drums with moisture resistant liners. Store in a cool and dry warehouse protected from humidity and incompatible materials in accordance with SDS guidelines.

name: Alpha Linoleic Acid
description: Alpha-linolenic acid is a plant-derived omega-3 ingredient valued in nutrition, functional foods, and personal care. Offered as a pale oil or concentrated ester/triglyceride form, ALA supports health-focused formulations and is sourced from established global producers ensuring consistency, purity, and oxidative stability.
physicalProperties: Alpha-linolenic acid is a plant-derived omega-3 ingredient valued in nutrition, functional foods, and personal care. Offered as a pale oil or concentrated ester/triglyceride form, ALA supports health-focused formulations and is sourced from established global producers ensuring consistency, purity, and oxidative stability.
safetyHandling: Generally recognised as safe with low toxicity. Oxidation can occur if improperly stored, forming off-flavours or peroxides. May cause digestive discomfort at excessive doses. Handle concentrates with standard PPE and maintain nitrogen protection when possible.
tradeRegulatory: Typically packed in 25 kg aluminium containers or 190 kg nitrogen-flushed steel drums. Store cool and protected from light to maintain freshness and stability.

name: Ammonium Nitrate
description: Ammonium nitrate (NH₄NO₃) is a white, crystalline oxidizing salt valued for its high nitrogen content and reactivity. It serves as both a fertilizer and a key oxidizer in explosives.
physicalProperties: Ammonium nitrate (NH₄NO₃) is a white, crystalline oxidizing salt valued for its high nitrogen content and reactivity. It serves as both a fertilizer and a key oxidizer in explosives.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer that may detonate under confinement or contamination. Avoid exposure to heat, fuel oils, or reducing agents. Storage requires strict temperature and segregation controls.
tradeRegulatory: Packed in 25–50 kg anti-caking, moisture-resistant bags. Store in approved oxidizer facilities away from combustibles and ignition sources.

name: 1-(1-Naphthylmethyl) Quinolinium Chloride
description: This compound is a quaternary ammonium salt with a stable crystalline structure and antimicrobial activity. It is light-sensitive and soluble in polar solvents.
physicalProperties: This compound is a quaternary ammonium salt with a stable crystalline structure and antimicrobial activity. It is light-sensitive and soluble in polar solvents.
safetyHandling: Toxic and irritant; avoid inhalation and direct contact. Store under inert atmosphere, away from light.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 1–5 kg amber glass bottles or 25 kg drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Alkyl Imidazolines
description: Alkyl imidazolines are cyclic amine surfactants produced by the reaction of fatty acids with ethylenediamine. They offer amphiphilic properties and corrosion inhibition in aqueous and oil-based systems.
physicalProperties: Alkyl imidazolines are cyclic amine surfactants produced by the reaction of fatty acids with ethylenediamine. They offer amphiphilic properties and corrosion inhibition in aqueous and oil-based systems.
safetyHandling: Can cause irritation upon contact with skin or eyes. Avoid prolonged exposure and ensure appropriate ventilation during blending.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 200 kg drums or IBC tanks as viscous liquids. Store between 10–30 °C in dry, closed containers away from oxidizers.

name: 4-Carboxyphenylboronic Acid
description: 4-Carboxyphenylboronic acid is a white crystalline compound containing both carboxyl and boronic functional groups. It serves as a versatile intermediate for Suzuki coupling reactions.
physicalProperties: 4-Carboxyphenylboronic acid is a white crystalline compound containing both carboxyl and boronic functional groups. It serves as a versatile intermediate for Suzuki coupling reactions.
safetyHandling: Stable under dry conditions; may irritate skin and eyes. Handle with gloves and eye protection.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 1–25 kg aluminum foil bags or glass bottles. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Alpha Olefin Sulfonate
description: Alpha Olefin Sulfonate (C₁₂–C₁₆ AOS) is an anionic surfactant derived from linear alpha olefins. It offers excellent foaming, detergency, and biodegradability, making it a preferred alternative to traditional sulfates.
physicalProperties: Alpha Olefin Sulfonate (C₁₂–C₁₆ AOS) is an anionic surfactant derived from linear alpha olefins. It offers excellent foaming, detergency, and biodegradability, making it a preferred alternative to traditional sulfates.
safetyHandling: Low acute toxicity but may cause skin or eye irritation in concentrated form. Avoid aerosol formation during mixing.
tradeRegulatory: Available in 200 kg drums or IBC tanks as 35–40% active solutions. Store at ambient temperature, sealed and protected from freezing.

name: Amyl Salicylate
description: Amyl salicylate (C₁₂H₁₆O₃) is an ester with a floral, sweet odor widely used as a fragrance intermediate. It is stable, colorless, and soluble in most perfumery solvents.
physicalProperties: Amyl salicylate (C₁₂H₁₆O₃) is an ester with a floral, sweet odor widely used as a fragrance intermediate. It is stable, colorless, and soluble in most perfumery solvents.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but may cause mild irritation on prolonged contact. Avoid oxidation and high-temperature storage.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg aluminum or HDPE drums. Store sealed in cool, dark, ventilated conditions.

name: Amoxicillin
description: Amoxicillin is a semi-synthetic beta-lactam antibiotic derived from penicillin. It is a broad-spectrum antibacterial active against Gram-positive and certain Gram-negative bacteria.
physicalProperties: Amoxicillin is a semi-synthetic beta-lactam antibiotic derived from penicillin. It is a broad-spectrum antibacterial active against Gram-positive and certain Gram-negative bacteria.
safetyHandling: Allergic reactions possible in penicillin-sensitive individuals. Avoid dust formation during handling; pharmaceutical GMP environments required.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied as sterile powder in aluminum-sealed drums or sachets. Store cool, dry, and protected from light.

name: Ammonium Alginate
description: Ammonium alginate is the ammonium salt of alginic acid, derived from brown seaweed polysaccharides. It provides thickening, gelling, and film-forming properties in water-based systems.
physicalProperties: Ammonium alginate is the ammonium salt of alginic acid, derived from brown seaweed polysaccharides. It provides thickening, gelling, and film-forming properties in water-based systems.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; handle powders to minimize inhalable dust. Avoid prolonged exposure to moisture.
tradeRegulatory: 25 kg kraft bags with inner polyethylene liners. Store sealed, cool, and dry to maintain viscosity.

name: Ammonium Dimolybdate
description: Ammonium dimolybdate [(NH₄)₂Mo₂O₇] is a white crystalline compound used as a precursor for molybdenum oxides and catalysts.
physicalProperties: Ammonium dimolybdate [(NH₄)₂Mo₂O₇] is a white crystalline compound used as a precursor for molybdenum oxides and catalysts.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but may cause irritation if inhaled. Avoid dust generation and contact with acids.
tradeRegulatory: 25 kg polyethylene-lined fiber drums or bags. Store sealed and dry, away from moisture and reducing agents.

name: Ammonium Heptamolybdate
description: Ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate [(NH₄)₆Mo₇O₂₄·4H₂O] is a white crystalline compound widely used as a source of molybdenum in industrial and laboratory applications. It dissolves readily in water to provide molybdate ions for catalysis and corrosion inhibition.
physicalProperties: Ammonium heptamolybdate tetrahydrate [(NH₄)₆Mo₇O₂₄·4H₂O] is a white crystalline compound widely used as a source of molybdenum in industrial and laboratory applications. It dissolves readily in water to provide molybdate ions for catalysis and corrosion inhibition.
safetyHandling: Low acute toxicity but may cause irritation to eyes and respiratory tract. Avoid generation of airborne dust and direct skin contact. Environmental discharge should be minimized due to metal ion content.
tradeRegulatory: 25 kg PE-lined fiber drums or kraft bags. Store sealed, cool, and dry, protected from moisture and acids.

name: Ammonium Calcium Alginate
description: Ammonium calcium alginate is a mixed salt of alginic acid produced from brown algae. It combines the gelling capacity of calcium alginate with the solubility of ammonium alginate, offering balanced stability in aqueous systems.
physicalProperties: Ammonium calcium alginate is a mixed salt of alginic acid produced from brown algae. It combines the gelling capacity of calcium alginate with the solubility of ammonium alginate, offering balanced stability in aqueous systems.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and biocompatible; handled as an organic powder with dust control measures. Avoid prolonged exposure to moisture to prevent loss of functionality.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 20–25 kg paper bags with inner PE liners. Store in cool, dry areas protected from light and humidity.

name: Instant BCAA
description: Instant BCAA is a water-dispersible blend of leucine, isoleucine, and valine in a 2:1:1 or 4:1:1 ratio, often instantized with lecithin. It appears as a white to off-white free-flowing powder.
physicalProperties: Instant BCAA is a water-dispersible blend of leucine, isoleucine, and valine in a 2:1:1 or 4:1:1 ratio, often instantized with lecithin. It appears as a white to off-white free-flowing powder.

name: Bovine Bone Broth Powder

name: Sepiolite
description: Sepiolite is a hydrated magnesium silicate clay mineral with the formula Mg4Si6O15(OH)2·6H2O, appearing as a white or light gray fibrous powder. It has a density of about 2.0-2.3 g/cm³, high porosity (up to 200-300 m²/g surface area), and is insoluble in water but highly absorbent. Non-hygroscopic, it decomposes at high temperatures without melting and is sourced from natural deposits, often used in purified forms for industrial applications.
physicalProperties: Sepiolite is a hydrated magnesium silicate clay mineral with the formula Mg4Si6O15(OH)2·6H2O, appearing as a white or light gray fibrous powder. It has a density of about 2.0-2.3 g/cm³, high porosity (up to 200-300 m²/g surface area), and is insoluble in water but highly absorbent. safetyHandling: May cause eye, skin, and respiratory irritation from dust; avoid inhalation to prevent silicosis risk. Non-flammable but use dry chemical for fires; incompatible with strong acids. Classified as non-hazardous but use PPE and ventilation in handling.

name: Sodium Selenite 45%
description: Sodium selenite 45% is a formulation containing 45% sodium selenite (Na2SeO3), a white crystalline solid with molecular weight 172.94 g/mol. It has a density greater than water, is highly soluble in water, and appears as a powder or granules. Insoluble in organic solvents, it decomposes on heating, releasing selenium oxides. This concentration is typically blended with carriers for stability and ease of handling in industrial applications.
physicalProperties: Sodium selenite 45% is a formulation containing 45% sodium selenite (Na2SeO3), a white crystalline solid with molecular weight 172.94 g/mol. It has a density greater than water, is highly soluble in water, and appears as a powder or granules. Insoluble in organic solvents, it decomposes on heating, releasing selenium oxides. This concentration is typically blended with carriers for stability and ease of handling in industrial applications.
safetyHandling: Toxic by ingestion; contact irritates skin, eyes, and mucous membranes, potentially causing burns or allergic reactions. Avoid inhalation of dust; incompatible with strong acids or oxidizers, which may liberate hazardous selenium oxides. Hazard Class 6.1; use PPE and ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg polypropylene bags with polyethylene liners or fiber drums; store in cool, dry areas; rinse containers for recycling or puncture for sanitary landfill disposal per regulations.

name: Sodium Selenite Premix 1%-10%
description: Sodium selenite premix 1%-10% consists of sodium selenite (Na2SeO3) diluted to 1-10% concentration with inert carriers like calcium carbonate for uniform distribution. The active compound is a white powder, soluble in water, with molecular weight 172.94 g/mol and density exceeding water. It ensures controlled selenium delivery in formulations.
physicalProperties: Sodium selenite premix 1%-10% consists of sodium selenite (Na2SeO3) diluted to 1-10% concentration with inert carriers like calcium carbonate for uniform distribution. The active compound is a white powder, soluble in water, with molecular weight 172.94 g/mol and density exceeding water. It ensures controlled selenium delivery in formulations.
safetyHandling: Toxic if ingested in large amounts; dust may irritate respiratory tract, skin, and eyes. Incompatible with oxidizers; potential for selenium oxide release on heating. Use PPE; no safety concerns at intended low doses in feeds.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags or drums with liners; store sealed in dry conditions; dispose rinsed containers via recycling or landfill per guidelines.

name: Sodium Molybdate Dihydrate
description: Sodium molybdate dihydrate, formula Na2MoO4·2H2O, is a white crystalline powder with molecular weight 241.95 g/mol. It has a density of 2.37 g/mL, melting point 100°C (decomposes), and is highly soluble in water. Bulk density 1000-1400 kg/m³; stable under ambient conditions, produced by dissolving molybdenum trioxide in sodium hydroxide.
physicalProperties: Sodium molybdate dihydrate, formula Na2MoO4·2H2O, is a white crystalline powder with molecular weight 241.95 g/mol. safetyHandling: Moderate toxicity; irritates eyes, skin, and respiratory system upon contact or inhalation. Incompatible with strong reducers; releases molybdenum oxides on combustion. Not combustible; Hazard Class not specified, use PPE and ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; store at 5-30°C in tightly sealed containers; rinse triply for recycling or puncture for disposal in landfills.

name: Sodium Hyaluronate
description: Sodium hyaluronate, the sodium salt of hyaluronic acid, is a white powder with formula (C14H20NNaO11)n and variable high molecular weight. Highly hygroscopic, soluble in water forming viscous solutions, insoluble in organics; stable for 3 years in sealed containers, produced from microbial fermentation or extraction.

name: Sialic Acid
description: Sialic acid, primarily N-acetylneuraminic acid (C11H19NO9), is a white powder with molecular weight 309.27 g/mol. Soluble in water, it has a melting point around 185°C (decomposes) and is biosynthesized via aldolase enzymes. Odorless, stable, and found naturally in animals and microbes.

name: Citicoline Monosodium Salt

name: Tartrazine Lake
description: Tartrazine is a synthetic lemon yellow azo dye primarily used as a food coloring. An anionic, hydrophilic azo dye with an orange-yellow color used in fabrics, foods and cosmetics, and as a biological stain. Tartrazine is a synthetic additive with high water solubility. Tartrazine is mostly used as a colorant in the manufacture of cheese, cake, bubble gum, pudding.

name: Strontium Peroxide

It has a density of 4.56 g/cm³, decomposes above 500°C releasing oxygen, and is insoluble in water but reacts with acids. Oxidizer, produced by reacting strontium hydroxide with hydrogen peroxide.

name: Vitamin A Acetate

name: Tranexamic Acid
description: Tranexamic acid is an antifibrinolytic used to reduce or prevent hemorrhagic episodes, especially in the context of hyperfibrinolytic disorders. name: Tylosin Tartrate
description: Tylosin is a bacteriostatic macrolide antibiotic and feed additive used in veterinary medicine. It has a broad spectrum of activity against Gram-positive organisms and a limited range of Gram-negative organisms. A veterinary antibacterial used to treat sensitive mycoplasma organisms which are involved in causing chronic respiratory disease in chickens and infectious sinusitis in turkeys. It is also effective in treating swine dysentery caused by Serpulina hyodysenteriae and arthritis caused by Mycoplasma hyosynoviae.
physicalProperties: Tylosin is a bacteriostatic macrolide antibiotic and feed additive used in veterinary medicine. It is also effective in treating swine dysentery caused by Serpulina hyodysenteriae and arthritis caused by Mycoplasma hyosynoviae.
safetyHandling: May cause allergic respiratory and skin reactions; germ cell mutagenicity no data available; carcinogenicity IARC no component classified as carcinogenic; avoid breathing dust/fume/gas/mist/vapors/spray; in case of inadequate ventilation wear respiratory protection; wash hands before breaks and immediately after handling the product.

physicalProperties: Red Beet pigment, primarily betanin, is a natural anthocyano-like extract from Beta vulgaris roots, red-purple powder or liquid highly water-soluble, stable at pH 3-7 but heat-sensitive, providing E162 colorant with beet-derived antioxidants per EFSA food additive specs.
