physicalProperties: Sodium sulfide is a yellow to red flake or powder with formula Na2S and molecular weight 78.04 g/mol. Density 1.86 g/cm³, melting point 950°C; soluble in water releasing H2S, strong rotten egg odor. Hygroscopic; produced by reducing sodium sulfate with carbon.
safetyHandling: Corrosive to eyes, skin, respiratory tract; toxic on ingestion. Flammable; releases H2S with acids; Hazard Class 8.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with liners; store airtight; dispose rinsed containers in landfills.

name: Gluten
description: Gluten is a natural protein complex composed of gliadin and glutenin fractions found in wheat. It forms viscoelastic networks when hydrated, giving dough its elasticity and strength.
physicalProperties: Gluten is a natural protein complex composed of gliadin and glutenin fractions found in wheat. It forms viscoelastic networks when hydrated, giving dough its elasticity and strength.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic for general handling, though unsuitable for individuals with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity. Avoid prolonged exposure to humidity to prevent spoilage.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg kraft paper bags with inner PE liners. Store in dry, cool warehouses away from moisture and pests.

name: Guar Gum
description: Guar gum is a polysaccharide obtained from the endosperm of Cyamopsis tetragonoloba seeds. It is a free-flowing powder with high viscosity even at low concentrations.
physicalProperties: Guar gum is a polysaccharide obtained from the endosperm of Cyamopsis tetragonoloba seeds. It is a free-flowing powder with high viscosity even at low concentrations.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and biodegradable. Inhalation of fine dust may cause mechanical irritation; handle under ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg multiwall paper bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed to maintain viscosity and prevent microbial contamination.

name: Gum Arabic
description: Gum Arabic is a natural exudate from Acacia senegal or Acacia seyal trees. It is a complex polysaccharide with high solubility and emulsifying properties.
physicalProperties: Gum Arabic is a natural exudate from Acacia senegal or Acacia seyal trees. It is a complex polysaccharide with high solubility and emulsifying properties.
safetyHandling: Recognized as safe for food use; sensitive to humidity and microbial contamination. Avoid prolonged exposure to moisture.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags or drums. Store sealed in dry, cool conditions away from sunlight.

name: Silver Nitrate
description: Silver nitrate is a colorless crystalline solid and a powerful oxidizing agent. It exhibits high solubility in water and strong light sensitivity, darkening upon exposure.
physicalProperties: Silver nitrate is a colorless crystalline solid and a powerful oxidizing agent. It exhibits high solubility in water and strong light sensitivity, darkening upon exposure.
safetyHandling: Corrosive and oxidizing; causes burns and stains on contact. Store away from light, organics, and reducing agents.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 1–25 kg amber glass bottles or HDPE containers. Store cool, dry, and dark.

name: Light Soda Ash
description: Light soda ash, or sodium carbonate light, is a white granular powder with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. It has a density of 0.5-0.6 g/cm³, melting point 851°C, highly soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), alkaline pH 11.6; hygroscopic, produced by Solvay process, lighter than dense grade for better dissolution.
physicalProperties: Light soda ash, or sodium carbonate light, is a white granular powder with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. It has a density of 0.5-0.6 g/cm³, melting point 851°C, highly soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), alkaline pH 11.6; hygroscopic, produced by Solvay process, lighter than dense grade for better dissolution.
safetyHandling: Irritates eyes, skin, and respiratory tract; harmful if swallowed causing gastrointestinal issues. Avoid acids releasing CO2; non-combustible but decomposes at high heat. Stable; Hazard Class not specified; use PPE, ventilate dusty areas.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25-50kg polypropylene bags or big bags; store in dry, covered warehouses per Brenntag product info.

name: Glycine
description: Glycine is the simplest amino acid, appearing as a white crystalline powder with sweet taste. It functions as a proteinogenic amino acid and biochemical buffer.
physicalProperties: Glycine is the simplest amino acid, appearing as a white crystalline powder with sweet taste. It functions as a proteinogenic amino acid and biochemical buffer.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; avoid moisture absorption.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg PE-lined bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Gluconic Acid
description: Gluconic acid is a mild organic acid produced by glucose oxidation using Aspergillus niger. It is biodegradable and chelates in nature.
physicalProperties: Gluconic acid is a mild organic acid produced by glucose oxidation using Aspergillus niger. It is biodegradable and chelates in nature.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; stable under normal conditions. Avoid mixing with strong oxidizers.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25–50 kg drums or IBC tanks. Store sealed, cool, and dry.

name: Sodium Nitrate
description: Sodium nitrate, known as Chile saltpeter, is a white crystalline solid with formula NaNO3 and molecular weight 84.99 g/mol. Density 2.26 g/cm³, melting point 308°C, decomposes above 380°C; highly soluble in water (921 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced via neutralization of nitric acid with sodium carbonate.
physicalProperties: Sodium nitrate, known as Chile saltpeter, is a white crystalline solid with formula NaNO3 and molecular weight 84.99 g/mol. Density 2.26 g/cm³, melting point 308°C, decomposes above 380°C; highly soluble in water (921 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced via neutralization of nitric acid with sodium carbonate.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer; fire risk with combustibles, toxic if ingested causing methemoglobinemia. Irritates skin/eyes; Hazard Class 5.1 for transport.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or bulk; store away from organics; rinse containers for recycling or puncture for sanitary disposal.

name: Sodium Nitrite
description: Sodium nitrite is a white to yellowish crystalline powder with formula NaNO2 and molecular weight 69.00 g/mol. Density 2.17 g/cm³, melting point 271°C, decomposes above 320°C; soluble in water (848 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with slight odor. Acts as both oxidant and reductant; produced by absorbing nitrogen oxides in sodium carbonate solution.
physicalProperties: Sodium nitrite is a white to yellowish crystalline powder with formula NaNO2 and molecular weight 69.00 g/mol. Density 2.17 g/cm³, melting point 271°C, decomposes above 320°C; soluble in water (848 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with slight odor. Acts as both oxidant and reductant; produced by absorbing nitrogen oxides in sodium carbonate solution.
safetyHandling: Toxic; forms carcinogenic nitrosamines, causing methemoglobinemia. Oxidizer; avoid acids and combustibles. Hazard Class 5.1/6.1 with PPE required.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; store cool and dry; rinse for recycling or puncture packaging for landfill.

name: Sodium Saccharin
description: Sodium saccharin, the sodium salt of saccharin, is a white crystalline powder with formula C7H4NNaO3S·2H2O and molecular weight 241.20 g/mol. Density 0.83 g/cm³, melting point >300°C (decomposes); highly soluble in water (up to 670 g/L), sweet taste 300-500 times sucrose. Odorless, pH neutral to alkaline in solution; synthesized from toluene via sulfonation and oxidation.
physicalProperties: Sodium saccharin, the sodium salt of saccharin, is a white crystalline powder with formula C7H4NNaO3S·2H2O and molecular weight 241.20 g/mol. Density 0.83 g/cm³, melting point >300°C (decomposes); highly soluble in water (up to 670 g/L), sweet taste 300-500 times sucrose. Odorless, pH neutral to alkaline in solution; synthesized from toluene via sulfonation and oxidation.
safetyHandling: Safe with ADI 9 mg/kg bw/day; may cause allergic reactions or sensitivity. Non-toxic but monitor intake for high consumers.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg cartons or drums with liners; rinse containers for recycling or puncture for sanitary disposal.

name: Sodium Cyclamate
description: Sodium cyclamate, also known as sodium N-cyclohexylsulfamate, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H12NNaO3S and molecular weight 201.22 g/mol. It is highly soluble in water (up to 200 g/L at 20°C), odorless with a sweet taste 30 times that of sucrose, pH 5.5-7.5 in solution. Hygroscopic, stable under normal conditions; produced by sulfonating cyclohexylamine with sodium hydroxide. Used as a non-nutritive sweetener but restricted in some regions.
physicalProperties: Sodium cyclamate, also known as sodium N-cyclohexylsulfamate, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H12NNaO3S and molecular weight 201.22 g/mol. It is highly soluble in water (up to 200 g/L at 20°C), odorless with a sweet taste 30 times that of sucrose, pH 5.5-7.5 in solution. Hygroscopic, stable under normal conditions; produced by sulfonating cyclohexylamine with sodium hydroxide. Used as a non-nutritive sweetener but restricted in some regions.
safetyHandling: May cause cancer; banned in US per FDA; irritant to eyes, skin, respiratory tract; avoid ingestion, inhalation. Not flammable but incompatible with strong oxidizers; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg fiber drums or polypropylene bags with PE liners; rinse containers for recycling or dispose in sanitary landfills per regulations.

name: Sodium Silicate
description: Sodium silicate, also known as water glass, is a colorless to white viscous liquid or powder with formula Na2SiO3 and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol. Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, decomposes in acids; produced by fusing silica with sodium carbonate.
physicalProperties: Sodium silicate, also known as water glass, is a colorless to white viscous liquid or powder with formula Na2SiO3 and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol. Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, decomposes in acids; produced by fusing silica with sodium carbonate.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive, causes severe burns to skin, eyes, mucous membranes; inhalation irritates respiratory tract. Avoid acids; Hazard Class 8; use PPE and ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg bags, drums, or bulk tanks; rinse containers triply for recycling or puncture for landfill disposal.

name: Sodium Sulfate
description: Sodium sulfate, also known as Glauber’s salt, is a white crystalline solid with formula Na2SO4 and molecular weight 142.04 g/mol. Density 2.66 g/cm³, melting point 884°C; highly soluble in water (up to 139 g/L at 20°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced from natural deposits or as a byproduct of chemical processes.
physicalProperties: Sodium sulfate, also known as Glauber’s salt, is a white crystalline solid with formula Na2SO4 and molecular weight 142.04 g/mol. Density 2.66 g/cm³, melting point 884°C; highly soluble in water (up to 139 g/L at 20°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced from natural deposits or as a byproduct of chemical processes.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but dust irritates eyes, skin, respiratory tract; ingestion causes gastrointestinal upset. Non-flammable; avoid strong acids; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg or 50kg bags with liners; store dry; rinse for recycling or dispose in landfills.

name: Glucosamine Hydrochloride
description: Glucosamine hydrochloride is an amino sugar derived from the exoskeletons of shellfish or produced by fermentation. It is a white crystalline powder with high solubility in water and stability in tablet formulations.
physicalProperties: Glucosamine hydrochloride is an amino sugar derived from the exoskeletons of shellfish or produced by fermentation. It is a white crystalline powder with high solubility in water and stability in tablet formulations.
safetyHandling: Generally recognized as safe (GRAS); may cause mild gastrointestinal effects in sensitive individuals. Handle under dry, ventilated conditions to prevent hygroscopic clumping.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined fiber drums or aluminum foil bags. Store in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and moisture to maintain purity and flowability.

name: Sodium Metasilicate
description: Sodium metasilicate, also called sodium silicate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2SiO3 (anhydrous) or Na2SiO3·5H2O (pentahydrate) and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol (anhydrous). Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, it decomposes in acids releasing silica; produced by fusing sodium carbonate with silica.
physicalProperties: Sodium metasilicate, also called sodium silicate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2SiO3 (anhydrous) or Na2SiO3·5H2O (pentahydrate) and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol (anhydrous). Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, it decomposes in acids releasing silica; produced by fusing sodium carbonate with silica.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive; causes severe burns to skin and eyes, risk of blindness. Avoid acids; use PPE and ventilation. Hazard Class 8 for transport.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with secure closures; for disposal, rinse triply and recycle or puncture packaging for landfill.

name: Sodium Molybdate
description: Sodium molybdate, typically as dihydrate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2MoO4·2H2O and molecular weight 241.95 g/mol. Density 2.7 g/cm³, melting point 687°C (anhydrous); highly soluble in water (840 g/L at 20°C), insoluble in organics. Hygroscopic, pH 9-10 in solution; produced by dissolving molybdenum trioxide in sodium hydroxide.
physicalProperties: Sodium molybdate, typically as dihydrate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2MoO4·2H2O and molecular weight 241.95 g/mol. Density 2.7 g/cm³, melting point 687°C (anhydrous); highly soluble in water (840 g/L at 20°C), insoluble in organics. Hygroscopic, pH 9-10 in solution; produced by dissolving molybdenum trioxide in sodium hydroxide.
safetyHandling: Moderate toxicity; irritates eyes and skin, inhalation risk low. Avoid ingestion; use PPE. Non-flammable but incompatible with strong reducers.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with polyethylene liners; rinse for recycling or puncture for disposal in landfills per guidelines.

name: Glycidyl Methacrylate
description: Glycidyl methacrylate (C₇H₁₀O₃) is a reactive monomer containing both epoxy and methacrylate groups, offering strong adhesion and crosslinking potential.
physicalProperties: Glycidyl methacrylate (C₇H₁₀O₃) is a reactive monomer containing both epoxy and methacrylate groups, offering strong adhesion and crosslinking potential.
safetyHandling: Irritating to skin and eyes; sensitizer by contact. Avoid heat and sunlight to prevent polymerization.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 180–200 kg drums. Store cool, dry, and stabilized under nitrogen.

name: Sodium Carboxymethyl Cellulose
description: Sodium carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC or carboxymethylcellulose sodium) is a white, odorless, hygroscopic powder derived from cellulose, with formula [C6H7O2(OH)2OCH2COONa]n and variable molecular weight (90,000-700,000 g/mol). Density 1.6 g/cm³, no fixed melting point (decomposes >300°C); forms viscous colloidal solutions in water, insoluble in organics. pH 6.5-8.5; degree of substitution 0.7-1.2 affects viscosity. Produced by reacting cellulose with chloroacetic acid and NaOH.
physicalProperties: Sodium carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC or carboxymethylcellulose sodium) is a white, odorless, hygroscopic powder derived from cellulose, with formula [C6H7O2(OH)2OCH2COONa]n and variable molecular weight (90,000-700,000 g/mol). Density 1.6 g/cm³, no fixed melting point (decomposes >300°C); forms viscous colloidal solutions in water, insoluble in organics. pH 6.5-8.5; degree of substitution 0.7-1.2 affects viscosity. Produced by reacting cellulose with chloroacetic acid and NaOH.
safetyHandling: Safe, but dust irritates eyes/respiratory; incompatible with strong acids. Low explosion risk; GRAS by FDA, no consumer concerns in additives.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg multi-layer paper bags with PE or fiber drums. Store dry to prevent caking.

name: Sodium Carbonate
description: Sodium carbonate, or soda ash, is a white, hygroscopic powder or granules with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. Density 2.54 g/cm³ (anhydrous), melting point 851°C, decomposes before boiling. Soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), forming alkaline solutions pH 11-12; insoluble in organics. Absorbs moisture and CO2 from air. Exists as anhydrous, monohydrate, or decahydrate; produced via Solvay process from NaCl and limestone.
physicalProperties: Sodium carbonate, or soda ash, is a white, hygroscopic powder or granules with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. Density 2.54 g/cm³ (anhydrous), melting point 851°C, decomposes before boiling. Soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), forming alkaline solutions pH 11-12; insoluble in organics. Absorbs moisture and CO2 from air. Exists as anhydrous, monohydrate, or decahydrate; produced via Solvay process from NaCl and limestone.
safetyHandling: Irritates eyes; mildly toxic if ingested. GRAS by FDA/EPA; avoid acids to prevent CO2 release. Not hazardous for transport.
tradeRegulatory: In 25-50kg bags or bulk supersacks. Store dry to avoid caking.

name: Sodium Chlorate
description: Sodium chlorate is a white, odorless, hygroscopic crystalline solid with formula NaClO3 and molecular weight 106.44 g/mol. Density 2.49 g/cm³, melting point 248°C, decomposes above 300°C releasing oxygen. Highly soluble in water (790 g/L at 0°C), slightly in alcohols; pH neutral. Strong oxidizer; produced electrolytically from NaCl. Forms dihydrate or tetrahydrate in humid conditions.
physicalProperties: Sodium chlorate is a white, odorless, hygroscopic crystalline solid with formula NaClO3 and molecular weight 106.44 g/mol. Density 2.49 g/cm³, melting point 248°C, decomposes above 300°C releasing oxygen. Highly soluble in water (790 g/L at 0°C), slightly in alcohols; pH neutral. Strong oxidizer; produced electrolytically from NaCl. Forms dihydrate or tetrahydrate in humid conditions.
safetyHandling: Powerful oxidizer; fire/explosion risk with organics/metals. Toxic if ingested; irritates skin/eyes. UN1495, Hazard Class 5.1.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or 1MT big bags. Store away from combustibles, cool and dry.

name: Sodium Chlorite
description: Sodium chlorite is a white to pale yellow crystalline solid or flakes with formula NaClO2 and molecular weight 90.44 g/mol. Density 2.5 g/cm³, decomposes explosively above 180-200°C; highly soluble in water (390 g/L at 17°C), slightly in methanol. pH of solutions 10-11; strong oxidizer, forms trihydrate or monohydrate. Produced by reducing sodium chlorate with acids or reductants.
physicalProperties: Sodium chlorite is a white to pale yellow crystalline solid or flakes with formula NaClO2 and molecular weight 90.44 g/mol. Density 2.5 g/cm³, decomposes explosively above 180-200°C; highly soluble in water (390 g/L at 17°C), slightly in methanol. pH of solutions 10-11; strong oxidizer, forms trihydrate or monohydrate. Produced by reducing sodium chlorate with acids or reductants.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer; fire risk with organics/sulfur. Corrosive; ingestion causes nausea. UN1496, Hazard Class 5.1; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25-50kg iron/fiber drums with liners; solutions in plastic. Store cool, dry.

name: Sodium Chloride
description: Sodium chloride, commonly known as table salt or halite, is a white, odorless crystalline solid with the formula NaCl and molecular weight 58.44 g/mol. It has a density of 2.16 g/cm³, melting point of 801°C, and boiling point of 1465°C. Highly soluble in water (up to 359 g/L at 25°C), it forms neutral solutions and is insoluble in most organic solvents. It is hygroscopic, absorbing moisture from air, and is produced via evaporation of seawater or mining rock salt deposits.
physicalProperties: Sodium chloride, commonly known as table salt or halite, is a white, odorless crystalline solid with the formula NaCl and molecular weight 58.44 g/mol. It has a density of 2.16 g/cm³, melting point of 801°C, and boiling point of 1465°C. Highly soluble in water (up to 359 g/L at 25°C), it forms neutral solutions and is insoluble in most organic solvents. It is hygroscopic, absorbing moisture from air, and is produced via evaporation of seawater or mining rock salt deposits.
safetyHandling: Generally safe but excessive ingestion can cause hypertension and cardiovascular diseases like stroke; dust may irritate eyes and respiratory tract. Non-flammable; avoid mixing with strong oxidizers. Classified non-hazardous for transport; use PPE in handling.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg or 50kg polypropylene bags with polyethylene liners, or fiber drums; containers can be rinsed for recycling or punctured for disposal in sanitary landfills to ensure environmental compliance.

name: Sodium Lauryl Sarcosinate
description: Sodium lauryl sarcosinate, also known as sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, is a white to off-white powder or liquid with formula C15H28NNaO3 and molecular weight 293.38 g/mol. It has a density of about 1.03 g/cm³ in solution, pH 7.5-8.5, and is freely soluble in water, forming mild foaming solutions. Derived from sarcosine (N-methylglycine) and lauroyl chloride, it is an amino acid-based anionic surfactant with good biodegradability and low toxicity.
physicalProperties: Sodium lauryl sarcosinate, also known as sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, is a white to off-white powder or liquid with formula C15H28NNaO3 and molecular weight 293.38 g/mol. It has a density of about 1.03 g/cm³ in solution, pH 7.5-8.5, and is freely soluble in water, forming mild foaming solutions. Derived from sarcosine (N-methylglycine) and lauroyl chloride, it is an amino acid-based anionic surfactant with good biodegradability and low toxicity.
safetyHandling: Mild irritant to eyes and skin; low toxicity but avoid inhalation of dust or prolonged contact. Biocompatible; stable with chlorine; use ventilation and PPE to minimize exposure risks.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags or drums with inner liners; for disposal, rinse containers and offer for recycling or puncture for landfill disposal per regulations.

name: Acai Berry Extract
description: Acai berry extract is obtained from Euterpe oleracea fruit and is rich in anthocyanins, flavonoids, and essential fatty acids. It exhibits powerful antioxidant capacity, supporting anti-aging and anti-inflammatory benefits.
physicalProperties: Acai berry extract is obtained from Euterpe oleracea fruit and is rich in anthocyanins, flavonoids, and essential fatty acids. It exhibits powerful antioxidant capacity, supporting anti-aging and anti-inflammatory benefits.
safetyHandling: Generally recognized as safe; sensitive to heat and oxidation. Avoid prolonged exposure to air and humidity to preserve antioxidant potency.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 10–25 kg foil-lined fiber drums or PE-sealed bags. Store in a cool, dark, and dry area to maintain bioactivity.

name: Boswellia Serrata Extract
description: Boswellia serrata extract is a brown resin powder with boswellic acids (60-90%). Soluble in ethanol, used for anti-inflammatory; standardized extract from frankincense tree.
physicalProperties: Boswellia serrata extract is a brown resin powder with boswellic acids (60-90%). Soluble in ethanol, used for anti-inflammatory; standardized extract from frankincense tree.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause diarrhea or skin rash. Irritates eyes; stable. Use PPE; no major hazards but avoid in pregnancy.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg multi-wall paper bags with liners; store in dry, ventilated warehouses per general extract guidelines.

name: African Geranium Extract
description: African Geranium (Pelargonium sidoides) extract is a botanical preparation standardized for coumarins and polyphenols. It provides antimicrobial and expectorant properties widely used in respiratory health products.
physicalProperties: African Geranium (Pelargonium sidoides) extract is a botanical preparation standardized for coumarins and polyphenols. It provides antimicrobial and expectorant properties widely used in respiratory health products.
safetyHandling: Considered safe at therapeutic doses. Overuse may cause mild gastrointestinal discomfort. Store in dry, sealed containers to prevent microbial contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied as dry extract powder in 10–25 kg aluminum drums or PE-lined bags. Store at ambient temperature, away from humidity and light.

name: Citrus Extract
otherNames: Bitter Orange Extract, Citrus Aurantium Extract
description: Citrus extract is a yellow to orange powder obtained from citrus peels rich in flavonoids, vitamin C, and limonoids. It provides antioxidant and antimicrobial activity.
physicalProperties: Citrus extract is a yellow to orange powder obtained from citrus peels rich in flavonoids, vitamin C, and limonoids. It provides antioxidant and antimicrobial activity.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and biodegradable; avoid prolonged exposure to light.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 10–25 kg foil-lined fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Ginkgo Biloba Extract
description: Ginkgo biloba extract is obtained from the dried leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree and standardized to ginkgoflavone glycosides and terpene lactones. It offers antioxidant, neuroprotective, and vasodilatory properties.
physicalProperties: Ginkgo biloba extract is obtained from the dried leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree and standardized to ginkgoflavone glycosides and terpene lactones. It offers antioxidant, neuroprotective, and vasodilatory properties.
safetyHandling: Generally well-tolerated at recommended doses; high intake may increase bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants. Avoid exposure to humidity and sunlight to maintain potency.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 10–25 kg aluminum-lined fiber drums or vacuum-sealed foil bags. Store in cool, dry, and dark conditions.

name: Berberine
description: Berberine is a yellow crystalline powder with formula C20H18NO4+ and molecular weight 336.36 g/mol. Density 1.3 g/cm³, melting point 145°C, soluble in hot water and ethanol; alkaloid from plants like Berberis, used for antimicrobial effects.
physicalProperties: Berberine is a yellow crystalline powder with formula C20H18NO4+ and molecular weight 336.36 g/mol. Density 1.3 g/cm³, melting point 145°C, soluble in hot water and ethanol; alkaloid from plants like Berberis, used for antimicrobial effects.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause gastrointestinal issues or drug interactions. Irritates eyes; avoid with medications. Stable; use PPE; monitor for cytochrome inhibition.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg fiber drums with inner PE liners; store in dry, cool places per ChemicalBook MSDS to avoid moisture.

name: Bromelain Extract
description: Bromelain extract is a yellow powder from pineapple stem, containing proteolytic enzymes (activity 2400 GDU/g). Soluble in water, pH 5-7; produced by extraction, used for protein digestion.
physicalProperties: Bromelain extract is a yellow powder from pineapple stem, containing proteolytic enzymes (activity 2400 GDU/g). Soluble in water, pH 5-7; produced by extraction, used for protein digestion.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause allergies or bleeding risk with anticoagulants. Irritates skin; avoid dust. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but monitor sensitivities.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg fiber drums with moisture barriers; store in cool, dry areas per Sigma-Aldrich SDS to preserve enzyme activity.

name: Hesperidin
description: Hesperidin is a citrus bioflavonoid commonly extracted from orange and lemon peels. It appears as a pale-yellow crystalline powder and possesses strong antioxidant and capillary-protective effects.
physicalProperties: Hesperidin is a citrus bioflavonoid commonly extracted from orange and lemon peels. It appears as a pale-yellow crystalline powder and possesses strong antioxidant and capillary-protective effects.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; store dry and protected from light to prevent oxidation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 10–25 kg aluminum-lined drums or foil bags. Store sealed in a cool, dry environment.

name: Mallow Extract
description: Mallow extract is derived from Malva sylvestris flowers and leaves, rich in mucilage, flavonoids, and anthocyanins. It provides soothing, anti-inflammatory, and emollient effects.
physicalProperties: Mallow extract is derived from Malva sylvestris flowers and leaves, rich in mucilage, flavonoids, and anthocyanins. It provides soothing, anti-inflammatory, and emollient effects.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and well-tolerated; avoid microbial contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 10–25 kg aluminum-lined drums or foil pouches. Store cool, dark, and dry.

name: L-Spreng Extract
description: L-Spreng extract, derived from Uncaria tomentosa, contains oxindole alkaloids and polyphenols with immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects. It appears as a brown fine powder.
physicalProperties: L-Spreng extract, derived from Uncaria tomentosa, contains oxindole alkaloids and polyphenols with immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects. It appears as a brown fine powder.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and GRAS; store away from light and moisture to maintain potency.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 10–25 kg foil-lined drums or pouches. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Quercetin
description: Quercetin is a yellow crystalline flavonoid naturally found in fruits and vegetables. It exhibits strong antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.
physicalProperties: Quercetin is a yellow crystalline flavonoid naturally found in fruits and vegetables. It exhibits strong antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; sensitive to light and oxidation. Store in airtight containers away from humidity.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 1–25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dark, and sealed.

name: Artichoke Extract
otherNames: Pelargonium Sidoides Extract, Geranium Root Extract
description: Artichoke extract is obtained from Cynara scolymus leaves, standardized for cynarin and chlorogenic acids. It supports liver detoxification and lipid metabolism.
physicalProperties: Artichoke extract is obtained from Cynara scolymus leaves, standardized for cynarin and chlorogenic acids. It supports liver detoxification and lipid metabolism.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic under normal use; store away from light and humidity to protect active compounds.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 10–25 kg foil-lined fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Avena Sativa Extract
description: Avena sativa (oat) extract is obtained from oat kernels and is rich in avenanthramides, saponins, and polysaccharides. It provides soothing and antioxidant properties in topical and nutritional applications.
physicalProperties: Avena sativa (oat) extract is obtained from oat kernels and is rich in avenanthramides, saponins, and polysaccharides. It provides soothing and antioxidant properties in topical and nutritional applications.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and hypoallergenic. Avoid microbial contamination by maintaining dry, sealed storage conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied as powder or liquid extract in 10–25 kg drums or HDPE containers. Store in cool, dry, and dark environments.

name: Bearberry Extract
description: Bearberry extract is derived from the leaves of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, rich in natural arbutin and polyphenols. It is known for its depigmenting, antioxidant, and astringent properties, making it highly valued in skincare and cosmetic formulations.
physicalProperties: Bearberry extract is derived from the leaves of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, rich in natural arbutin and polyphenols. It is known for its depigmenting, antioxidant, and astringent properties, making it highly valued in skincare and cosmetic formulations.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic under cosmetic use concentrations. Prolonged exposure to high temperatures or UV light may degrade arbutin. Avoid direct inhalation of powder; store away from sunlight.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied as a light-brown powder in 10–25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store in a cool, dry place, sealed tightly to protect from moisture.

name: Zinc Glycinate
description: Zinc glycinate is a chelated mineral complex combining zinc with glycine for enhanced absorption and reduced gastrointestinal irritation. It is a white, free-flowing powder used across nutrition sectors.
physicalProperties: Zinc glycinate is a chelated mineral complex combining zinc with glycine for enhanced absorption and reduced gastrointestinal irritation. It is a white, free-flowing powder used across nutrition sectors.
safetyHandling: Safe under typical dietary usage. Avoid prolonged exposure to moisture and high temperatures to prevent degradation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums with inner PE liners. Store in a cool, dry environment, tightly closed.

name: Propolis Extract
description: Propolis Extract is a complex resinous substance harvested by bees from plant exudates, typically a dark brown viscous liquid or powder rich in polyphenols, terpenoids, and aromatic acids. Ethanol-soluble with antimicrobial and antioxidant profiles, it varies in composition based on geographic origin for tailored bioactivity.
physicalProperties: Propolis Extract is a complex resinous substance harvested by bees from plant exudates, typically a dark brown viscous liquid or powder rich in polyphenols, terpenoids, and aromatic acids. Ethanol-soluble with antimicrobial and antioxidant profiles, it varies in composition based on geographic origin for tailored bioactivity.
safetyHandling: Potential for contact allergies in bee-sensitive individuals; mild hepatotoxicity in high doses. Test for propolis-specific sensitivities; dilute extracts and avoid immunocompromised formulations per quality assurance.
tradeRegulatory: Poured into 5 kg amber glass carboys with phenolic caps for light blocking. Refrigerate in allergen-controlled zones to sustain potency.

name: Zinc Picolinate
description: Zinc picolinate (C₁₂H₈N₂O₄Zn) is a highly bioavailable zinc chelate formed with picolinic acid. It appears as a white crystalline powder with excellent absorption efficiency.
physicalProperties: Zinc picolinate (C₁₂H₈N₂O₄Zn) is a highly bioavailable zinc chelate formed with picolinic acid. It appears as a white crystalline powder with excellent absorption efficiency.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic at nutritional dosages. Avoid direct sunlight and humidity. Handle with standard hygiene practices.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg aluminum foil drums or PE-lined bags. Store sealed, cool, and dry.

name: Vitex Extract
description: Vitex extract, derived from Vitex agnus-castus berries, is a brown powder or liquid with active compounds like casticin and vitexin. It has a characteristic herbal odor, soluble in ethanol and water, standardized to 0.5-5% agnusides or 0.6% amentoflavone. Density around 1.2 g/cm³, pH 4-6 in solution; produced by solvent extraction or supercritical CO2, used for hormonal balance in supplements.
physicalProperties: Vitex extract, derived from Vitex agnus-castus berries, is a brown powder or liquid with active compounds like casticin and vitexin. It has a characteristic herbal odor, soluble in ethanol and water, standardized to 0.5-5% agnusides or 0.6% amentoflavone. Density around 1.2 g/cm³, pH 4-6 in solution; produced by solvent extraction or supercritical CO2, used for hormonal balance in supplements.
safetyHandling: May cause nausea or rash in sensitive individuals; avoid in pregnancy due to hormonal effects. Low toxicity but interact with medications; stable but light-sensitive. Use PPE; no major hazards but monitor for allergies.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg fiber drums with PE liners or glass bottles for lab; store in cool, dark places per Sigma-Aldrich SDS.

name: Hydroxy Tryptophan
description: 5-Hydroxytryptophan is a naturally occurring amino acid and serotonin precursor extracted from Griffonia simplicifolia. It appears as a white to off-white crystalline powder.
physicalProperties: 5-Hydroxytryptophan is a naturally occurring amino acid and serotonin precursor extracted from Griffonia simplicifolia. It appears as a white to off-white crystalline powder.
safetyHandling: Generally safe at recommended doses; excessive intake may cause mild gastrointestinal upset. Avoid moisture exposure.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 10–25 kg foil-lined fiber drums or pouches. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Iron Ammonium Citrate
description: Iron ammonium citrate is a brown or green powder with formula variable (C6H8FeNO7), molecular weight ~261 g/mol. Soluble in water, used as iron source; obtained from citric acid and iron salts.
physicalProperties: Iron ammonium citrate is a brown or green powder with formula variable (C6H8FeNO7), molecular weight ~261 g/mol. Soluble in water, used as iron source; obtained from citric acid and iron salts.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; irritant to eyes/skin. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but avoid ingestion.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags; store cool per Sigma-Aldrich SDS.

name: Iron Lactate
description: Iron lactate is a green powder with formula C6H10FeO6 and molecular weight 257.99 g/mol. Density ~1.6 g/cm³, soluble in water, used as iron fortifier; obtained from lactic acid.
physicalProperties: Iron lactate is a green powder with formula C6H10FeO6 and molecular weight 257.99 g/mol. Density ~1.6 g/cm³, soluble in water, used as iron fortifier; obtained from lactic acid.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may irritate skin. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but monitor intake.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg drums; store dry per ChemicalBook MSDS.

name: Iron Fumarate
description: Iron fumarate is a red powder with formula FeC4H2O4 and molecular weight 169.90 g/mol. Density 2.0 g/cm³, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; used as iron supplement.
physicalProperties: Iron fumarate is a red powder with formula FeC4H2O4 and molecular weight 169.90 g/mol. Density 2.0 g/cm³, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; used as iron supplement.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; irritates eyes/skin. Stable; use PPE; no major risks but avoid dust.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg fiber bags; store dry per Sigma-Aldrich catalog.

name: Electrolytic Iron Powder
description: Electrolytic iron powder is a high-purity (≥99.5%) form of iron produced by electrodeposition. It has controlled particle size and high reactivity.
physicalProperties: Electrolytic iron powder is a high-purity (≥99.5%) form of iron produced by electrodeposition. It has controlled particle size and high reactivity.
safetyHandling: Inert but dust may cause irritation and is combustible in fine form. Avoid ignition sources and use dust control systems.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined drums or steel containers. Store cool, dry, and sealed against moisture.

name: L-Selenomethionine
description: L-Selenomethionine is a naturally occurring organic selenium compound appearing as a white crystalline powder. It provides high selenium bioavailability and antioxidant support.
physicalProperties: L-Selenomethionine is a naturally occurring organic selenium compound appearing as a white crystalline powder. It provides high selenium bioavailability and antioxidant support.
safetyHandling: Safe within nutritional dosage limits; handle in low humidity and away from heat.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 1–25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Magnesium Aspartate
description: Magnesium aspartate is a chelated compound combining magnesium with aspartic acid. It appears as a white crystalline powder soluble in water and offering high bioavailability.
physicalProperties: Magnesium aspartate is a chelated compound combining magnesium with aspartic acid. It appears as a white crystalline powder soluble in water and offering high bioavailability.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and physiologically well-tolerated. Protect from humidity and heat to maintain stability.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Magnesium Taurinate
description: Magnesium taurinate is a chelate formed between magnesium and taurine, resulting in a highly bioavailable and gentle mineral supplement. It is a white crystalline powder with mild solubility in water.
physicalProperties: Magnesium taurinate is a chelate formed between magnesium and taurine, resulting in a highly bioavailable and gentle mineral supplement. It is a white crystalline powder with mild solubility in water.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; avoid long-term exposure to moisture or high temperatures.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store in a dry, cool, and airtight environment.

name: Magnesium Sulfate Monohydrate
description: Magnesium sulfate monohydrate is a colorless crystalline solid containing one molecule of water of hydration. It provides a concentrated form of magnesium for industrial and agricultural use.
physicalProperties: Magnesium sulfate monohydrate is a colorless crystalline solid containing one molecule of water of hydration. It provides a concentrated form of magnesium for industrial and agricultural use.
safetyHandling: Stable and non-toxic. Avoid exposure to excess humidity.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags or 1,000 kg bulk sacks. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Potassium Citrate
description: Potassium Citrate, tribasic with formula K3C6H5O7, is a deliquescent white granular powder highly soluble in water, providing alkalizing effects and chelation of calcium ions. Derived from citric acid fermentation, it offers a mild saline taste and is used for electrolyte balancing in neutral pH environments.
physicalProperties: Potassium Citrate, tribasic with formula K3C6H5O7, is a deliquescent white granular powder highly soluble in water, providing alkalizing effects and chelation of calcium ions. Derived from citric acid fermentation, it offers a mild saline taste and is used for electrolyte balancing in neutral pH environments.
safetyHandling: Mildly irritating to gastrointestinal tract in excess; low dermal hazard but dust can affect eyes. GRAS classification; monitor potassium levels in formulations and use anti-caking agents to prevent handling clumps.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg woven polypropylene bulks with anti-static liners. House in dehumidified bulk handling areas for flow optimization.

name: Molybdenum Aspartate
description: Molybdenum aspartate is a chelated mineral compound containing molybdenum bound to the amino acid aspartate. It is a fine, white to off-white powder with excellent solubility.
physicalProperties: Molybdenum aspartate is a chelated mineral compound containing molybdenum bound to the amino acid aspartate. It is a fine, white to off-white powder with excellent solubility.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable under standard conditions. Avoid contact with moisture and light.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg aluminum-lined drums or sealed foil bags. Store cool, dry, and tightly sealed.

name: Magnesium Sulfate Anhydrous
description: Magnesium sulfate anhydrous is a white, odorless crystalline powder that readily absorbs moisture. It serves as a concentrated source of magnesium and sulfur for industrial and laboratory use.
physicalProperties: Magnesium sulfate anhydrous is a white, odorless crystalline powder that readily absorbs moisture. It serves as a concentrated source of magnesium and sulfur for industrial and laboratory use.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable under ambient conditions. Avoid contact with moisture to prevent conversion to the heptahydrate form.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg paper bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Iron Pyrophosphate
description: Iron pyrophosphate is a tan powder with formula Fe4(P2O7)3 and molecular weight 745.20 g/mol. Density ~2.5 g/cm³, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; used as iron fortifier, obtained by precipitation.
physicalProperties: Iron pyrophosphate is a tan powder with formula Fe4(P2O7)3 and molecular weight 745.20 g/mol. Density ~2.5 g/cm³, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; used as iron fortifier, obtained by precipitation.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; dust irritates lungs. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but avoid inhalation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg bags; store dry per Sigma-Aldrich SDS.

name: Calcium Gluconate
description: Calcium gluconate is a white crystalline or granular compound formed by neutralizing gluconic acid with calcium carbonate. It is water-soluble and non-irritating.
physicalProperties: Calcium gluconate is a white crystalline or granular compound formed by neutralizing gluconic acid with calcium carbonate. It is water-soluble and non-irritating.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and well-tolerated. Avoid moisture and microbial contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and tightly closed.

name: Calcium Iodate
description: Calcium iodate is a white crystalline compound functioning as a stable iodine source. It is insoluble in alcohol and slightly soluble in water.
physicalProperties: Calcium iodate is a white crystalline compound functioning as a stable iodine source. It is insoluble in alcohol and slightly soluble in water.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; avoid contact with reducing agents to prevent decomposition.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined bags or drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Calcium Sulfate Dihydrate
description: Calcium sulfate dihydrate, commonly known as gypsum, is a white crystalline powder with moderate solubility in water.
physicalProperties: Calcium sulfate dihydrate, commonly known as gypsum, is a white crystalline powder with moderate solubility in water.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable. Avoid moisture exposure that may alter physical properties.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg bags or 1,000 kg bulk sacks. Store dry and sealed.

name: Copper Glycinate
description: Copper glycinate is a blue powder with formula Cu(C2H4NO2)2 and molecular weight 211.66 g/mol. Soluble in water, used as bioavailable copper source; chelated form with glycine, produced by complexation.
physicalProperties: Copper glycinate is a blue powder with formula Cu(C2H4NO2)2 and molecular weight 211.66 g/mol. Soluble in water, used as bioavailable copper source; chelated form with glycine, produced by complexation.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; irritant to eyes upon contact. Avoid overdosage; stable. Use PPE; no major hazards but monitor copper levels in blood.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg multi-wall paper bags with liners; store in cool, dry conditions per Sigma-Aldrich catalog to prevent degradation.

name: Dicalcium Phosphate
description: Dicalcium phosphate is a white powder with formula CaHPO4 and molecular weight 136.06 g/mol. Density 2.31 g/cm³, melting point 1090°C, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; used as calcium source.
physicalProperties: Dicalcium phosphate is a white powder with formula CaHPO4 and molecular weight 136.06 g/mol. Density 2.31 g/cm³, melting point 1090°C, insoluble in water, soluble in acids; used as calcium source.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, GRAS; irritates eyes/skin. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but ventilate dust.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg multi-wall bags; store dry per Sigma-Aldrich FCC.

name: Iron Gluconate
description: Iron gluconate is a yellowish powder with formula C12H22FeO14 and molecular weight 448.35 g/mol. Density 1.8 g/cm³, soluble in water, used as iron supplement; obtained from gluconic acid.
physicalProperties: Iron gluconate is a yellowish powder with formula C12H22FeO14 and molecular weight 448.35 g/mol. Density 1.8 g/cm³, soluble in water, used as iron supplement; obtained from gluconic acid.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause constipation. Irritates eyes; stable. Use PPE; monitor for overload.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg drums; store dry per ChemicalBook MSDS.

name: Calcium Oxide
description: Calcium oxide, also known as quicklime, is a white, caustic, and hygroscopic solid produced by calcination of limestone. It reacts exothermically with water to form calcium hydroxide.
physicalProperties: Calcium oxide, also known as quicklime, is a white, caustic, and hygroscopic solid produced by calcination of limestone. It reacts exothermically with water to form calcium hydroxide.
safetyHandling: Corrosive and generates heat upon hydration; handle with gloves and eye protection. Avoid contact with moisture.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg bags or bulk silos. Store dry, airtight, and away from acids.

name: Potassium Metabisulfite
description: Potassium Metabisulfite, K2S2O5, is a white granular inorganic salt that dissolves in water to liberate sulfur dioxide gas, serving as a potent antioxidant and antimicrobial preservative. It has a sulfurous odor and is stable under dry conditions but decomposes in acidic media for controlled release applications.
physicalProperties: Potassium Metabisulfite, K2S2O5, is a white granular inorganic salt that dissolves in water to liberate sulfur dioxide gas, serving as a potent antioxidant and antimicrobial preservative. It has a sulfurous odor and is stable under dry conditions but decomposes in acidic media for controlled release applications.
safetyHandling: Releases irritating SO2 vapors triggering asthma attacks; corrosive to skin in wet forms. Acid interactions produce toxic gases; equip workspaces with gas detectors and respiratory protection, following strict exposure limits.
tradeRegulatory: Contained in 25 kg multi-ply polyethylene bags with valve closures for easy dispensing. Ventilate storage silos, keeping distant from acidic inventories.

name: Chromium Picolinate
description: Chromium picolinate is a red-violet crystalline coordination compound combining trivalent chromium with picolinic acid. It is highly stable and exhibits strong bioavailability.
physicalProperties: Chromium picolinate is a red-violet crystalline coordination compound combining trivalent chromium with picolinic acid. It is highly stable and exhibits strong bioavailability.
safetyHandling: Generally recognized as safe at low doses; high intake may cause mild gastrointestinal discomfort. Avoid exposure to direct light and moisture to prevent degradation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or aluminum-lined bags. Store sealed, cool, and dry.

name: Calcium Ascorbate
description: Calcium ascorbate is a white crystalline, non-acidic form of vitamin C, combining calcium carbonate with ascorbic acid. It is water-soluble and stable under normal conditions.
physicalProperties: Calcium ascorbate is a white crystalline, non-acidic form of vitamin C, combining calcium carbonate with ascorbic acid. It is water-soluble and stable under normal conditions.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and well-tolerated. Avoid prolonged humidity exposure to preserve potency.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags with PE liners or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: S-Adenosyl-L-Methionine
description: S-Adenosyl-L-methionine (SAMe) is a white hygroscopic powder with formula C15H22N6O5S and molecular weight 398.44 g/mol. Soluble in water, it decomposes in light and heat, pH acidic in solution, and is a key methyl donor in biological processes, produced biosynthetically or synthetically.
physicalProperties: S-Adenosyl-L-methionine (SAMe) is a white hygroscopic powder with formula C15H22N6O5S and molecular weight 398.44 g/mol. Soluble in water, it decomposes in light and heat, pH acidic in solution, and is a key methyl donor in biological processes, produced biosynthetically or synthetically.
safetyHandling: Limited long-term data; may cause gastrointestinal upset or mania in bipolar patients. Avoid antidepressants; stable in dark, inert atmosphere. Use PPE to prevent contact irritation.
tradeRegulatory: In sealed containers or 25kg drums; store frozen under -20°C in dark; rinse for recycling or dispose per guidelines.

name: L-Theanine
description: L-Theanine is an amino acid naturally found in green tea, known for its relaxation and cognitive enhancement properties. It appears as a white crystalline powder with mild sweetness.
physicalProperties: L-Theanine is an amino acid naturally found in green tea, known for its relaxation and cognitive enhancement properties. It appears as a white crystalline powder with mild sweetness.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; sensitive to high humidity.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg foil bags or drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: L-Tyrosine
description: L-Tyrosine is a non-essential aromatic amino acid synthesized from phenylalanine. It is a white crystalline powder with slight sweetness and high stability.
physicalProperties: L-Tyrosine is a non-essential aromatic amino acid synthesized from phenylalanine. It is a white crystalline powder with slight sweetness and high stability.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; store in dry conditions to prevent clumping.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Zinc Aspartate
description: Zinc aspartate is an amino acid chelate formed from zinc and aspartic acid, offering high bioavailability and neutral taste. It appears as a white crystalline powder soluble in water.
physicalProperties: Zinc aspartate is an amino acid chelate formed from zinc and aspartic acid, offering high bioavailability and neutral taste. It appears as a white crystalline powder soluble in water.
safetyHandling: Safe under dietary use conditions; excess intake may cause mild gastrointestinal discomfort. Store dry to prevent oxidation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or PE bags. Store in cool, dry, and sealed conditions.

name: Zinc Lactate
description: Zinc lactate (C₆H₁₀O₆Zn) is a zinc salt of lactic acid, known for its mild taste and high solubility. It provides an effective and bioavailable source of zinc.
physicalProperties: Zinc lactate (C₆H₁₀O₆Zn) is a zinc salt of lactic acid, known for its mild taste and high solubility. It provides an effective and bioavailable source of zinc.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic at regulated levels; excessive intake may lead to nausea. Keep dry and sealed to avoid hygroscopic clumping.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined drums or bags. Store cool, dry, and protected from direct sunlight.

name: Copper Gluconate
description: Copper gluconate is a light blue powder with formula Cu(C6H11O7)2 and molecular weight 453.84 g/mol. It has a density of 1.8 g/cm³, melting point >300°C (decomposes), and is soluble in water (100 g/L at 20°C), slightly soluble in ethanol. Odorless, it is the copper salt of gluconic acid, produced by reacting copper carbonate with gluconic acid, used as a copper source in nutrition.
physicalProperties: Copper gluconate is a light blue powder with formula Cu(C6H11O7)2 and molecular weight 453.84 g/mol. It has a density of 1.8 g/cm³, melting point >300°C (decomposes), and is soluble in water (100 g/L at 20°C), slightly soluble in ethanol. Odorless, it is the copper salt of gluconic acid, produced by reacting copper carbonate with gluconic acid, used as a copper source in nutrition.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but high doses cause gastrointestinal upset or liver damage; irritant to eyes and skin. Avoid strong oxidizers; stable. Use PPE; monitor for copper overload symptoms like nausea.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg multi-layer paper bags with PE liners or fiber drums; store in cool, dry places per Sigma-Aldrich guidelines to prevent moisture absorption.

name: Calcium Lactate
description: Calcium lactate is a white, odorless crystalline powder produced from lactic acid and calcium carbonate. It has good solubility and bioavailability.
physicalProperties: Calcium lactate is a white, odorless crystalline powder produced from lactic acid and calcium carbonate. It has good solubility and bioavailability.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and safe; store dry to avoid caking.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg multiwall bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Sodium Borate
description: Sodium borate, also known as borax, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2B4O7·10H2O (decahydrate) and molecular weight 381.37 g/mol. Density 1.73 g/cm³, melting point 75°C (loses water), highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions, and sourced from natural minerals or synthesis.
physicalProperties: Sodium borate, also known as borax, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2B4O7·10H2O (decahydrate) and molecular weight 381.37 g/mol. Density 1.73 g/cm³, melting point 75°C (loses water), highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions, and sourced from natural minerals or synthesis.
safetyHandling: Irritates eyes and skin; reproductive toxins may impair fertility. Avoid ingestion causing gastrointestinal issues; non-combustible but decomposes releasing boron oxides. Hazard Class 8; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; store at 2-8°C; rinse triply for recycling or disposal in landfills.

name: Mono Calcium Phosphate
description: Mono calcium phosphate (MCP), also known as calcium dihydrogen phosphate, is a white granular or crystalline powder with formula Ca(H2PO4)2·H2O (monohydrate) and molecular weight 252.07 g/mol. It has a density of 2.22 g/cm³, melting point >109°C (decomposes), and is highly soluble in water (18 g/100mL at 30°C), slightly acidic in solution (pH 3-4). Insoluble in ethanol, it is hygroscopic and produced by reaction of calcium carbonate with phosphoric acid; used as a leavening agent and nutrient supplement.
physicalProperties: Mono calcium phosphate (MCP), also known as calcium dihydrogen phosphate, is a white granular or crystalline powder with formula Ca(H2PO4)2·H2O (monohydrate) and molecular weight 252.07 g/mol. It has a density of 2.22 g/cm³, melting point >109°C (decomposes), and is highly soluble in water (18 g/100mL at 30°C), slightly acidic in solution (pH 3-4). Insoluble in ethanol, it is hygroscopic and produced by reaction of calcium carbonate with phosphoric acid; used as a leavening agent and nutrient supplement.
safetyHandling: Nonhazardous per GHS but avoid dust causing eye/respiratory irritation; prolonged exposure may lead to skin dryness. Low toxicity LD50 oral (rat) >5000 mg/kg; non-combustible but decomposes to phosphorus oxides at high heat. Incompatible with strong bases; use PPE, ventilate; no carcinogenic effects.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg multi-wall paper bags with PE liners or 50kg fiber drums to prevent moisture; store tightly closed in cool, dry areas; empty containers retain residue—do not reuse, dispose per local regulations.

name: Copper Citrate
description: Copper citrate is a blue-green powder with formula Cu2C6H4O7 and molecular weight 275.10 g/mol. Density 2.0 g/cm³, decomposes above 200°C, soluble in water (50 g/L), slightly in acids; copper salt of citric acid, used as copper supplement.
physicalProperties: Copper citrate is a blue-green powder with formula Cu2C6H4O7 and molecular weight 275.10 g/mol. Density 2.0 g/cm³, decomposes above 200°C, soluble in water (50 g/L), slightly in acids; copper salt of citric acid, used as copper supplement.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause nausea in excess or allergic reactions. Irritates eyes; avoid dust. Stable; use PPE; no major risks but monitor intake levels.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg fiber drums with double PE bags; store in dry, ventilated areas per ChemicalBook product specs to maintain stability.

name: Ascorbyl Palmitate
description: Ascorbyl palmitate (C₂₂H₃₈O₇) is a lipid-soluble ester of ascorbic acid and palmitic acid. It combines antioxidant activity with fat compatibility, making it ideal for lipid systems.
physicalProperties: Ascorbyl palmitate (C₂₂H₃₈O₇) is a lipid-soluble ester of ascorbic acid and palmitic acid. It combines antioxidant activity with fat compatibility, making it ideal for lipid systems.
safetyHandling: Recognized as safe; handle powders in ventilated areas to prevent dust inhalation. Sensitive to heat and light; store tightly closed.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 10–25 kg fiber drums or aluminum bags. Store cool, dry, and away from direct sunlight.

name: Povidone Iodine
description: Povidone Iodine is a stable iodophor complex of polyvinylpyrrolidone and triiodide ions, forming a reddish-brown amorphous powder or aqueous solution with broad-spectrum microbicidal activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi. It releases free iodine gradually, ensuring prolonged efficacy without significant staining in diluted forms.
physicalProperties: Povidone Iodine is a stable iodophor complex of polyvinylpyrrolidone and triiodide ions, forming a reddish-brown amorphous powder or aqueous solution with broad-spectrum microbicidal activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi. It releases free iodine gradually, ensuring prolonged efficacy without significant staining in diluted forms.
safetyHandling: May induce thyroid imbalances with chronic use; irritants to eyes and open wounds in concentrated states. Iodine hypersensitivity contraindication; dilute prior to application and provide iodine-neutralizing agents like thiosulfate in first-aid setups.
tradeRegulatory: Filled in 10 kg tinted polypropylene jugs with drip-proof spouts. Store in light-protected chemical lockers with temperature logging for stability assurance.

name: Vitamin B8 İnositol
description: Inositol, also known as vitamin B8, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H12O6 and molecular weight 180.16 g/mol. Density 1.75 g/cm³, melting point 225-227°C, highly soluble in water (14 g/100mL), odorless with sweet taste; cyclic polyol, essential for cell signaling.
physicalProperties: Inositol, also known as vitamin B8, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H12O6 and molecular weight 180.16 g/mol. Density 1.75 g/cm³, melting point 225-227°C, highly soluble in water (14 g/100mL), odorless with sweet taste; cyclic polyol, essential for cell signaling.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause gastrointestinal upset in high doses. Irritates eyes; avoid dust. Stable; use PPE, ventilate. No major hazards but monitor for diarrhea.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags or drums with liners; store in cool, dry places per ChemicalBook recommendations.

name: Adenosine Triphosphate
description: Adenosine triphosphate (C₁₀H₁₆N₅O₁₃P₃) is the universal cellular energy carrier. Industrially, ATP is synthesized enzymatically for research and specialized cosmetic formulations.
physicalProperties: Adenosine triphosphate (C₁₀H₁₆N₅O₁₃P₃) is the universal cellular energy carrier. Industrially, ATP is synthesized enzymatically for research and specialized cosmetic formulations.
safetyHandling: Stable in dry form; aqueous solutions degrade rapidly. Non-hazardous at standard concentrations but handled under cleanroom conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied as freeze-dried powder in sealed vials or glass ampoules. Store at −20 °C, protected from moisture and light.

name: Beta Alanine
description: Beta alanine (C₃H₇NO₂) is a non-essential amino acid serving as a precursor to carnosine in muscle tissues. It enhances endurance and reduces lactic acid accumulation during exercise.
physicalProperties: Beta alanine (C₃H₇NO₂) is a non-essential amino acid serving as a precursor to carnosine in muscle tissues. It enhances endurance and reduces lactic acid accumulation during exercise.
safetyHandling: Generally recognized as safe (GRAS). High doses may cause transient tingling (paresthesia). Store in dry, cool conditions to prevent hygroscopic absorption.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg HDPE bags or fiber drums. Store sealed, dry, and protected from light.

name: L-Cysteine Hydrochloride
description: L-Cysteine hydrochloride is a white crystalline amino acid derivative with high solubility and reducing properties.
physicalProperties: L-Cysteine hydrochloride is a white crystalline amino acid derivative with high solubility and reducing properties.
safetyHandling: Stable and non-toxic; slightly hygroscopic—keep tightly sealed.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg PE-lined fiber drums or paper bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: L-Carnitine Fumarate
description: L-Carnitine fumarate is a salt combining L-carnitine and fumaric acid, offering improved stability and bioavailability. It appears as a white crystalline powder.
physicalProperties: L-Carnitine fumarate is a salt combining L-carnitine and fumaric acid, offering improved stability and bioavailability. It appears as a white crystalline powder.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and safe; store away from humidity to prevent clumping.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Dimethyl Glycine
description: Dimethyl glycine is a white powder with formula C4H9NO2 and molecular weight 103.12 g/mol. Density 1.07 g/cm³, melting point 178°C, soluble in water, used as biomarker; N-methylated glycine derivative.
physicalProperties: Dimethyl glycine is a white powder with formula C4H9NO2 and molecular weight 103.12 g/mol. Density 1.07 g/cm³, melting point 178°C, soluble in water, used as biomarker; N-methylated glycine derivative.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; irritant to eyes/skin. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but avoid inhalation.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25g bottles; store dry per Sigma-Aldrich catalog.

name: Sodium Hexanoate
description: Sodium hexanoate, also known as sodium caproate, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H11NaO2 and molecular weight 138.14 g/mol. It is soluble in water and ethanol, with a faint fatty odor, pH alkaline in solution, and derived from hexanoic acid neutralization. Stable under normal conditions but hygroscopic.
physicalProperties: Sodium hexanoate, also known as sodium caproate, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H11NaO2 and molecular weight 138.14 g/mol. It is soluble in water and ethanol, with a faint fatty odor, pH alkaline in solution, and derived from hexanoic acid neutralization. Stable under normal conditions but hygroscopic.
safetyHandling: Irritant to skin and eyes; may cause respiratory issues if inhaled. Combustible; avoid strong oxidizers to prevent reactions. Low toxicity but handled with PPE; stable but decomposes on heating emitting acrid smoke.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg drums or bags with PE liners; store in cool, dry places; rinse triply for recycling or puncture for landfill disposal.

name: Tolytriazole
description: Tolyltriazole is a chemical compound with the molecular formula C9H9N3. It is a white to off-white powder. Tan to light brown granules or beige pellets with a characteristic odor.
physicalProperties: Tolyltriazole is a chemical compound with the molecular formula C9H9N3. It is a white to off-white powder. Tan to light brown granules or beige pellets with a characteristic odor.
safetyHandling: May cause eye and pulmonary irritation; symptoms of exposure to this compound may include eye and pulmonary irritation; avoid breathing mist, gas or vapours; avoid contacting with skin and eye; use personal protective equipment; wear chemical impermeable gloves.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg fiber drums or 25 gallon all fiber containers; keep container tightly closed; store in cool, dry places as per Ashland guidelines.

name: Hemp Fiber
description: Hemp fiber is a natural bast fiber extracted from Cannabis sativa stems. It exhibits high tensile strength, breathability, and resistance to UV degradation.
physicalProperties: Hemp fiber is a natural bast fiber extracted from Cannabis sativa stems. It exhibits high tensile strength, breathability, and resistance to UV degradation.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; store dry to prevent mold growth.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in bales or 25 kg bundles. Store cool, dry, and ventilated.

name: Agave Inulin
description: Agave inulin is a fructan polysaccharide extracted from the blue agave plant. It functions as a soluble dietary fiber and prebiotic ingredient with mild sweetness and fat-replacement capability.
physicalProperties: Agave inulin is a fructan polysaccharide extracted from the blue agave plant. It functions as a soluble dietary fiber and prebiotic ingredient with mild sweetness and fat-replacement capability.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and safe for consumption. Excessive intake may cause mild gastrointestinal discomfort. Keep dry to avoid microbial contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 20–25 kg paper bags or foil-lined drums. Store in cool, dry conditions, sealed against humidity.

name: Citrus Fiber
description: Citrus fiber is one example of a clean label ingredient that can provide multiple functional properties such as mouthfeel, emulsification, and/or gelling. The patented physical process does not use any chemical modifications. Citri-Fi can be labeled as citrus fiber, dried citrus pulp or citrus flour. Citrus wastes as sustainable materials for active and intelligent food packaging.
physicalProperties: Citrus fiber is one example of a clean label ingredient that can provide multiple functional properties such as mouthfeel, emulsification, and/or gelling. The patented physical process does not use any chemical modifications. Citri-Fi can be labeled as citrus fiber, dried citrus pulp or citrus flour. Citrus wastes as sustainable materials for active and intelligent food packaging.
safetyHandling: The usual precautionary measure should be adhered; wear safety glasses with side shields (or goggles); this substance is registered under the REACH Regulation and is manufactured in and / or imported to the European Economic Area.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 20kg or 25kg multi-wall paper bags with moisture barrier; store in dry warehouses as per Cargill specifications for bulk shipment.

name: Cocoa Fiber
description: Cocoa fiber is a byproduct of cocoa processing, containing insoluble dietary fibers, polyphenols, and trace cocoa aroma.
physicalProperties: Cocoa fiber is a byproduct of cocoa processing, containing insoluble dietary fibers, polyphenols, and trace cocoa aroma.
safetyHandling: Food-grade and non-toxic. Avoid moisture and contamination during storage.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 20–25 kg paper bags with PE liners. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Apple Fiber
description: Apple fiber is a natural dietary fiber obtained from dried and milled apple pomace. It is rich in insoluble cellulose and hemicellulose, providing both nutritional and texturizing benefits.
physicalProperties: Apple fiber is a natural dietary fiber obtained from dried and milled apple pomace. It is rich in insoluble cellulose and hemicellulose, providing both nutritional and texturizing benefits.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and food-grade. Avoid moisture absorption and microbial contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 20–25 kg kraft paper bags with PE liners. Store sealed, cool, and dry away from humidity.

name: Psyllium Husk Powder
description: Psyllium Husk Powder originates from the outer coating of Plantago ovata seeds, a fine, light beige powder high in soluble dietary fiber mucilage that absorbs water to form viscous gels. It contains approximately 85% fiber content, promoting bulk in formulations while being gluten-free and neutral in taste for versatile integration.
physicalProperties: Psyllium Husk Powder originates from the outer coating of Plantago ovata seeds, a fine, light beige powder high in soluble dietary fiber mucilage that absorbs water to form viscous gels. It contains approximately 85% fiber content, promoting bulk in formulations while being gluten-free and neutral in taste for versatile integration.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic but may cause esophageal obstruction if not hydrated properly; allergenic potential in dust form for respiratory systems. Contraindicated for bowel obstruction patients; ensure adequate hydration instructions and use filtered air systems during powder sifting.
tradeRegulatory: Bagged in 25 kg kraft paper sacks with internal vapor barriers for freshness. Stack in food-grade warehouses with humidity controls to prevent mold growth.

name: Indian Bay Leaf Extract
description: Indian bay leaf extract is a concentrated form of Cinnamomum tamala containing volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins. It offers antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties.
physicalProperties: Indian bay leaf extract is a concentrated form of Cinnamomum tamala containing volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins. It offers antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties.
safetyHandling: Safe and non-toxic at standard dosages; sensitive to oxidation. Protect from air and moisture.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 10–25 kg aluminum-lined drums. Store sealed, cool, and away from light.

name: Potassium Tetraoxalate
description: Potassium Tetraoxalate, chemically KHC2O4·K2C2O4·2H2O, is a white crystalline dihydrate salt with strong acidic characteristics, highly soluble in water to form buffering solutions around pH 2-3. It acts as a chelating agent for metal ions, derived from oxalic acid neutralization, and is valued for its role in precise analytical calibrations.
physicalProperties: Potassium Tetraoxalate, chemically KHC2O4·K2C2O4·2H2O, is a white crystalline dihydrate salt with strong acidic characteristics, highly soluble in water to form buffering solutions around pH 2-3. It acts as a chelating agent for metal ions, derived from oxalic acid neutralization, and is valued for its role in precise analytical calibrations.
safetyHandling: Corrosive nature poses risks of severe eye and skin burns upon contact; ingestion can lead to systemic oxalate poisoning affecting kidneys. Incompatible with strong bases generating heat; mandate acid-resistant PPE and emergency neutralization kits in handling zones per safety data sheets.
tradeRegulatory: Housed in 25 kg corrosion-resistant polypropylene containers with child-proof closures. Place in acid storage cabinets with spill trays, ensuring separation from alkaline materials.

name: Potassium Hydrogen Oxalate
description: Potassium Hydrogen Oxalate, formula KH(C2O4), is a colorless monoclinic crystalline compound with acidic properties, soluble in water and ethanol, functioning as a reducing agent in redox reactions. It decomposes at high temperatures releasing carbon dioxide, commonly synthesized from potassium hydroxide and oxalic acid for industrial purity.
physicalProperties: Potassium Hydrogen Oxalate, formula KH(C2O4), is a colorless monoclinic crystalline compound with acidic properties, soluble in water and ethanol, functioning as a reducing agent in redox reactions. It decomposes at high temperatures releasing carbon dioxide, commonly synthesized from potassium hydroxide and oxalic acid for industrial purity.
safetyHandling: Toxic if swallowed, causing hyperoxaluria and potential renal failure; strong irritant to mucous membranes. Avoid contact with oxidizers to prevent violent decomposition; implement fume extraction and full-face shields for safe manipulation in confined spaces.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 20 kg vented fiberglass-reinforced plastic bins with moisture indicators. Secure in ventilated chemical depots, elevated to avoid floor-level contamination risks.

name: Disodium Inosinate
description: Disodium inosinate is a white powder with formula C10H11N4Na2O8P and molecular weight 388.15 g/mol. Density ~1.5 g/cm³, melting point >300°C, soluble in water, odorless; obtained by fermentation or synthesis, used as flavor enhancer.
physicalProperties: Disodium inosinate is a white powder with formula C10H11N4Na2O8P and molecular weight 388.15 g/mol. Density ~1.5 g/cm³, melting point >300°C, soluble in water, odorless; obtained by fermentation or synthesis, used as flavor enhancer.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, GRAS; may cause gout in high doses. Irritates eyes; stable. Use PPE; no major hazards but monitor purine intake.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg cartons with inner bags; store dry per Sigma-Aldrich FCC specs.

name: Disodium Guanylate
description: Disodium guanylate is a white powder with formula C10H12N5Na2O8P and molecular weight 407.14 g/mol. Density ~1.5 g/cm³, melting point >300°C, soluble in water, odorless; obtained by fermentation, used as flavor enhancer.
physicalProperties: Disodium guanylate is a white powder with formula C10H12N5Na2O8P and molecular weight 407.14 g/mol. Density ~1.5 g/cm³, melting point >300°C, soluble in water, odorless; obtained by fermentation, used as flavor enhancer.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, GRAS; may exacerbate gout. Irritates eyes/skin; stable. Use PPE; no major risks but limit in purine-sensitive.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg fiber drums; store cool and dry per Sigma-Aldrich guidelines.

name: I+G Ribonucleotide
description: I+G is a synergistic blend of disodium inosinate (IMP) and disodium guanylate (GMP), enhancing umami flavor intensity. It appears as a white crystalline powder highly soluble in water.
physicalProperties: I+G is a synergistic blend of disodium inosinate (IMP) and disodium guanylate (GMP), enhancing umami flavor intensity. It appears as a white crystalline powder highly soluble in water.
safetyHandling: Food-grade and non-toxic. Avoid humidity to prevent caking.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags. Store sealed, cool, and dry.

name: Magnesium Taurate
description: Magnesium taurate is a white crystalline compound formed by the chelation of magnesium with taurine. It combines cardiovascular and neurological support benefits.
physicalProperties: Magnesium taurate is a white crystalline compound formed by the chelation of magnesium with taurine. It combines cardiovascular and neurological support benefits.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and safe. Avoid high humidity to maintain powder integrity.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Disodium Sebacate
description: Disodium sebacate is a white powder with formula C10H16Na2O4 and molecular weight 270.21 g/mol. Density ~1.2 g/cm³, melting point >300°C, soluble in water, used as corrosion inhibitor; obtained from castor oil.
physicalProperties: Disodium sebacate is a white powder with formula C10H16Na2O4 and molecular weight 270.21 g/mol. Density ~1.2 g/cm³, melting point >300°C, soluble in water, used as corrosion inhibitor; obtained from castor oil.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; irritant to eyes. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but rinse after contact.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg bags; store dry per Sigma-Aldrich SDS.

name: Yucca Schidigera Extract
description: Yucca schidigera extract is a brown powder or liquid from a yucca plant, containing saponins (10-60%), resveratrol, and yuccaols. Soluble in water, with soapy taste; density 1.1 g/cm³ for liquid, pH 4-6; produced by extraction, used for odor control and anti-inflammatory.
