name: Hydroxyphosphonoacetic Acid
description: Hydroxyphosphonoacetic acid (C₂H₅O₆P) is an organophosphorus compound known for its excellent scale inhibition and chelating properties. It is a colorless to pale-yellow liquid soluble in water and stable under various pH conditions.
physicalProperties: Hydroxyphosphonoacetic acid (C₂H₅O₆P) is an organophosphorus compound known for its excellent scale inhibition and chelating properties. It is a colorless to pale-yellow liquid soluble in water and stable under various pH conditions.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but corrosive in concentrated form. Avoid contact with skin and eyes and ensure local exhaust ventilation during handling.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 30 kg plastic drums or 250 kg IBC tanks. Store sealed in cool, dry, and well-ventilated areas away from strong oxidizers and alkalis.

name: Magnesium Malate
description: Magnesium malate is a white crystalline compound combining malic acid with magnesium, offering high absorption and neutral taste.
physicalProperties: Magnesium malate is a white crystalline compound combining malic acid with magnesium, offering high absorption and neutral taste.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; store away from humidity and heat sources.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg aluminum foil bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Stearic Acid
description: Stearic acid, also known as octadecanoic acid, is a white waxy solid with formula C18H36O2 and molecular weight 284.48 g/mol. It has a density of 0.94 g/cm³, melting point 69-70°C, and is insoluble in water but soluble in organics like ethanol. Derived from animal or vegetable fats, it is non-hygroscopic, odorless or faintly fatty, and stable under ambient conditions.
physicalProperties: Stearic acid, also known as octadecanoic acid, is a white waxy solid with formula C18H36O2 and molecular weight 284.48 g/mol. It has a density of 0.94 g/cm³, melting point 69-70°C, and is insoluble in water but soluble in organics like ethanol. Derived from animal or vegetable fats, it is non-hygroscopic, odorless or faintly fatty, and stable under ambient conditions.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but dust irritates eyes and throat; causes skin and eye irritation upon contact. Combustible; avoid strong oxidizers which may cause fires. Use PPE and ventilation; Hazard Class not specified.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg bags or drums with inner liners; rinse containers triply for recycling or puncture for sanitary landfill disposal to ensure compliance.

name: Strontium Carbonate
description: Strontium carbonate is a milky white free-flowing powder with formula SrCO3 and molecular weight 147.63 g/mol. It has a density of 3.7 g/cm³, melting point 1494°C (decomposes), and is sparingly soluble in water (0.011 g/L) but soluble in acids. Non-hygroscopic, odorless, and stable under ambient conditions; produced from celestite ore.
physicalProperties: Strontium carbonate is a milky white free-flowing powder with formula SrCO3 and molecular weight 147.63 g/mol. It has a density of 3.7 g/cm³, melting point 1494°C (decomposes), and is sparingly soluble in water (0.011 g/L) but soluble in acids. Non-hygroscopic, odorless, and stable under ambient conditions; produced from celestite ore.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but dust irritates eyes and respiratory tract; ingestion may cause gastrointestinal issues. Nonflammable; avoid strong acids which release CO2. Use PPE and ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or fiber drums; store in cool, dry areas; offer rinsed containers for recycling or incinerate with flue gas scrubbing.

name: Hydroquinone
description: Hydroquinone is an industrial grade aromatic compound supplied as white crystalline solids with strong reducing properties. It is produced through controlled chemical synthesis to ensure high purity and stable performance in demanding manufacturing environments. The material dissolves readily in water and common solvents and is valued for its reliability in formulations that require precise oxidation control or polymer stabilisation.
physicalProperties: Hydroquinone is an industrial grade aromatic compound supplied as white crystalline solids with strong reducing properties. It is produced through controlled chemical synthesis to ensure high purity and stable performance in demanding manufacturing environments. The material dissolves readily in water and common solvents and is valued for its reliability in formulations that require precise oxidation control or polymer stabilisation.
safetyHandling: Irritant substance that may affect skin, eyes and respiratory system. Avoid prolonged contact. Handle with PPE and good ventilation. Sensitive to light and air which can cause gradual degradation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kilogram fibre drums or HDPE containers with inner liners. Store in cool, dry and dark conditions away from oxidants and sources of heat in accordance with SDS guidance.

name: Magnesium Sulfate
description: Magnesium sulfate, commonly known as Epsom salt, is a colorless crystalline compound with high solubility in water. It provides essential magnesium ions for biochemical and industrial processes.
physicalProperties: Magnesium sulfate, commonly known as Epsom salt, is a colorless crystalline compound with high solubility in water. It provides essential magnesium ions for biochemical and industrial processes.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic at recommended doses; excessive intake may cause gastrointestinal discomfort. Avoid moisture contamination.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg woven bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store dry, cool, and sealed.

name: Sucralose
description: Sucralose is a white crystalline powder with formula C12H19Cl3O8 and molecular weight 397.64 g/mol. It has a density of 1.69 g/cm³, melting point 125°C, highly soluble in water (up to 280 g/L), and 600 times sweeter than sucrose. Odorless, stable, and produced by chlorinating sucrose.
physicalProperties: Sucralose is a white crystalline powder with formula C12H19Cl3O8 and molecular weight 397.64 g/mol. It has a density of 1.69 g/cm³, melting point 125°C, highly soluble in water (up to 280 g/L), and 600 times sweeter than sucrose. Odorless, stable, and produced by chlorinating sucrose.
safetyHandling: Generally safe and nontoxic; approved by FDA after 110 studies, but may cause irritation if mishandled. Avoid strong oxidizers; low environmental impact.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg cartons or drums with liners; rinse triply for recycling or puncture for landfill per guidelines.

name: Hydrochloric Acid
description: Hydrochloric acid is a colorless fuming liquid with formula HCl and molecular weight 36.46 g/mol. Density 1.18 g/cm³ (37% solution), boiling point 84°C (20%), melting point -114°C, vapor pressure 190 mmHg at 25°C; highly soluble in water, pungent odor, produced by burning hydrogen in chlorine or as byproduct; strong mineral acid.
physicalProperties: Hydrochloric acid is a colorless fuming liquid with formula HCl and molecular weight 36.46 g/mol. Density 1.18 g/cm³ (37% solution), boiling point 84°C (20%), melting point -114°C, vapor pressure 190 mmHg at 25°C; highly soluble in water, pungent odor, produced by burning hydrogen in chlorine or as byproduct; strong mineral acid.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive, causes severe burns to skin/eyes/lungs; toxic vapors. Reacts with metals releasing hydrogen; non-flammable. Hazard Class 8; use PPE, ventilate; avoid bases.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 200L plastic drums or glass bottles; store in ventilated areas per Sigma-Aldrich specs.

name: Succinic Acid
description: Succinic acid, also known as butanedioic acid, is a white crystalline powder with formula C4H6O4 and molecular weight 118.09 g/mol. It has a density of 1.56 g/cm³, melting point 185-187°C, and is soluble in water (83 g/L at 25°C). Odorless with acidic taste, hygroscopic, and produced via fermentation or synthesis.
physicalProperties: Succinic acid, also known as butanedioic acid, is a white crystalline powder with formula C4H6O4 and molecular weight 118.09 g/mol. It has a density of 1.56 g/cm³, melting point 185-187°C, and is soluble in water (83 g/L at 25°C). Odorless with acidic taste, hygroscopic, and produced via fermentation or synthesis.
safetyHandling: GRAS status; corrosive to eyes and skin, causes burns. Avoid strong oxidizers; use PPE and ventilation; low aquatic toxicity.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with PE liners; store in cool, dry places; rinse for recycling or disposal punctured in landfills.

name: Magnesium Stearate
description: Magnesium stearate is a white, fine, hydrophobic powder composed of magnesium salts of stearic acid. It exhibits lubricating and anti-adherent properties.
physicalProperties: Magnesium stearate is a white, fine, hydrophobic powder composed of magnesium salts of stearic acid. It exhibits lubricating and anti-adherent properties.
safetyHandling: Inert and non-toxic; excessive inhalation may cause minor respiratory irritation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 20–25 kg paper bags or fiber drums. Store in a cool, dry area.

name: Sodium Thiocyanate
description: Sodium thiocyanate is a white crystalline powder with formula NaSCN and molecular weight 81.07 g/mol. Density 1.74 g/cm³, melting point 287°C; highly soluble in water (up to 1390 g/L at 21°C), deliquescent. Produced by reacting sodium cyanide with sulfur.
physicalProperties: Sodium thiocyanate is a white crystalline powder with formula NaSCN and molecular weight 81.07 g/mol. Density 1.74 g/cm³, melting point 287°C; highly soluble in water (up to 1390 g/L at 21°C), deliquescent. Produced by reacting sodium cyanide with sulfur.
safetyHandling: Harmful if swallowed, skin contact, inhaled; causes eye damage. Avoid acids; Hazard Class 6.1; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; rinse for recycling or puncture for disposal per guidelines.

name: Sodium Thiosulfate
description: Sodium thiosulfate, also known as hypo, is a white crystalline solid with formula Na2S2O3 and molecular weight 158.11 g/mol (anhydrous). Density 1.67 g/cm³, melting point 48°C (pentahydrate); highly soluble in water, hygroscopic. Produced by boiling sodium sulfite with sulfur.
physicalProperties: Sodium thiosulfate, also known as hypo, is a white crystalline solid with formula Na2S2O3 and molecular weight 158.11 g/mol (anhydrous). Density 1.67 g/cm³, melting point 48°C (pentahydrate); highly soluble in water, hygroscopic. Produced by boiling sodium sulfite with sulfur.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but irritates eyes, skin; releases toxic gases in fire. Avoid oxidizers; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; rinse triply for recycling or reconditioning.

name: Manganese Sulfate
description: Manganese sulfate is a pale pink crystalline salt soluble in water and slightly acidic in solution. It serves as a primary source of manganese for industrial and agricultural applications.
physicalProperties: Manganese sulfate is a pale pink crystalline salt soluble in water and slightly acidic in solution. It serves as a primary source of manganese for industrial and agricultural applications.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but avoid inhalation of dust or prolonged exposure to prevent metal accumulation. Handle with gloves and respiratory protection.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg woven bags or 1,000 kg bulk sacks. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Sodium Tripolyphosphate
description: Sodium tripolyphosphate (STPP) is a white powder with formula Na5P3O10 and molecular weight 367.86 g/mol. Density 2.52 g/cm³, decomposes above 100°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions. Hygroscopic; produced by heating sodium phosphate mixtures.
physicalProperties: Sodium tripolyphosphate (STPP) is a white powder with formula Na5P3O10 and molecular weight 367.86 g/mol. Density 2.52 g/cm³, decomposes above 100°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions. Hygroscopic; produced by heating sodium phosphate mixtures.
safetyHandling: Irritates skin, eyes; harmful if swallowed or inhaled. Avoid dust; use PPE; low environmental risk.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags with liners; offer rinsed containers for recycling or puncture for landfill.

name: Sorbic Acid
description: Sorbic acid is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H8O2 and molecular weight 112.13 g/mol. Density 1.20 g/cm³, melting point 134°C; slightly soluble in water, soluble in organics. Has a faint odor; produced by condensing ketene with crotonaldehyde.
physicalProperties: Sorbic acid is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H8O2 and molecular weight 112.13 g/mol. Density 1.20 g/cm³, melting point 134°C; slightly soluble in water, soluble in organics. Has a faint odor; produced by condensing ketene with crotonaldehyde.
safetyHandling: Irritates skin, eyes; harmful to aquatic life. Avoid light exposure, oxidizers; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg cartons or drums; rinse for recycling or disposal punctured in landfills.

name: Maltodextrin
description: Maltodextrin is a white, free-flowing carbohydrate powder derived from starch hydrolysis. It has neutral flavor and good solubility in water.
physicalProperties: Maltodextrin is a white, free-flowing carbohydrate powder derived from starch hydrolysis. It has neutral flavor and good solubility in water.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and easily digestible; store away from moisture to prevent caking.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg multilayer paper bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Sorbitol
description: Sorbitol, also known as D-glucitol, is a white crystalline powder or colorless syrup with the formula C6H14O6 and molecular weight 182.17 g/mol. It is a polyhydric alcohol with about half the sweetness of sucrose, highly soluble in water (up to 2350 g/L at 25°C), and insoluble in most organic solvents. Hygroscopic with a melting point of 95°C, it occurs naturally in fruits and is produced synthetically from glucose via hydrogenation.
physicalProperties: Sorbitol, also known as D-glucitol, is a white crystalline powder or colorless syrup with the formula C6H14O6 and molecular weight 182.17 g/mol. It is a polyhydric alcohol with about half the sweetness of sucrose, highly soluble in water (up to 2350 g/L at 25°C), and insoluble in most organic solvents. Hygroscopic with a melting point of 95°C, it occurs naturally in fruits and is produced synthetically from glucose via hydrogenation.
safetyHandling: Generally regarded as safe (GRAS) with low toxicity; may cause gastrointestinal upset in high doses. Nonflammable and noncorrosive; avoid dust inhalation which irritates nose and throat. Use PPE; stable but incompatible with strong oxidizers.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg polypropylene bags or fiber drums with PE liners; store tightly closed in dry, cool, ventilated areas; rinse containers for recycling or dispose in sanitary landfills per regulations.

name: Malic Acid
description: Malic acid is a white crystalline dicarboxylic acid naturally found in fruits. It imparts tartness and enhances flavor stability.
physicalProperties: Malic acid is a white crystalline dicarboxylic acid naturally found in fruits. It imparts tartness and enhances flavor stability.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may irritate eyes and skin. Handle with gloves and avoid dust formation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined bags or drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Hydrogenated Castor Oil
description: Hydrogenated castor oil is a white to pale yellow waxy solid with formula C57H110O9 (approximate) and molecular weight around 932 g/mol. Density 0.98 g/cm³, melting point 85-88°C, insoluble in water, soluble in hot organics; hard butter-like, produced by hydrogenation of castor oil, used as lubricant and thickener.
physicalProperties: Hydrogenated castor oil is a white to pale yellow waxy solid with formula C57H110O9 (approximate) and molecular weight around 932 g/mol. Density 0.98 g/cm³, melting point 85-88°C, insoluble in water, soluble in hot organics; hard butter-like, produced by hydrogenation of castor oil, used as lubricant and thickener.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity, GRAS; may cause mild irritation to eyes and skin. Non-flammable; avoid strong oxidizers. Stable; use PPE; no major hazards but ventilate dusty areas.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags or flakes in supersacks; store in cool, dry places per Sigma-Aldrich catalog.

name: Heptanoic Acid
description: Heptanoic acid, also known as enanthic acid or oenanthic acid, is a colorless oily liquid with the formula C7H14O2 and molecular weight 130.18 g/mol. It has a density of 0.918 g/cm³, boiling point 223°C, melting point -7.5°C, flash point 118°C, and refractive index 1.421-1.422. Slightly soluble in water (0.24 g/100mL at 15°C), miscible with ethanol and ether, it has a rancid odor and is produced by oxidation of heptanal or castor oil hydrolysis.
physicalProperties: Heptanoic acid, also known as enanthic acid or oenanthic acid, is a colorless oily liquid with the formula C7H14O2 and molecular weight 130.18 g/mol. It has a density of 0.918 g/cm³, boiling point 223°C, melting point -7.5°C, flash point 118°C, and refractive index 1.421-1.422. Slightly soluble in water (0.24 g/100mL at 15°C), miscible with ethanol and ether, it has a rancid odor and is produced by oxidation of heptanal or castor oil hydrolysis.
safetyHandling: Irritates skin, eyes, and respiratory tract; harmful if swallowed or inhaled causing burns. Combustible; avoid strong oxidizers to prevent reactions. Stable under normal conditions but decomposes at high temperatures; use PPE, ventilate areas; may cause environmental harm if released.
tradeRegulatory: Typically supplied in 200kg iron drums or plastic barrels; store in cool, dry, ventilated places away from incompatibles per ChemicalBook guidelines.

name: Hydrogen Bromide
description: Hydrogen bromide is a colorless gas or fuming liquid (aqueous solution) with the formula HBr and molecular weight 80.91 g/mol. It has a density of 1.49 g/cm³ (liquid), boiling point -66.8°C, melting point -86.9°C, and is highly soluble in water (forming hydrobromic acid, up to 68% at 25°C). It has a sharp, irritating odor and is produced by reaction of bromine with hydrogen or hydrolysis of phosphorus tribromide; strong acid used in synthesis.
physicalProperties: Hydrogen bromide is a colorless gas or fuming liquid (aqueous solution) with the formula HBr and molecular weight 80.91 g/mol. It has a density of 1.49 g/cm³ (liquid), boiling point -66.8°C, melting point -86.9°C, and is highly soluble in water (forming hydrobromic acid, up to 68% at 25°C). It has a sharp, irritating odor and is produced by reaction of bromine with hydrogen or hydrolysis of phosphorus tribromide; strong acid used in synthesis.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive, causes severe burns to skin, eyes, and lungs; toxic by inhalation leading to pulmonary edema. Reacts exothermically with bases and metals; non-flammable but supports combustion; use PPE, ventilate; Hazard Class 8 (corrosive) and 2.3 (toxic gas).
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in steel cylinders or glass bottles for solutions; store in cool, ventilated areas away from bases per Sigma-Aldrich specifications.

name: Hydrogen Peroxide
description: Hydrogen peroxide is a colorless liquid with formula H2O2 and molecular weight 34.01 g/mol. It has a density of 1.11 g/cm³ (30% solution), boiling point 150.2°C (decomposes), melting point -0.43°C, and is miscible with water. It has a bitter taste, acts as an oxidizer, and is produced by anthraquinone process; available in concentrations like 3-50%, unstable and decomposes to water and oxygen.
physicalProperties: Hydrogen peroxide is a colorless liquid with formula H2O2 and molecular weight 34.01 g/mol. It has a density of 1.11 g/cm³ (30% solution), boiling point 150.2°C (decomposes), melting point -0.43°C, and is miscible with water. It has a bitter taste, acts as an oxidizer, and is produced by anthraquinone process; available in concentrations like 3-50%, unstable and decomposes to water and oxygen.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer, causes burns to skin and eyes; inhalation irritates lungs. Decomposes violently with catalysts or heat releasing oxygen; avoid organics and metals. Stable with stabilizers; Hazard Class 5.1 (oxidizer) and 8 (corrosive); use PPE, ventilate.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 30-65kg plastic drums or IBC totes; store in cool, ventilated places away from sunlight per Sigma-Aldrich recommendations.

name: Sodium Sulfhydrate
description: Sodium sulfhydrate, also known as sodium hydrosulfide, is a colorless to yellow flake or liquid with formula NaHS and molecular weight 56.06 g/mol. Density 1.79 g/cm³, melting point 350°C (decomposes); soluble in water releasing H2S, pungent odor. Produced by reacting sodium sulfide with hydrogen sulfide.
physicalProperties: Sodium sulfhydrate, also known as sodium hydrosulfide, is a colorless to yellow flake or liquid with formula NaHS and molecular weight 56.06 g/mol. Density 1.79 g/cm³, melting point 350°C (decomposes); soluble in water releasing H2S, pungent odor. Produced by reacting sodium sulfide with hydrogen sulfide.
safetyHandling: Corrosive, toxic if swallowed or inhaled; releases H2S gas with acids. Flammable; Hazard Class 8/4.2; use PPE, ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg iron drums or bags with liners; rinse triply for recycling or puncture for disposal.

name: Sodium Sulfite
description: Sodium sulfite is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2SO3 and molecular weight 126.04 g/mol. Density 2.63 g/cm³, decomposes above 100°C; highly soluble in water (up to 220 g/L at 20°C), slightly sulfurous odor. Acts as a reducing agent; produced by reacting sulfur dioxide with sodium carbonate.
physicalProperties: Sodium sulfite is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2SO3 and molecular weight 126.04 g/mol. Density 2.63 g/cm³, decomposes above 100°C; highly soluble in water (up to 220 g/L at 20°C), slightly sulfurous odor. Acts as a reducing agent; produced by reacting sulfur dioxide with sodium carbonate.
safetyHandling: Moderately toxic; irritates skin, eyes, respiratory tract; releases SO2 with acids. Avoid oxidizers; use PPE, ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; moisten spills before sweeping; rinse containers for recycling.

name: Sodium Sulfide
otherNames: Sodium Monosulfide, Sodium Salt of Sulfur
description: Sodium sulfide is a yellow to red flake or powder with formula Na2S and molecular weight 78.04 g/mol. Density 1.86 g/cm³, melting point 950°C; soluble in water releasing H2S, strong rotten egg odor. Hygroscopic; produced by reducing sodium sulfate with carbon.
physicalProperties: Sodium sulfide is a yellow to red flake or powder with formula Na2S and molecular weight 78.04 g/mol. Density 1.86 g/cm³, melting point 950°C; soluble in water releasing H2S, strong rotten egg odor. Hygroscopic; produced by reducing sodium sulfate with carbon.
safetyHandling: Corrosive to eyes, skin, respiratory tract; toxic on ingestion. Flammable; releases H2S with acids; Hazard Class 8.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with liners; store airtight; dispose rinsed containers in landfills.

name: Gluten
description: Gluten is a natural protein complex composed of gliadin and glutenin fractions found in wheat. It forms viscoelastic networks when hydrated, giving dough its elasticity and strength.
physicalProperties: Gluten is a natural protein complex composed of gliadin and glutenin fractions found in wheat. It forms viscoelastic networks when hydrated, giving dough its elasticity and strength.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic for general handling, though unsuitable for individuals with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity. Avoid prolonged exposure to humidity to prevent spoilage.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg kraft paper bags with inner PE liners. Store in dry, cool warehouses away from moisture and pests.

name: Guar Gum
description: Guar gum is a polysaccharide obtained from the endosperm of Cyamopsis tetragonoloba seeds. It is a free-flowing powder with high viscosity even at low concentrations.
physicalProperties: Guar gum is a polysaccharide obtained from the endosperm of Cyamopsis tetragonoloba seeds. It is a free-flowing powder with high viscosity even at low concentrations.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and biodegradable. Inhalation of fine dust may cause mechanical irritation; handle under ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg multiwall paper bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed to maintain viscosity and prevent microbial contamination.

name: Gum Arabic
description: Gum Arabic is a natural exudate from Acacia senegal or Acacia seyal trees. It is a complex polysaccharide with high solubility and emulsifying properties.
physicalProperties: Gum Arabic is a natural exudate from Acacia senegal or Acacia seyal trees. It is a complex polysaccharide with high solubility and emulsifying properties.
safetyHandling: Recognized as safe for food use; sensitive to humidity and microbial contamination. Avoid prolonged exposure to moisture.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags or drums. Store sealed in dry, cool conditions away from sunlight.

name: Silver Nitrate
description: Silver nitrate is a colorless crystalline solid and a powerful oxidizing agent. It exhibits high solubility in water and strong light sensitivity, darkening upon exposure.
physicalProperties: Silver nitrate is a colorless crystalline solid and a powerful oxidizing agent. It exhibits high solubility in water and strong light sensitivity, darkening upon exposure.
safetyHandling: Corrosive and oxidizing; causes burns and stains on contact. Store away from light, organics, and reducing agents.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 1–25 kg amber glass bottles or HDPE containers. Store cool, dry, and dark.

name: Light Soda Ash
description: Light soda ash, or sodium carbonate light, is a white granular powder with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. It has a density of 0.5-0.6 g/cm³, melting point 851°C, highly soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), alkaline pH 11.6; hygroscopic, produced by Solvay process, lighter than dense grade for better dissolution.
physicalProperties: Light soda ash, or sodium carbonate light, is a white granular powder with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. It has a density of 0.5-0.6 g/cm³, melting point 851°C, highly soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), alkaline pH 11.6; hygroscopic, produced by Solvay process, lighter than dense grade for better dissolution.
safetyHandling: Irritates eyes, skin, and respiratory tract; harmful if swallowed causing gastrointestinal issues. Avoid acids releasing CO2; non-combustible but decomposes at high heat. Stable; Hazard Class not specified; use PPE, ventilate dusty areas.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25-50kg polypropylene bags or big bags; store in dry, covered warehouses per Brenntag product info.

name: Glycine
description: Glycine is the simplest amino acid, appearing as a white crystalline powder with sweet taste. It functions as a proteinogenic amino acid and biochemical buffer.
physicalProperties: Glycine is the simplest amino acid, appearing as a white crystalline powder with sweet taste. It functions as a proteinogenic amino acid and biochemical buffer.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; avoid moisture absorption.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg PE-lined bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and sealed.

name: Gluconic Acid
description: Gluconic acid is a mild organic acid produced by glucose oxidation using Aspergillus niger. It is biodegradable and chelates in nature.
physicalProperties: Gluconic acid is a mild organic acid produced by glucose oxidation using Aspergillus niger. It is biodegradable and chelates in nature.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; stable under normal conditions. Avoid mixing with strong oxidizers.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25–50 kg drums or IBC tanks. Store sealed, cool, and dry.

name: Sodium Nitrate
description: Sodium nitrate, known as Chile saltpeter, is a white crystalline solid with formula NaNO3 and molecular weight 84.99 g/mol. Density 2.26 g/cm³, melting point 308°C, decomposes above 380°C; highly soluble in water (921 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced via neutralization of nitric acid with sodium carbonate.
physicalProperties: Sodium nitrate, known as Chile saltpeter, is a white crystalline solid with formula NaNO3 and molecular weight 84.99 g/mol. Density 2.26 g/cm³, melting point 308°C, decomposes above 380°C; highly soluble in water (921 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced via neutralization of nitric acid with sodium carbonate.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer; fire risk with combustibles, toxic if ingested causing methemoglobinemia. Irritates skin/eyes; Hazard Class 5.1 for transport.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or bulk; store away from organics; rinse containers for recycling or puncture for sanitary disposal.

name: Sodium Nitrite
description: Sodium nitrite is a white to yellowish crystalline powder with formula NaNO2 and molecular weight 69.00 g/mol. Density 2.17 g/cm³, melting point 271°C, decomposes above 320°C; soluble in water (848 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with slight odor. Acts as both oxidant and reductant; produced by absorbing nitrogen oxides in sodium carbonate solution.
physicalProperties: Sodium nitrite is a white to yellowish crystalline powder with formula NaNO2 and molecular weight 69.00 g/mol. Density 2.17 g/cm³, melting point 271°C, decomposes above 320°C; soluble in water (848 g/L at 25°C), hygroscopic with slight odor. Acts as both oxidant and reductant; produced by absorbing nitrogen oxides in sodium carbonate solution.
safetyHandling: Toxic; forms carcinogenic nitrosamines, causing methemoglobinemia. Oxidizer; avoid acids and combustibles. Hazard Class 5.1/6.1 with PPE required.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums; store cool and dry; rinse for recycling or puncture packaging for landfill.

name: Sodium Saccharin
description: Sodium saccharin, the sodium salt of saccharin, is a white crystalline powder with formula C7H4NNaO3S·2H2O and molecular weight 241.20 g/mol. Density 0.83 g/cm³, melting point >300°C (decomposes); highly soluble in water (up to 670 g/L), sweet taste 300-500 times sucrose. Odorless, pH neutral to alkaline in solution; synthesized from toluene via sulfonation and oxidation.
physicalProperties: Sodium saccharin, the sodium salt of saccharin, is a white crystalline powder with formula C7H4NNaO3S·2H2O and molecular weight 241.20 g/mol. Density 0.83 g/cm³, melting point >300°C (decomposes); highly soluble in water (up to 670 g/L), sweet taste 300-500 times sucrose. Odorless, pH neutral to alkaline in solution; synthesized from toluene via sulfonation and oxidation.
safetyHandling: Safe with ADI 9 mg/kg bw/day; may cause allergic reactions or sensitivity. Non-toxic but monitor intake for high consumers.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg cartons or drums with liners; rinse containers for recycling or puncture for sanitary disposal.

name: Sodium Cyclamate
description: Sodium cyclamate, also known as sodium N-cyclohexylsulfamate, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H12NNaO3S and molecular weight 201.22 g/mol. It is highly soluble in water (up to 200 g/L at 20°C), odorless with a sweet taste 30 times that of sucrose, pH 5.5-7.5 in solution. Hygroscopic, stable under normal conditions; produced by sulfonating cyclohexylamine with sodium hydroxide. Used as a non-nutritive sweetener but restricted in some regions.
physicalProperties: Sodium cyclamate, also known as sodium N-cyclohexylsulfamate, is a white crystalline powder with formula C6H12NNaO3S and molecular weight 201.22 g/mol. It is highly soluble in water (up to 200 g/L at 20°C), odorless with a sweet taste 30 times that of sucrose, pH 5.5-7.5 in solution. Hygroscopic, stable under normal conditions; produced by sulfonating cyclohexylamine with sodium hydroxide. Used as a non-nutritive sweetener but restricted in some regions.
safetyHandling: May cause cancer; banned in US per FDA; irritant to eyes, skin, respiratory tract; avoid ingestion, inhalation. Not flammable but incompatible with strong oxidizers; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg fiber drums or polypropylene bags with PE liners; rinse containers for recycling or dispose in sanitary landfills per regulations.

name: Sodium Silicate
description: Sodium silicate, also known as water glass, is a colorless to white viscous liquid or powder with formula Na2SiO3 and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol. Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, decomposes in acids; produced by fusing silica with sodium carbonate.
physicalProperties: Sodium silicate, also known as water glass, is a colorless to white viscous liquid or powder with formula Na2SiO3 and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol. Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, decomposes in acids; produced by fusing silica with sodium carbonate.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive, causes severe burns to skin, eyes, mucous membranes; inhalation irritates respiratory tract. Avoid acids; Hazard Class 8; use PPE and ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25kg bags, drums, or bulk tanks; rinse containers triply for recycling or puncture for landfill disposal.

name: Sodium Sulfate
description: Sodium sulfate, also known as Glauber’s salt, is a white crystalline solid with formula Na2SO4 and molecular weight 142.04 g/mol. Density 2.66 g/cm³, melting point 884°C; highly soluble in water (up to 139 g/L at 20°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced from natural deposits or as a byproduct of chemical processes.
physicalProperties: Sodium sulfate, also known as Glauber’s salt, is a white crystalline solid with formula Na2SO4 and molecular weight 142.04 g/mol. Density 2.66 g/cm³, melting point 884°C; highly soluble in water (up to 139 g/L at 20°C), hygroscopic with neutral pH. Produced from natural deposits or as a byproduct of chemical processes.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but dust irritates eyes, skin, respiratory tract; ingestion causes gastrointestinal upset. Non-flammable; avoid strong acids; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg or 50kg bags with liners; store dry; rinse for recycling or dispose in landfills.

name: Glucosamine Hydrochloride
description: Glucosamine hydrochloride is an amino sugar derived from the exoskeletons of shellfish or produced by fermentation. It is a white crystalline powder with high solubility in water and stability in tablet formulations.
physicalProperties: Glucosamine hydrochloride is an amino sugar derived from the exoskeletons of shellfish or produced by fermentation. It is a white crystalline powder with high solubility in water and stability in tablet formulations.
safetyHandling: Generally recognized as safe (GRAS); may cause mild gastrointestinal effects in sensitive individuals. Handle under dry, ventilated conditions to prevent hygroscopic clumping.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined fiber drums or aluminum foil bags. Store in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and moisture to maintain purity and flowability.

name: Sodium Metasilicate
description: Sodium metasilicate, also called sodium silicate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2SiO3 (anhydrous) or Na2SiO3·5H2O (pentahydrate) and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol (anhydrous). Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, it decomposes in acids releasing silica; produced by fusing sodium carbonate with silica.
physicalProperties: Sodium metasilicate, also called sodium silicate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2SiO3 (anhydrous) or Na2SiO3·5H2O (pentahydrate) and molecular weight 122.06 g/mol (anhydrous). Density 2.4 g/cm³, melting point 1089°C; highly soluble in water forming alkaline solutions (pH 12-13), insoluble in alcohols. Hygroscopic, it decomposes in acids releasing silica; produced by fusing sodium carbonate with silica.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive; causes severe burns to skin and eyes, risk of blindness. Avoid acids; use PPE and ventilation. Hazard Class 8 for transport.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with secure closures; for disposal, rinse triply and recycle or puncture packaging for landfill.

name: Sodium Molybdate
description: Sodium molybdate, typically as dihydrate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2MoO4·2H2O and molecular weight 241.95 g/mol. Density 2.7 g/cm³, melting point 687°C (anhydrous); highly soluble in water (840 g/L at 20°C), insoluble in organics. Hygroscopic, pH 9-10 in solution; produced by dissolving molybdenum trioxide in sodium hydroxide.
physicalProperties: Sodium molybdate, typically as dihydrate, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2MoO4·2H2O and molecular weight 241.95 g/mol. Density 2.7 g/cm³, melting point 687°C (anhydrous); highly soluble in water (840 g/L at 20°C), insoluble in organics. Hygroscopic, pH 9-10 in solution; produced by dissolving molybdenum trioxide in sodium hydroxide.
safetyHandling: Moderate toxicity; irritates eyes and skin, inhalation risk low. Avoid ingestion; use PPE. Non-flammable but incompatible with strong reducers.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with polyethylene liners; rinse for recycling or puncture for disposal in landfills per guidelines.

name: Glycidyl Methacrylate
description: Glycidyl methacrylate (C₇H₁₀O₃) is a reactive monomer containing both epoxy and methacrylate groups, offering strong adhesion and crosslinking potential.
physicalProperties: Glycidyl methacrylate (C₇H₁₀O₃) is a reactive monomer containing both epoxy and methacrylate groups, offering strong adhesion and crosslinking potential.
safetyHandling: Irritating to skin and eyes; sensitizer by contact. Avoid heat and sunlight to prevent polymerization.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 180–200 kg drums. Store cool, dry, and stabilized under nitrogen.

name: Sodium Carboxymethyl Cellulose
description: Sodium carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC or carboxymethylcellulose sodium) is a white, odorless, hygroscopic powder derived from cellulose, with formula [C6H7O2(OH)2OCH2COONa]n and variable molecular weight (90,000-700,000 g/mol). Density 1.6 g/cm³, no fixed melting point (decomposes >300°C); forms viscous colloidal solutions in water, insoluble in organics. pH 6.5-8.5; degree of substitution 0.7-1.2 affects viscosity. Produced by reacting cellulose with chloroacetic acid and NaOH.
physicalProperties: Sodium carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC or carboxymethylcellulose sodium) is a white, odorless, hygroscopic powder derived from cellulose, with formula [C6H7O2(OH)2OCH2COONa]n and variable molecular weight (90,000-700,000 g/mol). Density 1.6 g/cm³, no fixed melting point (decomposes >300°C); forms viscous colloidal solutions in water, insoluble in organics. pH 6.5-8.5; degree of substitution 0.7-1.2 affects viscosity. Produced by reacting cellulose with chloroacetic acid and NaOH.
safetyHandling: Safe, but dust irritates eyes/respiratory; incompatible with strong acids. Low explosion risk; GRAS by FDA, no consumer concerns in additives.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg multi-layer paper bags with PE or fiber drums. Store dry to prevent caking.

name: Sodium Carbonate
description: Sodium carbonate, or soda ash, is a white, hygroscopic powder or granules with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. Density 2.54 g/cm³ (anhydrous), melting point 851°C, decomposes before boiling. Soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), forming alkaline solutions pH 11-12; insoluble in organics. Absorbs moisture and CO2 from air. Exists as anhydrous, monohydrate, or decahydrate; produced via Solvay process from NaCl and limestone.
physicalProperties: Sodium carbonate, or soda ash, is a white, hygroscopic powder or granules with formula Na2CO3 and molecular weight 105.99 g/mol. Density 2.54 g/cm³ (anhydrous), melting point 851°C, decomposes before boiling. Soluble in water (215 g/L at 20°C), forming alkaline solutions pH 11-12; insoluble in organics. Absorbs moisture and CO2 from air. Exists as anhydrous, monohydrate, or decahydrate; produced via Solvay process from NaCl and limestone.
safetyHandling: Irritates eyes; mildly toxic if ingested. GRAS by FDA/EPA; avoid acids to prevent CO2 release. Not hazardous for transport.
tradeRegulatory: In 25-50kg bags or bulk supersacks. Store dry to avoid caking.

name: Sodium Chlorate
description: Sodium chlorate is a white, odorless, hygroscopic crystalline solid with formula NaClO3 and molecular weight 106.44 g/mol. Density 2.49 g/cm³, melting point 248°C, decomposes above 300°C releasing oxygen. Highly soluble in water (790 g/L at 0°C), slightly in alcohols; pH neutral. Strong oxidizer; produced electrolytically from NaCl. Forms dihydrate or tetrahydrate in humid conditions.
physicalProperties: Sodium chlorate is a white, odorless, hygroscopic crystalline solid with formula NaClO3 and molecular weight 106.44 g/mol. Density 2.49 g/cm³, melting point 248°C, decomposes above 300°C releasing oxygen. Highly soluble in water (790 g/L at 0°C), slightly in alcohols; pH neutral. Strong oxidizer; produced electrolytically from NaCl. Forms dihydrate or tetrahydrate in humid conditions.
safetyHandling: Powerful oxidizer; fire/explosion risk with organics/metals. Toxic if ingested; irritates skin/eyes. UN1495, Hazard Class 5.1.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or 1MT big bags. Store away from combustibles, cool and dry.

name: Sodium Chlorite
description: Sodium chlorite is a white to pale yellow crystalline solid or flakes with formula NaClO2 and molecular weight 90.44 g/mol. Density 2.5 g/cm³, decomposes explosively above 180-200°C; highly soluble in water (390 g/L at 17°C), slightly in methanol. pH of solutions 10-11; strong oxidizer, forms trihydrate or monohydrate. Produced by reducing sodium chlorate with acids or reductants.
physicalProperties: Sodium chlorite is a white to pale yellow crystalline solid or flakes with formula NaClO2 and molecular weight 90.44 g/mol. Density 2.5 g/cm³, decomposes explosively above 180-200°C; highly soluble in water (390 g/L at 17°C), slightly in methanol. pH of solutions 10-11; strong oxidizer, forms trihydrate or monohydrate. Produced by reducing sodium chlorate with acids or reductants.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer; fire risk with organics/sulfur. Corrosive; ingestion causes nausea. UN1496, Hazard Class 5.1; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 25-50kg iron/fiber drums with liners; solutions in plastic. Store cool, dry.

name: Sodium Chloride
description: Sodium chloride, commonly known as table salt or halite, is a white, odorless crystalline solid with the formula NaCl and molecular weight 58.44 g/mol. It has a density of 2.16 g/cm³, melting point of 801°C, and boiling point of 1465°C. Highly soluble in water (up to 359 g/L at 25°C), it forms neutral solutions and is insoluble in most organic solvents. It is hygroscopic, absorbing moisture from air, and is produced via evaporation of seawater or mining rock salt deposits.
physicalProperties: Sodium chloride, commonly known as table salt or halite, is a white, odorless crystalline solid with the formula NaCl and molecular weight 58.44 g/mol. It has a density of 2.16 g/cm³, melting point of 801°C, and boiling point of 1465°C. Highly soluble in water (up to 359 g/L at 25°C), it forms neutral solutions and is insoluble in most organic solvents. It is hygroscopic, absorbing moisture from air, and is produced via evaporation of seawater or mining rock salt deposits.
safetyHandling: Generally safe but excessive ingestion can cause hypertension and cardiovascular diseases like stroke; dust may irritate eyes and respiratory tract. Non-flammable; avoid mixing with strong oxidizers. Classified non-hazardous for transport; use PPE in handling.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg or 50kg polypropylene bags with polyethylene liners, or fiber drums; containers can be rinsed for recycling or punctured for disposal in sanitary landfills to ensure environmental compliance.

name: Sodium Lauryl Sarcosinate
description: Sodium lauryl sarcosinate, also known as sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, is a white to off-white powder or liquid with formula C15H28NNaO3 and molecular weight 293.38 g/mol. It has a density of about 1.03 g/cm³ in solution, pH 7.5-8.5, and is freely soluble in water, forming mild foaming solutions. Derived from sarcosine (N-methylglycine) and lauroyl chloride, it is an amino acid-based anionic surfactant with good biodegradability and low toxicity.
physicalProperties: Sodium lauryl sarcosinate, also known as sodium lauroyl sarcosinate, is a white to off-white powder or liquid with formula C15H28NNaO3 and molecular weight 293.38 g/mol. It has a density of about 1.03 g/cm³ in solution, pH 7.5-8.5, and is freely soluble in water, forming mild foaming solutions. Derived from sarcosine (N-methylglycine) and lauroyl chloride, it is an amino acid-based anionic surfactant with good biodegradability and low toxicity.
safetyHandling: Mild irritant to eyes and skin; low toxicity but avoid inhalation of dust or prolonged contact. Biocompatible; stable with chlorine; use ventilation and PPE to minimize exposure risks.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25kg bags or drums with inner liners; for disposal, rinse containers and offer for recycling or puncture for landfill disposal per regulations.

name: Sodium Lignosulfonate
description: Sodium lignosulfonate, a derivative of lignin from wood pulping, is a light tan to brown amorphous powder with formula C20H24Na2O10S2 (approximate) and average molecular weight 5,000-52,000 g/mol. It has a density of 0.5-0.7 g/cm³, pH 7-11 in solution, and is highly soluble in water but insoluble in organics. Non-hygroscopic, it decomposes above 200°C without melting and has a slight odor; produced as a byproduct of sulfite pulping.
physicalProperties: Sodium lignosulfonate, a derivative of lignin from wood pulping, is a light tan to brown amorphous powder with formula C20H24Na2O10S2 (approximate) and average molecular weight 5,000-52,000 g/mol. It has a density of 0.5-0.7 g/cm³, pH 7-11 in solution, and is highly soluble in water but insoluble in organics. Non-hygroscopic, it decomposes above 200°C without melting and has a slight odor; produced as a byproduct of sulfite pulping.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may irritate eyes or skin as dust, inhalation hazard considered. Non-flammable; avoid strong oxidizers. Generally regarded as safe with proper handling.
tradeRegulatory: Available in 25kg bags or big bags with polyethylene liners; for disposal, incinerate with flue gas scrubbing or recycle rinsed containers.

name: Sodium Metabisulfite
description: Sodium metabisulfite, also known as sodium disulfite or pyrosulfite, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2S2O5 and molecular weight 190.11 g/mol. It has a density of 1.48 g/cm³, decomposes above 150°C, and is highly soluble in water (up to 650 g/L at 20°C), releasing sulfur dioxide. With a pungent SO2 odor, it acts as a strong reducing agent and is produced by reacting sodium bisulfite with sulfur dioxide.
physicalProperties: Sodium metabisulfite, also known as sodium disulfite or pyrosulfite, is a white crystalline powder with formula Na2S2O5 and molecular weight 190.11 g/mol. It has a density of 1.48 g/cm³, decomposes above 150°C, and is highly soluble in water (up to 650 g/L at 20°C), releasing sulfur dioxide. With a pungent SO2 odor, it acts as a strong reducing agent and is produced by reacting sodium bisulfite with sulfur dioxide.
safetyHandling: Corrosive; releases toxic SO2 gas with acids, causing irritation to eyes, skin, and respiratory system; avoid inhalation and acids. Hazard Class 8 with proper ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or drums with inner liners for moisture protection; rinse containers for recycling or puncture for sanitary landfill disposal.

name: Sodium Gluconate
description: Sodium Gluconate is gluconic acid salt, white crystalline chelator soluble in water, sequestering calcium and metals. Fermented from glucose, per Brenntag chelators and EFSA sequestrants.
physicalProperties: Sodium Gluconate is gluconic acid salt, white crystalline chelator soluble in water, sequestering calcium and metals. Fermented from glucose, per Brenntag chelators and EFSA sequestrants.
safetyHandling: Slight eye irritation; biodegradable with low risk. GRAS; ventilate powdery areas per PubChem profiles.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg kraft multi-wall sacks. Store in gluconate general dry warehouses.

name: Propyl Gallate
description: Propyl gallate is a white crystalline powder synthesized from gallic acid and propanol. It is an ester-type antioxidant with high stability in fats and oils, exhibiting strong free radical scavenging activity.
physicalProperties: Propyl gallate is a white crystalline powder synthesized from gallic acid and propanol. It is an ester-type antioxidant with high stability in fats and oils, exhibiting strong free radical scavenging activity.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity; may cause mild skin or eye irritation. Avoid exposure to light and heat to maintain antioxidant potency.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber drums or aluminum foil bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed, away from oxidizing agents and moisture.

name: Sodium Hexametaphosphate
description: Sodium hexametaphosphate, also known as Graham’s salt or SHMP, is a white powder or colorless transparent glassy solid with the chemical formula (NaPO3)6 and molecular weight of 611.77 g/mol. It is highly hygroscopic, readily soluble in water forming a clear solution, but insoluble in organic solvents. With a density of about 2.484 g/cm³, it has a melting point of 616°C where it decomposes. It acts as a sequestrant by forming complexes with metal ions like calcium and magnesium, and its pH in 1% solution is 5.8-7.3. Produced by heating sodium metaphosphate, it is a mixture of polymeric metaphosphates.
physicalProperties: Sodium hexametaphosphate, also known as Graham’s salt or SHMP, is a white powder or colorless transparent glassy solid with the chemical formula (NaPO3)6 and molecular weight of 611.77 g/mol. It is highly hygroscopic, readily soluble in water forming a clear solution, but insoluble in organic solvents. With a density of about 2.484 g/cm³, it has a melting point of 616°C where it decomposes. It acts as a sequestrant by forming complexes with metal ions like calcium and magnesium, and its pH in 1% solution is 5.8-7.3. Produced by heating sodium metaphosphate, it is a mixture of polymeric metaphosphates.
safetyHandling: Generally safe but can irritate eyes, skin, and respiratory tract. Avoid strong acids or oxidizers to prevent heat or decomposition. Use PPE and ventilate areas. Not hazardous for transport; store dry to avoid moisture.
tradeRegulatory: Typically in 25kg polypropylene bags with PE liners or 50kg fiber drums. Store in cool, dry, ventilated spaces away from incompatibles.

name: Sodium Hydroxide
description: Sodium hydroxide, commonly called caustic soda or lye, is a white, odorless, hygroscopic solid with formula NaOH and molecular weight 40.00 g/mol. It has a density of 2.13 g/cm³, melting point of 318°C, and boiling point of 1390°C. Highly soluble in water (up to 1090 g/L at 20°C), it generates heat upon dissolution, forming strongly alkaline solutions with pH around 13-14. It absorbs CO2 from air, forming sodium carbonate. Produced via electrolysis of NaCl or lime-soda process.
physicalProperties: Sodium hydroxide, commonly called caustic soda or lye, is a white, odorless, hygroscopic solid with formula NaOH and molecular weight 40.00 g/mol. It has a density of 2.13 g/cm³, melting point of 318°C, and boiling point of 1390°C. Highly soluble in water (up to 1090 g/L at 20°C), it generates heat upon dissolution, forming strongly alkaline solutions with pH around 13-14. It absorbs CO2 from air, forming sodium carbonate. Produced via electrolysis of NaCl or lime-soda process.
safetyHandling: Highly corrosive, causing severe burns to skin, eyes, and mucous membranes; inhalation may damage lungs. Reacts exothermically with water, acids, and metals, producing hydrogen gas. Wear protective gear; UN1823 (solid), Hazard Class 8.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags, drums, or bulk tanks for solutions. Airtight containers prevent moisture and CO2 absorption. Transport as corrosive material.

name: Sodium Hydrosulfite
otherNames: Sodium Sulfhydrate, Sodium dithionite or sodium hypodisulfite
description: Sodium hydrosulfite, also known as sodium dithionite or sodium hypodisulfite, is a white to grayish crystalline powder with formula Na2S2O4 and molecular weight 174.11 g/mol. It has a density of 2.38 g/cm³, decomposes above 90°C, and emits a faint sulfurous odor. Highly soluble in water (up to 182 g/L at 20°C) but insoluble in alcohols; unstable in air, oxidizing to sodium bisulfate. Acts as a powerful reducing agent, produced from zinc and SO2 or formate reduction.
physicalProperties: Sodium hydrosulfite, also known as sodium dithionite or sodium hypodisulfite, is a white to grayish crystalline powder with formula Na2S2O4 and molecular weight 174.11 g/mol. It has a density of 2.38 g/cm³, decomposes above 90°C, and emits a faint sulfurous odor. Highly soluble in water (up to 182 g/L at 20°C) but insoluble in alcohols; unstable in air, oxidizing to sodium bisulfate. Acts as a powerful reducing agent, produced from zinc and SO2 or formate reduction.
safetyHandling: Spontaneously combustible in moist air; reacts with water or acids to release toxic SO2. Irritates skin, eyes, and respiratory system. Store dry and cool; UN1384, Hazard Class 4.2; use PPE.
tradeRegulatory: In 50kg iron or fiber drums with PE liners to exclude moisture. Handle carefully to prevent ignition from friction.

name: Sodium Hypochlorite
description: Sodium hypochlorite, or bleach, is a pale greenish-yellow liquid or solid with formula NaClO and molecular weight 74.44 g/mol. Typically available as 5-15% aqueous solutions with chlorine odor; density 1.11-1.25 g/mL, decomposes above 40°C releasing oxygen and chlorine. Soluble in water, unstable in light or heat; pH 11-13. Produced by electrolyzing NaCl solution or reacting chlorine with NaOH.
physicalProperties: Sodium hypochlorite, or bleach, is a pale greenish-yellow liquid or solid with formula NaClO and molecular weight 74.44 g/mol. Typically available as 5-15% aqueous solutions with chlorine odor; density 1.11-1.25 g/mL, decomposes above 40°C releasing oxygen and chlorine. Soluble in water, unstable in light or heat; pH 11-13. Produced by electrolyzing NaCl solution or reacting chlorine with NaOH.
safetyHandling: Corrosive, causing burns to skin/eyes; inhalation irritates lungs. Reacts with acids to form chlorine gas; avoid ammonia to prevent explosions. UN1791, Hazard Class 8.
tradeRegulatory: In 5-55 gallon plastic drums or bulk tanks. Store cool, dark, ventilated; pentahydrate in sealed containers.

name: Sodium Isobutyl Xanthate
description: Sodium isobutyl xanthate (SIBX) is a pale yellow to yellowish powder or pellet with pungent odor, formula C5H9NaOS2, molecular weight 172.24 g/mol. Density around 1.1 g/cm³, decomposes above 150°C; freely soluble in water (up to 300 g/L), forms insoluble metal complexes. pH of 1% solution 7-9; produced by reacting isobutanol with CS2 and NaOH. Acts as a flotation collector for sulfide minerals.
physicalProperties: Sodium isobutyl xanthate (SIBX) is a pale yellow to yellowish powder or pellet with pungent odor, formula C5H9NaOS2, molecular weight 172.24 g/mol. Density around 1.1 g/cm³, decomposes above 150°C; freely soluble in water (up to 300 g/L), forms insoluble metal complexes. pH of 1% solution 7-9; produced by reacting isobutanol with CS2 and NaOH. Acts as a flotation collector for sulfide minerals.
safetyHandling: Irritates skin/eyes; inhalation causes respiratory issues. Combustible; incompatible with oxidizers. UN3342, Hazard Class 4.2; wear PPE, ventilate.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or 850kg wooden boxes with liners. Store dry and cool to prevent decomposition.

name: Sodium Isopropyl Xanthate
description: Sodium isopropyl xanthate (SIPX), also proxan-sodium, is a pale yellow powder with formula C4H7NaOS2, molecular weight 158.22 g/mol. Density 1.0-1.2 g/cm³, decomposes at high temperatures; soluble in water (300 g/L), pH 7-9 in solution. Forms chelates with metals; synthesized from isopropanol, CS2, and NaOH. Used as a selective collector in mining.
physicalProperties: Sodium isopropyl xanthate (SIPX), also proxan-sodium, is a pale yellow powder with formula C4H7NaOS2, molecular weight 158.22 g/mol. Density 1.0-1.2 g/cm³, decomposes at high temperatures; soluble in water (300 g/L), pH 7-9 in solution. Forms chelates with metals; synthesized from isopropanol, CS2, and NaOH. Used as a selective collector in mining.
safetyHandling: Skin/eye irritant; decomposes in acids. Combustible; avoid acids/oxidizers. Handle with gloves; UN3342, Hazard Class 4.2.
tradeRegulatory: In 25kg bags or iron drums, airtight to preserve stability. Professional transport required.

name: Propylene Glycol Methyl Ether
description: Propylene Glycol Methyl Ether is a colourless liquid solvent with excellent solvency and low odour. It offers good compatibility with water and organic systems and is valued for its controlled evaporation rate and formulation flexibility in industrial applications.
physicalProperties: Propylene Glycol Methyl Ether is a colourless liquid solvent with excellent solvency and low odour. It offers good compatibility with water and organic systems and is valued for its controlled evaporation rate and formulation flexibility in industrial applications.
safetyHandling: May cause irritation to eyes and respiratory system. Vapours may cause discomfort in enclosed spaces. Use appropriate PPE and adequate ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 180 kilogram steel drums, IBC containers or bulk supply. Store in cool and dry areas away from ignition sources.

name: Potassium Titanate
description: Potassium titanate is a white to gray ceramic compound composed of layered titanium oxide structures bonded with potassium ions. It exhibits excellent heat resistance and reinforcing characteristics.
physicalProperties: Potassium titanate is a white to gray ceramic compound composed of layered titanium oxide structures bonded with potassium ions. It exhibits excellent heat resistance and reinforcing characteristics.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic but may irritate respiratory passages if inhaled as fine dust. Use PPE and dust extraction during handling.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg paper bags or fiber drums. Store in a dry, ventilated place, sealed against humidity and contamination.

name: Sodium Fluoride
description: Sodium Fluoride is inorganic fluoride salt, white powder soluble in water, anticariogenic for tooth enamel strengthening. Produced from hydrofluoric acid, per Brenntag fluorides and ChemSpider halides.
physicalProperties: Sodium Fluoride is inorganic fluoride salt, white powder soluble in water, anticariogenic for tooth enamel strengthening. Produced from hydrofluoric acid, per Brenntag fluorides and ChemSpider halides.
safetyHandling: Acute poisoning with cardiac effects; chronic fluorosis on bones. Strict dosing; PPE for dust; monitor intake per EFSA limits.
tradeRegulatory: Sealed in 25 kg plastic-lined metal pails with locks. Store in fluoride secure cabinets.

name: Propargyl Alcohol
description: Propargyl alcohol is a clear, colorless liquid with a pungent odor and reactive terminal alkyne group. It is miscible with water, alcohol, and ether, and has high chemical reactivity in substitution and addition reactions.
physicalProperties: Propargyl alcohol is a clear, colorless liquid with a pungent odor and reactive terminal alkyne group. It is miscible with water, alcohol, and ether, and has high chemical reactivity in substitution and addition reactions.
safetyHandling: Toxic and flammable; harmful if inhaled or absorbed through skin. Handle under ventilation, avoiding ignition sources.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 180 kg steel drums or 1,000 L IBC tanks. Store cool, dry, and tightly closed in explosion-proof storage facilities.

name: Sodium Formate
description: Sodium Formate is formic acid sodium salt, white deliquescent crystals soluble, reducing agent for de-icing and tanning. From carbon monoxide reaction, compliant with PubChem formates and Sigma-Aldrich organics.
physicalProperties: Sodium Formate is formic acid sodium salt, white deliquescent crystals soluble, reducing agent for de-icing and tanning. From carbon monoxide reaction, compliant with PubChem formates and Sigma-Aldrich organics.
safetyHandling: Eye and skin mild irritant; low toxicity but hygroscopic dust. Avoid oxidants; gloves for handling per ChemicalBook safety.
tradeRegulatory: Bagged in 25 kg woven with moisture barriers. Keep in formate low-humidity storage.

name: Potassium Permanganate
description: Potassium permanganate is a dark purple crystalline oxidizing agent with strong disinfectant and bleaching capabilities. It is soluble in water, producing violet-colored solutions with high redox potential.
physicalProperties: Potassium permanganate is a dark purple crystalline oxidizing agent with strong disinfectant and bleaching capabilities. It is soluble in water, producing violet-colored solutions with high redox potential.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer; reacts vigorously with organics and reducing agents. May cause burns upon contact with skin. Handle with care under ventilation and non-sparking conditions.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg HDPE drums or 50 kg composite containers. Store cool, dry, and tightly closed away from combustibles and acids.

name: Potassium Persulfate
description: Potassium persulfate is a white crystalline oxidizing salt with strong bleaching and radical-generating properties. It decomposes to release active oxygen species upon heating or in solution.
physicalProperties: Potassium persulfate is a white crystalline oxidizing salt with strong bleaching and radical-generating properties. It decomposes to release active oxygen species upon heating or in solution.
safetyHandling: Strong oxidizer and irritant; avoid contact with organic matter and moisture. Handle with gloves, goggles, and proper ventilation.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store in a cool, dry, and well-ventilated area, isolated from combustible substances.

name: Potassium Sodium Tartrate
description: Potassium sodium tartrate, commonly known as Rochelle salt, is a colorless crystalline compound exhibiting piezoelectric and optical activity. It is freely soluble in water and forms neutral solutions.
physicalProperties: Potassium sodium tartrate, commonly known as Rochelle salt, is a colorless crystalline compound exhibiting piezoelectric and optical activity. It is freely soluble in water and forms neutral solutions.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable under normal conditions; prolonged humidity exposure may cause clumping.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags or 50 kg fiber drums. Store in a cool, dry, and sealed environment protected from moisture.

name: Potassium Sorbate
description: Potassium sorbate is a white crystalline salt of sorbic acid with strong antimicrobial and antifungal properties. It is highly soluble in water, forming a neutral solution.
physicalProperties: Potassium sorbate is a white crystalline salt of sorbic acid with strong antimicrobial and antifungal properties. It is highly soluble in water, forming a neutral solution.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and GRAS-approved; may cause mild irritation at high concentrations. Avoid prolonged exposure to humidity.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags or fiber drums. Store in a cool, dry, and tightly sealed location away from direct sunlight.

name: Sodium Bisulfate
description: Sodium Bisulfate is sodium hydrogen sulfate, white granular acid salt soluble in water, a dry form of sulfuric acid for pH reduction without liquid risks. Synthesized from sodium chloride and sulfuric acid, per ChemicalBook sulfates and EFSA acidifiers.
physicalProperties: Sodium Bisulfate is sodium hydrogen sulfate, white granular acid salt soluble in water, a dry form of sulfuric acid for pH reduction without liquid risks. Synthesized from sodium chloride and sulfuric acid, per ChemicalBook sulfates and EFSA acidifiers.
safetyHandling: Corrosive to eyes and skin; dust causes throat irritation. Neutralize with bases; avoid aluminum contact; wear acid-resistant gear per GHS standards.
tradeRegulatory: Packed in 25 kg polypropylene bags with inner seals for moisture block. Store in bisulfate dry containment with acid spill trays.

name: Sodium Bromate
description: Sodium bromate is sodium salt of bromic acid, white crystalline oxidizer soluble in water, used for controlled oxidation in baking and chemistry. Produced from bromine electrolysis, compliant with PubChem oxidizers and Sigma-Aldrich halides.
physicalProperties: Sodium bromate is sodium salt of bromic acid, white crystalline oxidizer soluble in water, used for controlled oxidation in baking and chemistry. Produced from bromine electrolysis, compliant with PubChem oxidizers and Sigma-Aldrich halides.
safetyHandling: Potential carcinogen; explosive with combustibles. Banned in EU foods; limit exposure with respirators; fire suppression ready per EFSA restrictions.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg fiber-reinforced plastic cans with labels. Isolate in bromate oxidizer storage with fire doors.

name: Sodium Bromide
description: Sodium Bromide is bromide anion salt, white hygroscopic powder highly soluble, serving as bromide source for sedatives and fluids. Obtained from seawater or brines, per Brenntag halides and ChemSpider ions.
physicalProperties: Sodium Bromide is bromide anion salt, white hygroscopic powder highly soluble, serving as bromide source for sedatives and fluids. Obtained from seawater or brines, per Brenntag halides and ChemSpider ions.
safetyHandling: Chronic bromism causes rashes; mild eye irritants. Low acute toxicity; ventilate for dust; monitor thyroid per PubChem health effects.
tradeRegulatory: Bagged in 25 kg kraft with polyethylene liners for hygiene. Keep in bromide general chemical rooms.

name: Sodium Diacetate
description: Sodium Diacetate is a 1:1 complex of sodium acetate and acetic acid, white hygroscopic powder with acetic odor, acting as mold inhibitor and flavorant. Synthesized from acetic acid, meeting food standards from EFSA preservatives and ChemicalBook acetates.
physicalProperties: Sodium Diacetate is a 1:1 complex of sodium acetate and acetic acid, white hygroscopic powder with acetic odor, acting as mold inhibitor and flavorant. Synthesized from acetic acid, meeting food standards from EFSA preservatives and ChemicalBook acetates.
safetyHandling: Irritates eyes and nose from vapors; skin dryness possible. GRAS but odor control needed; use exhaust systems per Sigma-Aldrich data.
tradeRegulatory: Boxed in 25 kg cartons with vapor barriers for freshness. Store in diacetate odor-isolated facilities.

name: Sodium Etasulfate
description: Sodium Etasulfate, or sodium 2-ethylhexyl sulfate, is a branched anionic surfactant, clear liquid highly soluble, providing low foam wetting for industrial cleaning. Derived from 2-ethylhexanol sulfation, per Brenntag sulfates and PubChem surfactants.
physicalProperties: Sodium Etasulfate, or sodium 2-ethylhexyl sulfate, is a branched anionic surfactant, clear liquid highly soluble, providing low foam wetting for industrial cleaning. Derived from 2-ethylhexanol sulfation, per Brenntag sulfates and PubChem surfactants.
safetyHandling: Defats skin leading to dermatitis; eye corrosive in concentrates. Biodegradable; dilute with water; protective eyewear per GHS irritant labels.
tradeRegulatory: Contained in 200 kg HDPE barrels with screw caps for secure pouring. Upright in etasulfate chemical storage bays.

name: Potassium Sulfate
description: Potassium sulfate, or sulfate of potash, is a colorless crystalline solid that provides both potassium and sulfur nutrients without chloride contamination. It is moderately soluble in water and non-hygroscopic.
physicalProperties: Potassium sulfate, or sulfate of potash, is a colorless crystalline solid that provides both potassium and sulfur nutrients without chloride contamination. It is moderately soluble in water and non-hygroscopic.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; avoid contact with strong acids to prevent sulfur dioxide release.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg woven polypropylene bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store in a dry, cool warehouse away from moisture.

name: Sodium Fluorosilicate
description: Sodium fluorosilicate is sodium hexafluorosilicate, white crystalline powder slightly soluble, used as a fluoride source for water and insecticides. Synthesized from silicon tetrafluoride, aligned with Sigma-Aldrich fluorides and EFSA fluoride additives.
physicalProperties: Sodium fluorosilicate is sodium hexafluorosilicate, white crystalline powder slightly soluble, used as a fluoride source for water and insecticides. Synthesized from silicon tetrafluoride, aligned with Sigma-Aldrich fluorides and EFSA fluoride additives.
safetyHandling: Fluoride toxicity causes nausea and bone issues; dust inhalation is hazardous. Aquatic persistent; calcium antidotes ready per PubChem warnings.
tradeRegulatory: Packaged in 25 kg lined polypropylene sacks with ties. Secure in fluorosilicate ventilated zones with spill kits.

name: Potassium Iodide
description: Potassium iodide is a white crystalline solid, odorless and highly soluble in water, ethanol, and glycerin. It is hygroscopic and releases iodine upon exposure to light or oxidants.
physicalProperties: Potassium iodide is a white crystalline solid, odorless and highly soluble in water, ethanol, and glycerin. It is hygroscopic and releases iodine upon exposure to light or oxidants.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic in regulated use; excessive intake may disturb thyroid function. Protect from light and air exposure.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg aluminum-lined fiber drums or double-layer foil bags. Store in cool, dry, and sealed environments away from oxidizers.

name: Potassium Carbonate
description: Potassium carbonate, also called potash, is a white hygroscopic powder soluble in water, producing mildly alkaline solutions. It has excellent buffering and deliquescent properties.
physicalProperties: Potassium carbonate, also called potash, is a white hygroscopic powder soluble in water, producing mildly alkaline solutions. It has excellent buffering and deliquescent properties.
safetyHandling: Low toxicity but may irritate eyes and skin. Avoid contact with acids as CO₂ release may occur.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg plastic-lined woven bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store cool, dry, and sealed to prevent caking.

name: Potassium Chlorate
description: Potassium chlorate is a white crystalline oxidizing compound that decomposes above 400 °C to liberate oxygen. It is soluble in water and stable under ambient conditions.
physicalProperties: Potassium chlorate is a white crystalline oxidizing compound that decomposes above 400 °C to liberate oxygen. It is soluble in water and stable under ambient conditions.
safetyHandling: Highly reactive oxidizer; contact with organic materials or sulfur may cause explosion. Handle under strict safety protocols.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags. Store in a cool, dry, non-combustible area away from reducing agents and heat.

name: Potassium Chloride
description: Potassium chloride is a colorless crystalline salt with a saline taste, readily soluble in water and glycerin. It provides a major potassium source for industrial and biological uses.
physicalProperties: Potassium chloride is a colorless crystalline salt with a saline taste, readily soluble in water and glycerin. It provides a major potassium source for industrial and biological uses.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; avoid excessive ingestion or contact with strong acids.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PP bags or 1,000 kg bulk sacks. Store cool, dry, and sealed against humidity.

name: Potassium Cocoate
description: Potassium cocoate is a natural surfactant obtained by neutralizing coconut fatty acids with potassium hydroxide. It forms a light-colored viscous liquid or solid soap base.
physicalProperties: Potassium cocoate is a natural surfactant obtained by neutralizing coconut fatty acids with potassium hydroxide. It forms a light-colored viscous liquid or solid soap base.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and non-irritating in diluted form; concentrated solutions may cause mild eye irritation.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg HDPE drums or 200 kg IBC containers. Store in cool, dry, and sealed containers away from strong acids.

name: Potassium Metavanadate
description: Potassium metavanadate is a white crystalline inorganic vanadium(V) compound with moderate solubility in water. It exhibits strong oxidizing and catalytic activity.
physicalProperties: Potassium metavanadate is a white crystalline inorganic vanadium(V) compound with moderate solubility in water. It exhibits strong oxidizing and catalytic activity.
safetyHandling: Toxic upon inhalation or ingestion; may irritate respiratory and digestive tracts. Use PPE and fume extraction during handling.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and tightly sealed.

name: Potassium Nitrate
description: Potassium nitrate, commonly known as saltpeter, is a white crystalline solid highly soluble in water and slightly hygroscopic. It decomposes upon heating to release oxygen, leaving potassium nitrite as a residue.
physicalProperties: Potassium nitrate, commonly known as saltpeter, is a white crystalline solid highly soluble in water and slightly hygroscopic. It decomposes upon heating to release oxygen, leaving potassium nitrite as a residue.
safetyHandling: Classified as an oxidizer; may intensify combustion. Avoid mixing with organic materials or strong acids. Use protective gloves and goggles during handling.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg plastic-lined woven bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store in a cool, dry, and ventilated environment, separated from flammables and reducing agents.

name: Potassium Orthovanadate
description: Potassium orthovanadate is a white crystalline compound containing trivalent vanadium species in alkaline form. It dissolves in water to produce strongly basic solutions and exhibits oxidizing potential.
physicalProperties: Potassium orthovanadate is a white crystalline compound containing trivalent vanadium species in alkaline form. It dissolves in water to produce strongly basic solutions and exhibits oxidizing potential.
safetyHandling: Toxic if inhaled or ingested; may cause respiratory and gastrointestinal irritation. Handle with gloves, goggles, and fume extraction.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg PE-lined paper bags or fiber drums. Store in a cool, dry, and sealed area away from acids and organic materials.

name: Potassium Bicarbonate
otherNames: Potassium Hydrogen Carbonate, Potassium Acid Carbonate
description: Potassium bicarbonate is a white crystalline powder soluble in water and mildly alkaline in solution.
physicalProperties: Potassium bicarbonate is a white crystalline powder soluble in water and mildly alkaline in solution.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic and stable; avoid contact with acids.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg paper bags or fiber drums. Store cool, dry, and tightly sealed.

name: Potassium Bitartrate
description: Potassium bitartrate, also known as cream of tartar, is a white crystalline organic acid salt derived from tartaric acid.
physicalProperties: Potassium bitartrate, also known as cream of tartar, is a white crystalline organic acid salt derived from tartaric acid.
safetyHandling: Non-toxic; avoid excessive humidity.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg paper bags or drums. Store in cool, dry, and sealed conditions.

name: Potassium Bromide
description: Potassium bromide is a colorless to white crystalline salt, odorless and highly soluble in water and glycerol. It is neutral in reaction and forms part of the halide family, structurally similar to sodium chloride. When heated, it releases hydrogen bromide vapors.
physicalProperties: Potassium bromide is a colorless to white crystalline salt, odorless and highly soluble in water and glycerol. It is neutral in reaction and forms part of the halide family, structurally similar to sodium chloride. When heated, it releases hydrogen bromide vapors.
safetyHandling: Non-flammable but harmful in excessive ingestion. Inhalation of dust should be avoided. Use PPE in laboratory settings.

name: Potassium Ethyl Xanthate
description: Potassium ethyl xanthate is a pale-yellow crystalline powder or liquid with a distinct sulfurous odor. It’s soluble in water and decomposes upon heating or exposure to acids.
physicalProperties: Potassium ethyl xanthate is a pale-yellow crystalline powder or liquid with a distinct sulfurous odor. It’s soluble in water and decomposes upon heating or exposure to acids.

tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg drums, woven sacks, or 1,000 kg IBC tanks with moisture barriers. Store in cool, dry, and well-ventilated conditions away from heat and acids.

name: Potassium Fluorosilicate
description: Potassium fluorosilicate is a fine white crystalline powder containing about 60% fluorine. It has low solubility in cold water and decomposes above 300 °C, releasing hydrogen fluoride.
physicalProperties: Potassium fluorosilicate is a fine white crystalline powder containing about 60% fluorine. It has low solubility in cold water and decomposes above 300 °C, releasing hydrogen fluoride.
safetyHandling: Toxic by ingestion and inhalation; prolonged exposure may affect bones and teeth due to fluoride content. Handle with respiratory protection and chemical-resistant gloves.
tradeRegulatory: Supplied in 25 kg multiwall paper bags with polyethylene liners or 1,000 kg bulk sacks. Store in a cool, dry, well-ventilated area away from acids.

name: Potassium Hydroxide
description: Potassium hydroxide, commonly called caustic potash, is a white deliquescent solid that absorbs CO₂ from air. It dissolves in water with intense heat evolution, forming a strong alkaline solution.
physicalProperties: Potassium hydroxide, commonly called caustic potash, is a white deliquescent solid that absorbs CO₂ from air. It dissolves in water with intense heat evolution, forming a strong alkaline solution.
safetyHandling: Corrosive to skin, eyes, and metals. Avoid contact with acids or organic materials. Use protective gloves, goggles, and ventilation during handling.
tradeRegulatory: Delivered in 25 kg HDPE-lined PP bags or 1,000 kg big bags. Store in dry, cool conditions, away from acids, aluminum, and moisture sources.

name: Potassium Iodate
description: Potassium iodate is a white crystalline oxidizer with strong stability and high iodine content (~59%). It dissolves in water to yield mildly acidic solutions.
physicalProperties: Potassium iodate is a white crystalline oxidizer with strong stability and high iodine content (~59%). It dissolves in water to yield mildly acidic solutions.
